Author: (Thunb.) Sw.
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of southern China; also found in southern Asia and Australia.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal shrubland.
Morphology: Bipinnate leaves, ultimate lobes short and broad; sporangiophore spike borne on fertile pinnae margin, fringed.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Yunnan; widely distributed in pantropical regions.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal mangrove forests or waterside mudflats.
Morphology: Once-pinnate leaves clustered, pinnae mostly reaching 30 pairs, lanceolate, thickly leathery, apex rounded or notched; sporangia densely covering fertile pinnae lower surface.
Uses: Leaves used as a vegetable, ornamental, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Hainan; Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal mangrove forests or waterside mudflats.
Morphology: Petiole base covered with scale; pinnae about 15 pairs, broad lanceolate, apex slightly becoming narrow and short-acuminate.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Burm. f.) Bedd.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan and elsewhere; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in secondary open woodland in coastal areas.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; plant climbing; odd-pinnate, smooth, pinnae lanceolate, leathery, lustrous; fertile pinnae linear, sori densely covering lower leaf surface.

Author: (L.f.) C.Presl
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of eastern China; Asian coasts and Pacific islands also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal reef crevices.
Morphology: Odd-pinnate leaves, lateral pinnae ovate or ovate-lanceolate, leathery, smooth, entire, terminal pinnae two forked or three forked-shaped; sori covering the abaxial surface of the pinnae.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Stapf
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Central Asia and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, salinized and alkalized gravelly ground, dry river valleys or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Leaves mostly 3-lobed sparse 2-lobed, membranous; spherical flower often without stalk, female spherical flower bract each whorls 3, dry membranous; seeds often 3 grains. Flowering period May–June, seeds July–August.

Author: Schrenk & C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert slopes, dry river floodplains, Gobi gravel desert and saline land.
Morphology: Leaves 3-lobed and 2-lobed and persistent; female spherical flower bract 2 or 3, with membranous margins; female spherical flower at maturity succulent red, seeds 3 or 2 grains. Flowering period May–June, seeds July–August.

Author: (Roxb.) Thwaites
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; South Asia and Southeast Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground open woodland.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves alternate, papery, oblong, glabrous; flower pale yellow, petals 6 segments, 2 whorls; mature carpels berry-shaped, nearly round spherical-shaped, red or black. Flowering period entire year, fruiting period spring.

Author: (C.Presl) Kubitzki
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; southern Asia, East Africa to Pacific Ocean eastern part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or sandy beach.
Morphology: Simple leaves alternate, center-shaped orbicular, peltate-shaped, entire; panicle, flower white; fruit succulent, enclosing hidden on expanded large involucre inner, succulent, seeds 1 grains. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of southeastern China; also found in tropical Asia, Africa and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in shrubland on slopes or open woodland.
Morphology: Stem linear; leaves reduced to minute scales; spike, flowers small, white, without stalk; fruit small, ovoid, enclosed within the fleshy fruit receptacle. Flowering and fruiting period May–December.

Author: Asch.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Asia tropical regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Erect lateral branches long 1–1.5 cm; leaves 6–10 clustered stem apex, leaf blade long elliptic or lanceolate, entire, only with midrib and margin veins, without transverse veins; fruit ovate, with beak.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Zoll.) Hartog
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Africa and Asia tropical regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaves 2 opposite, green transparent, long elliptic or ovate, long 7–12 mm, transverse veins 3–8 pairs, and midrib jiao angles 70–90°; fruit ovate round-shaped or spherical-shaped.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (R.Br.) Hook.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaves long 1–4 cm, entire, glabrous, transverse veins 12–25 pairs, and midrib jiao angles 45–60°; fruit nearly spherical-shaped, with beak. Flowering period November–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ostenf.
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; Asia, Africa, Oceania and in South America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaves long 1–2.2 m, margin with slender teeth, both surfaces covered with pubescent, transverse veins 6–9 pairs, and midrib jiao angles 45–60°.

Author: (L.f.) Royle
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome creeping, nodes dense; leaves band-shaped flattened, entire, veins 13–19 strips; fruit ovate round-shaped, surface with dense two forked-shaped attached genus structure, with beak. Flowering period May.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Ehrenb. ex Solms) Asch.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow sea sandy seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome long and horizontally creeping; leaves band-shaped; female flowers style 6, stigma 2-lobed, long 1–1.5 cm; fruit globose, dehiscent is mostly valves.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or shallow water places.
Morphology: Plant coarse robust, with coarse spiny; leaf blade linear-lanceolate, wide about 2 mm, with coarse serrate; achene yellow-brown. Flowering and fruiting period September–November.

Author: All.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or shallow water places.
Morphology: Plant slender slender, smooth; leaf blade linear, wide 0.5–1 mm, with serrate; anthers 1 locules; achene narrow long elliptic, upper part gradually narrow and slightly curved. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: A.Braun ex Magnus
Distribution: Distributed in East China and Taiwan; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in ditches and channels margins, lakeshores or water fields.
Morphology: Leaf blade linear, wide about 0.5 mm, auricle truncate orbicular or obcordate; anthers 4 locules; achene yellow-brown, appearing crescent-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Delile
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or shallow water places.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1 mm; anthers 4 locules; achene long elliptic, not curved. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.f.) Royle
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or marshland.
Morphology: Xiu mian ya long ovate orbicular; leaves 3–8 whorled, linear or long linear, with serrate, sessile; flower single -like, with spathe; fruit cylindrical. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline marshland or seashore salt flat.
Morphology: Plant coarse robust; leaves linear; raceme, flower arranged relatively compact, pedicel long about 1 mm; capsule elliptic, with 6-ridged, 6 lobes dehiscent. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 20, 534; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline marshland or shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Plant weak small; leaves wide about 1 mm; flower arranged relatively sparse spreading, pedicel long about 2 mm; capsule clavate linear, 3 lobes dehiscent. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 23, 534; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Makino
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Liaoning and Shandong; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast reefs.
Morphology: Leaves wide 2–4.5 mm, apex obtuse round, often 5 veins, sparse 3 veins, stem base with red brown colored bristle-like fibers; spadix borne spathe inner; fruit crescent-shaped. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Makino
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Liaoning and Shandong; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low-tide line reefs or hard-textured sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves wide 1.5–2.5 mm, apex often emarginate, 3 veins; stem base bristle-like fibers appearing blackish brown. Flowering period March–May, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Asch. & Graebn.
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Liaoning, Shandong and Taiwan; Northern Hemisphere widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome surface layers most inner layers with fiber bundles; leaf sheath kai fang type, margin membranous, xiang hu die ya and amplexicaul; leaf blade with initially ji veins 3 strips; spadix rachis margin overall is persistent in bract-shaped attached genus structure. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Miki
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome nearly erect, internodes very short, plant structure body appearing tufted-shaped; leaf sheath often thick bi zu zhi becoming, persistent; leaves end emarginate. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Liaoning and Shandong; Asia, Africa, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome creeping, with short vegetative branches and long borne zhi branches; leaf blade linear, wide 3–6 mm, entire, veins 5–7 strips; spathe sheath green, spadix flattened; fruit oblong, seeds with keel. Flowering and fruiting period March–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 25, 537; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Miki
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 10–15 mm, leaves end truncate or emarginate; borne zhi branches without sterile branched; seeds surface without colored su spots dots. Flowering and fruiting period 7–yi year March.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Miki
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 5–8 mm; leaves end obtuse to projecting pointed; borne zhi branches only base branched fertile; seeds surface with colored su spots dots. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Pers.) Börner
Distribution: Distributed in Shaanxi, Ningxia, Qinghai and Xinjiang; north temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in alkaline -like water body.
Morphology: Leaves linear, long 3–7 cm, wide 0.3–0.5 mm, leaf sheath slender and appressed stem; spike terminal, flowers small; fruit without keel or keel inconspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (L.) Börner
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or marshland.
Morphology: Stem upper part much-branched; leaves linear, wide only 0.2–1 mm, leaf sheath margin free; pistil 4; fruit obovate, with beak. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia, Europe, Africa and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or marshland.
Morphology: Leaves linear, sessile, wide about 1 mm, entire, stipule and leaves free, margin connate, appearing tao tubular amplexicaul; fruit oblique obovate, with short beak. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in chu South China outer large parts provinces and regions; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or marshland.
Morphology: Te becoming xiu mian ya conspicuous; leaves linear, sessile, wide 3–10 mm, leaf margin undulate, with finely serrate; fruit ovate, fruit beak longer, abaxial keel with teeth teeth. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in chu South China outer large parts provinces and regions; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes, ponds, rivers or marshland.
Morphology: Leaves long elliptic to ovate-elliptic, sessile, subsessile to with short-stalked, wide 1–3.5 cm, apex pointed acute or with awn-shaped pointed tip; fruit long 3 mm, abaxial keel slightly acute. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides, stream banks or marsh shallow water places.
Morphology: Leaves slender linear, 3–4 whorled, wide 0.3–0.5 mm, entire; achene 2–4 clustered, with conspicuous small fruit stalk, crescent-shaped, abaxial keel with teeth, beak slender and long. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 536; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Rosén & Wahlenb.) Arcang.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides, stream banks or marsh shallow water places.
Morphology: Capsule 4–6 attached borne on overall fruit stalk on, small fruit stalk very short or without.

Author: Hook.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Oceania, China not distributed in, ci in front of recorded is filiform powdery alga cuo wu jian ding zhi.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome long and creeping; leaf blade textured ren, linear, slightly curved, wide 6–15 mm, entire; rou spike terminal or axillary, stamens 3; fruit fusiform, smooth, with beak.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and coastal areas of eastern China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore salt fields or inland saline-alkaline lake.
Morphology: Leaves narrowly linear, wide 0.3–0.5 mm, leaf sheath amplexicaul; spike enclosing on sheath inner, with 2 flowers flower, stamens 2, carpels 4–6; achene with short beak. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 535; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Miki) Hartog
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Asia tropical regions and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow seabed.
Morphology: Leaves 1–4 alternate, leaf blade linear, wide 0.5–0.8 mm, apex often flat truncate or slightly round, without in teeth, only with 2 lateral teeth; flowers small, solitary or 2 flowers and borne, without perianth; fruit ovate, long about 2 mm, beak lateral.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Forssk.) Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in estuaries curved or mangrove forest.
Morphology: Leaves wide 0.8–1.4 mm, base attenuate, apex often 3 teeth, in teeth mostly few rounded-obtuse, 2 lateral teeth conspicuous; fruit ovoid, long 2.5 mm, beak terminal.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Asch. & Schweinf.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hot band shallow sea mud textured seabed.
Morphology: Stolon slender slender; leaves 2–5 clustered, leaf blade linear, falcate-shaped, long 7–15 cm, wide 4 mm below, entire, leaf sheath slightly purple; flower single flowers terminal; fruit oblique and flattened, sessile, abaxial keel with pointed projecting teeth.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Asch.) Dandy
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and dong sand archipelagos; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hot band shallow sea mud textured seabed.
Morphology: Rhizome slender slender; leaves 2–3 alternate, leaf blade awl-shaped-shaped acicular, long 7–10 cm, wide 1–2 mm; cyme branched; fruit oblique obovate, with beak.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Kurz
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, open wild or forest interior.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree., phanerophyte
Morphology: Stem much-branched, without qi roots; leaves band-shaped, margin with spiny; male inflorescence spike-shaped, female inflorescence head-shaped; clustered flower fruit elliptic, drupe bundles 2–3 locules, persistent stigma 2, forked. Flowering period May–June.

Author: Parkinson ex Du Roi
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal shrubland or sandy beach.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Stem much-branched, with aerial roots; clustered flower fruit round head-shaped, drupe bundles 4–12 locules, persistent stigma appearing papillae-shaped. Flowering period January–May.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Otto Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Stem much-branched, with aerial roots; leaves narrower, leaves apex becoming narrow and with long tail bian, up to 15 cm; ovary (4-)5-6(-7) locules; fruit relatively small, subglobose, long about 8 cm, diameter 8 cm, consisting of 50-60 drupe bundles composed of, each one drupe bundles long about 2.5 cm, wide about 2 cm.

Author: Bory
Distribution: Native to Africa, China South China coastal areas introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem sparsely branched, with aerial roots; leaves margin with red spiny; clustered flower fruit nearly round spherical-shaped, drupe bundles 4–6 locules, persistent stigma ma ti-shaped. Flowering period May–August; fruiting period November–December.

Author: (Pall.) C.E.Lundstr.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang, Beijing and elsewhere cultivated; western Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow, gravelly sloping ground or moist saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Rhizome thickened horizontally creeping; leaves wide 1–2 cm; flower yellow, outer tepals ti qin shape, style branched flattened segments-shaped; capsule with 6 strips wings-shaped ridges. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 21, 539; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Bunge) Baker
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow, gravelly sloping ground or moist saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Flowers bluish purple, or tepals upper part is bluish purple, zhao part is yellow.

Author: (Fisch.) Koidz.
Distribution: Widely distributed northern China regions; widely distributed in arid regions of Asia.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or saline-alkaline flats.
Morphology: Rhizome woody and obliquely spreading, covered with fibers; leaves wide 4–6 mm; flowers pale blue, blue or bluish purple, outer tepals oblanceolate; capsule long, with 6 ribs. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–September.
Uses: Ornamental, medicinal, ecological use, soil and water conservation, fiber
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Kanitz
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China, Sichuan and Tibet; xi tian mountain mountain veins and elsewhere also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas toward sunny grassland.
Morphology: Old leaf sheath persistent; leaves jian ren, narrow linear, wide about 3 mm; flower stem shorter, with 1-2 flowers flower, flowers bluish purple, diameter 5.5-7 cm, perianth lobes oblanceolate, middle part with white veins; fruit long obovate to cylindrical. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (D.Don) Soó
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist meadow.
Morphology: Succulent tuber palmately divided; leaves wide 1.5–3 cm; inflorescence spike-shaped, flower purplish red, lip lobes with spots striations. Flowering period June–August.

Author: Turcz. ex Regel
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe salinized and alkalized sandy land, grassland or dry slopes.
Morphology: Scales stem compact clustered forming tuft, with fiber; leaves half round column-shaped, coarse 0.25–1 mm; umbel half spherical-shaped, flower purplish red, filaments connate at the base forming tube-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Fisch. ex Link
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant soft, stem straight, green; leaves-shaped branches slightly arcuate curved, not is spiny-shaped, usually oblique li; berry at maturity red. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.

Author: N.A.Ivanova ex Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or mostly sand wilderness.
Morphology: Plant structure jian ting, stem jiong bent, white; leaves-shaped branches slightly gang stiff, sometimes mostly few used as spiny-shaped, generally spreading or lower inclined. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–September.

Author: Schult. & Schult.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, Gobi gravel desert, riverbanks or wasteland.
Morphology: Roots slender and long or slightly swollen large, coarse 2–5 mm; branched and leaves-shaped branches without cartilaginous textured teeth; pedicel long 6–25 mm. Flowering period May, fruiting period August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, moist salinized and alkalized sandy land or slopes.
Morphology: Roots succulent, round column-shaped, coarse 7–15 mm; branched and leaves-shaped branches with cartilaginous textured teeth. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August.

Author: Wurmb
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Asia tropical regions and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hot band rivers into sea mouth mudflat or seashore mangrove forest.
Morphology: Leaves pinnatisect, petiole hard and coarse, pinnae numerous, regular arranged; inflorescence short on leaves; infructescence round head-shaped, fruit drupe-shaped, with six ridges. Flowering period July, fruiting period March.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan, Taiwan and Yunnan; hot band coastal areas widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Stem with ring-shaped leaves hen; leaves pinnatisect, long 3–4 m; fruit ovate spherical-shaped, base with 3 kong, fruit qiang containing with endosperm, embryo and sap ye (ye zi water). Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.

Author: (L.) G.Brückn.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy land.
Morphology: Stem often branched; leaves he leaves-shaped, long 4–10 cm, glabrous; leaf sheath with jie hairs; flowers small, blue, 1–5 flowers clusters borne on sheath-shaped involucral bracts inner; capsule spherical-shaped. Fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Regel & Maack
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Central China and East China; Asia other areas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in ponds, lakes and marshes or rice fields.
Morphology: Plant smooth glabrous, basal leaves wide ovate-shaped cordate, petiole very long, sometimes swollen large forming sac-shaped; raceme terminal, flowers blue; capsule long ovate orbicular. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Buch.-Ham. ex Graebn.) Buch.-Ham. ex Juz.
Distribution: Widely distributed northern China regions; northern Asia regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes and marshes, ponds or streams etc. Shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade lower part abaxial surface appearing keel-shaped projections or appearing three-angled; panicle spreading branched; infructescence round spherical-shaped, fruit inverted conical, with ridges. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: C.Presl
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.4–0.9 cm; female male inflorescence compact lian jie; female flowers without bracteole, stigma wide spatulate, white filiform hairs and style nearly etc. Long. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Maximum Salinity: 500 mM (seawater salinity); 200 mM (20 dS/m); 70 mM (7 dS/m)

Author: L.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.5–1.5 cm; female style head lanceolate, white filiform hairs conspicuous short on style. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Lepech.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.2–0.4 cm; female male inflorescence away from; male inflorescence axis with grayish white or pale yellow pubescent, apex not forked; female style head spatulate. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in each type shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.4–0.9 cm; female male inflorescence away from; male inflorescence axis densely covered brown flattened pubescent, single protruding or forked; female flowers with bracteole, stigma narrow linear, and style nearly etc. Wide. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition front matter

Author: Pers.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.3–0.8 cm; male inflorescence axis with sparse white or yellow-brown pubescent, from not forked; stigma wide linear to lanceolate, than style wide. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Kronf.) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Mainly distributed North China.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.3–0.5 cm; bracteole spatulate; stigma lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Funck
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.1–0.2 cm; flower scape base only with sheath-shaped leaves, without leaf blade; male inflorescence axis glabrous, base with 1 leaves-shaped bract. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Beijing and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem tufted, diameter 1–2 mm; leaves-shaped involucral bracts nearly resembling stem extending long, apex spiny pointed; compound cyme nearly accessory lateral, flower several flowers clustered; capsule ovoid, distant exceeding protruding perianth. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China and elsewhere; northern Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides, marshland or steppe wetland.
Morphology: Stem slightly compressed, diameter 0.5–1.5 mm; leaf blade linear, wide 0.5–1 mm; terminal compound cyme, flowers solitary; capsule ovoid, exceeding protruding perianth. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Satake & Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides, pond margins wetland or wet meadow.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide about 1 mm; flowers solitary; capsule oblong-ovate, short on persistent perianth segments. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wet grassland, lake shores, riversides or marshland.
Morphology: Stem slender weak; leaf blade linear, wide about 1 mm; inflorescence two forked clustered umbel-shaped, with leaves-shaped involucral bracts, flower arranged sparse pine; capsule three ridges-shaped elliptic. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 540; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition front matter

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow, sandy beach, riversides or ditch banks wetland.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened tubular, with conspicuous zhu nodes-shaped transverse ge; compound cyme terminal, small capitulum with 5–10 flowers flower; capsule three ridges-shaped long ovate, exceeding protruding tepals. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Huds.) Link
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline-alkaline land meadow or sandy soil.
Morphology: Culms spreading borne, nearly round column-shaped; leaves flat zhang, wide 1–3.5 mm; spike terminal, spikelets two rows attached, long 3.5–4 mm, flower two -like; lower position bristles short on nutlets or not persistent in. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Curtis
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides wetland or saline-alkaline marshland.
Morphology: Leaves wide 10–15 mm; spikelets 5–10, male spikelets 3–6, sometimes base with few female flowers, female spikelets long 3–6 cm, scale lanceolate, fruit sac leathery, with short beak; 3 stigmas. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Kar. & Kir
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline grassland.
Morphology: Leaves wide 3–5 mm; spikelets 3–4, male spikelets 1–2, female spikelets long 2–4 cm, scale ovate, fruit sac wheat-like culms yellow, often with brown spots kuai, with conspicuous veins. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in North China.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaves wide 2–3 mm; fruit sac wheat-like culms yellow, without brown spots kuai, at maturity veins inconspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, grassland or roadsides wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves wide 2–3 mm; spikelets 3–4, male spikelets 1–2, female spikelets ovate or oblong, long 8–18 mm, scale round ovate, fruit sac ovate orbicular, veins inconspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Steud.
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of eastern China; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach sandy land, wetland or field margins.
Morphology: Female spikelets oblong or nearly ovate, long 1.5–2 cm, scale broadly ovate, fruit sac oblong, long 7–8.5 mm; nutlets oblong, long about 5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of eastern China; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Ground lower stolon well developed, culms sparse tufted; fruit sac ovate, long about 6–6.5 mm; nutlets obovate, long about 3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: M.Bieb. ex Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline soil grassland or mountain gullies moist places.
Morphology: Female spikelets ovate or oblong, long 1–3 cm, scale ovate, with short awn, fruit sac ovate, long about 5 mm, veins emarginate; nutlets obovate, long about 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Boott
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains, lakeshores halophytic meadow or marsh meadow.
Morphology: Stolon well developed; lower part bract bristles-shaped; spikelets 2–4, male spikelets 1, female spikelets ovate or oblong, subsessile, scale only with midrib, fruit sac suborbicular, with very short beak. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and East China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marshland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Stolon slender and long; leaves wide 3–6 mm; female spikelets sessile, long 1–4.5 cm, female flowers scale pale brown, with 1–3 veins, apex acuminate, fruit sac long on scale, beak conspicuous, beak mouth with 2 teeth; stigma caducous. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; Korea, Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands, grass tuft or sandy land.
Morphology: Culms tufted; bract leaves-shaped and long; spike pointed pyramidal-shaped cylindrical, spikelets numerous, male female shun xu, fruit sac middle part above margin with wide wings, rust-colored yellow; stigma 2. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Russia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist river floodplains grassland.
Morphology: Culms with narrow wings; bract bristles-shaped; inflorescence constricted forming spike-shaped, spikelets numerous, male female shun xu, female flowers scale apex with awn pointed, fruit sac ovate-lanceolate, with conspicuous veins, on half part margin with narrow wings, base with sea woolly textured. Flowering and fruiting period August.

Author: Podp.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland.
Morphology: Culms without narrow wings; female flowers scale apex with short awn pointed, fruit sac ovate-lanceolate, both surfaces veins all inconspicuous; nutlets ovate, long 2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July.

Author: C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, steppe or saline land.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long; spike ovoid, spikelets 3–6, ovate, male female shun xu, scale broadly ovate, fruit sac flat convex-shaped, leathery; nutlets slightly sparse pine ground enclosing on fruit sac in; stigma 2. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: (Trautv.) V.I.Krecz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marsh becoming meadow or saline meadow.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long; leaf blade involute forming needle-like, wide about 1–1.5 mm; fruit sac flat convex-shaped, papery or membranous. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: M.-Bieb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Russia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or barren slopes sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long; leaves wide 1–2 mm, pairs bent or involute; spikelets 3–6, male female shun xu, dense forming capitulum; fruit sac at maturity very inflated forming spherical-shaped or elliptic, wide 5–10 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in coastal areas of eastern China; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Rhizome creeping; leaves wide 3–8 mm, leathery; spikelets numerous, dioecious, female flowers scale long 1.2–l.6 cm; fruit sac ovate-lanceolate, flat convex-shaped, thickly leathery; 3 stigmas. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Uses: Forage, medicinal, ecological use, sand stabilization
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Burm.f.) Trin. ex Hensch.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in marsh wetland.
Morphology: Tuber flattened spherical-shaped; culms coarse robust, diameter 1.5–3 mm, with transverse ge membrane; spikelets diameter 6–7 mm; nutlets brown colored, smooth, style base base with conspicuous ling-shaped ring. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: (Roem. & Schult.) Link ex Bluff, Nees & Schauer
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore moist saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spikelets long 2–4 mm, with 6–10 flower; nutlets diameter 9–1.5 mm, smooth, persistent style base three angles-shaped subulate. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: (L.) Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore wetland or water wetland.
Morphology: Spikelets ovate spherical-shaped, base 3–4 segments scale in hollow without flower; nutlets black and slightly purple, lower position bristles 6–8 strips, rust-colored colored, style base nearly on short conical. Flowering and fruiting period January–April.

Author: (Link) Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia, western part and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland.
Morphology: Spikelets ovate or oblong, scale oblong-lanceolate, base one segments scale in hollow without flower; nutlets yellow-brown, lower position bristles 6 strips, style base suborbicular. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: (L.) Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marsh wetland.
Morphology: Spikelets oblong, scale ovate, base two segments in hollow without flower, stigma 2; nutlets yellow-brown, lower position bristles 4 strips, style base conspicuous fleshy large, long is nutlets 1/2 or guo half. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marsh wetland.
Morphology: Culms with acute ribs strips; spikelets brown or wheat-like culms yellow, scale ovate, base 2 segments in hollow without flower; nutlets pale yellow, lower position bristles 4 strips, style base broadly ovate, long not to nutlets 1/2. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Rottb.) C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore or river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Culms tufted; leaves linear, wide 0.4–0.8 mm; spikelets forming clusters ground arranged forming head-shaped anthela, scale brown colored or yellowish green; nutlets with reticulate striations. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: (Wall.) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, riversides sandy land or water wetland.
Morphology: Leaves wide 0.5 mm; spikelets single ground arranged is simple or compound anthela, scale brown colored or dark brown colored, glabrous, apex obtuse; nutlets with small projections. Flowering and fruiting period April–December.

Author: Debeaux & Franch.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and Central China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy land.
Morphology: Anthela simple or compound, spikelets wide 1.5–2.5 mm, glumes rust-colored, mucronate, not curved outward; nutlets oblong, yellowish white, surface reticulate. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Poir.) R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Rhizome very long; leaves wide 1.5–3.2 mm, both surfaces densely covered with white silky hairs; anthela simple single, with 2–4 radiating branches, spikelets 3–15 clustered forming head-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Miq. ex Franch. & Sav.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or salt marsh ground.
Morphology: Culms base without leaf blade sheath; leaves wide 1 mm; anthela simple single, spikelets solitary, scale with 1 strips veins, abaxial surface upper part covered with grayish white short-pubescent; nutlets nearly on smooth. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Miq. ex Franch. & Sav.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or salt marsh ground.
Morphology: Culms base without leaf blade sheath; leaves wide 1 mm; anthela simple single, spikelets solitary, scale with 1 strips veins, abaxial surface upper part covered with grayish white short-pubescent; nutlets nearly on smooth. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Vahl
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy land.
Morphology: Culms tufted; anthela compound protruding, spikelets ovate, scale with 3–5 veins, stigma 2; nutlets surface with transverse oblong reticulate striations, longitudinal ribs conspicuous raised, base with brown short-stalked. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Nees) T.Koyama
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; Japan, Korea and Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys water wetland, seashore or salt marsh ground.
Morphology: Spikelets usually 1, terminal, han with 2, round column-shaped, long 8–30 mm, scale spirally arranged, brown colored; nutlets brown, base stalked. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Retz.) Vahl
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore moist saline soil ground.
Morphology: Spikelets single terminal, elliptic or oblong, long 5–8 mm, scale gray-green white; nutlets obovate, gray black, surface with transverse oblong reticulate striations and wart-like projections, stalk very short. Flowering and fruiting period March–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Vahl
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides terraces or wetland.
Morphology: Anthela simple or compound; spikelets spiny wei-shaped, with ridges angles, scale with long 1–1.8 mm awn-shaped mucronate, often toward outer curved; nutlets nearly smooth. Flowering and fruiting period September–November.

Author: (Forssk.) Bubani
Distribution: Widely distributed throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy land or slopes moist places.
Morphology: Leaves wide 0.7–1.5 mm; anthela compound protruding or mostly secondary compound protruding, scale brown, abaxial surface with green keel-shaped projections; nutlets wide obovate, surface with conspicuous transverse oblong reticulate striations. Flowering and fruiting period July–November.

Author: (Ohwi) Y.C.Yang & M.Zhan
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open water wetland.
Morphology: Culms acute three-angled; leaves wide 5–10 mm; anthela simple single, radiating branches up to 7 cm, spikelets ovate orbicular, rust-colored brown, lower position bristles 6 strips, several and nutlets etc. Long, 3 stigmas; nutlets three ridges-shaped. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (F.Schmidt) T.V.Egorova
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open water wetland.
Morphology: Anthela with few radiating branches or short contracted forming head-shaped, scale brown to dark brown, anthers long 2–4 mm, stigma 2; nutlets flattened, both surfaces slightly concave, long 3–3.5 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Palla
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and Taiwan; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow beach.
Morphology: Anthela often short contracted forming head-shaped, scale bright orange brown, anthers long 2–4 mm, stigma 2; nutlets flattened, both surfaces slightly concave, long about 2.5 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 542; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Roth) T.Koyama
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt marsh ground or salt-lake shores wetland.
Morphology: Culms three-angled; anthela often short contracted forming head-shaped, spikelets ovate, scale pale yellow, lower position bristles 6 strips, long about is nutlets one half, stamens long about 1 mm, stigma 2; nutlets paired convex-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Boeckeler) Soják
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores or ditch banks open water wetland.
Morphology: Culms acute three-angled, ridges wings-shaped; bract is culms extending long, up to 25 cm; inflorescence accessory lateral, spikelets oblong, scale slightly notched, with awn, lower position bristles 6 strips, with inverted spiny; nutlets long about 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (L.) Palla
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; northern Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in streams margins shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Culms three-angled; bract is culms extending long, long 1.5–7 cm; radiating branches long up to 5 cm, spikelets ovate, scale with mucronate, lower position bristles 3–5 strips. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (C.C.Gmel.) Palla
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes and marshes, ponds or streams etc. Shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Creeping rhizome coarse robust, culms round column-shaped; bract is culms extending long, short on inflorescence, radiating branches long up to 5 cm, spikelets long 4–7 mm, scale abaxial surface with tie rust-colored colored projections small dots, stigma 2; nutlets paired convex-shaped, lower position bristles 6 strips, with inverted spiny. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.) Palla
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia, South Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marshland or shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Spikelets long 6–15 mm, scale abaxial surface smooth, 3 stigmas; nutlets flattened three-angled. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (L.) J.Jung & H.K.Choi
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakes and marshes, ponds or streams margins shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Culms tufted, acute three-angled; bract erect or slightly spreading, long 2–9 cm; spikelets clustered forming head-shaped, scale ovate, lower position bristles 6 strips, with inverted spiny, 3 stigmas; nutlets flattened three-angled. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: (Roxb.) Lye
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in tidal wetland or watersides marshland.
Morphology: Culms round column-shaped; bract is culms extending long, long 3–15 cm; spikelets clustered forming head-shaped, accessory lateral, scale ovate; nutlets obovate, lower position bristles 5–6 strips, than nutlets short or etc. Long. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: (L.) Soják
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Beijing introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy land or seashore sand dunes.
Morphology: Culms tufted, three-angled, with slender teeth; bract is culms extending long, long on inflorescence; spikelets clustered forming compact capitulum, borne on long short not one radiating branches on. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan, Guangdong and Hainan; Asia and Oceania coastal areas also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Creeping rhizome long; leaves dense clustered on stem upper part, leathery; spike 2–7 dense clustered on stem apex, spikelets only with 1 flowers bisexual flowers, scale succulent; nutlets round column-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period September–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Retz.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China coastal areas; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline land.
Morphology: Creeping rhizome long, with tuber; radiating branches slightly spreading, long not exceeding 2 cm, spikelets compact, nearly resembling finger-like-shaped arranged, wide 2–3 mm, slightly swollen, rachis with narrow wings; nutlets obovate or elliptic. Flowering and fruiting period July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China south of each ground; Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes wasteland or watersides tidal wetland.
Morphology: With tuber; leaves wide 2–5 mm, flat zhang; radiating branches spreading, long up to 12 cm, spikelets spike-shaped arranged, linear, rachilla winged, scale covering wa-shaped, dark xue red; nutlets three ridges-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–November.

Author: (Vahl) T.Koyama
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow water wetland.
Morphology: Creeping rhizome long; culms base with long sheath, sheath apex with short leaf blade or without leaf blade; spikelets spike-shaped arranged, very spreading, linear, rachilla with transparent narrow margins, scale without mucronate, involute. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Rhizome short; anthela short contracted forming head-shaped, accessory lateral, without radiating branches; spikelets oblong, slightly swollen, scale boat-shaped-shaped, two lateral dark xue red; nutlets abaxial adaxial compressed, surface toward rachilla. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 31, 543; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Rottb.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt marsh ground, rice fields or valleys shaded and moist sandy land.
Morphology: Rhizome short; radiating branches numerous, spikelets numerous, compact, erect, linear, scale wheat-like culms colored or red brown colored, apex obtuse; nutlets two lateral compressed, ridges toward rachilla, flattened paired convex-shaped, with slender dots. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Houtt.
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan, Guangdong, Hainan and xi sand archipelagos; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline soil on or saline-alkaline marshland.
Morphology: Anthela compound protruding, spike round tube-shaped, spikelets mostly and flattened round, pale wheat-like culms yellow, rachilla base upper surface with joint, scale without keel projections; nutlets three leng shape, surface toward rachilla. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy land.
Life Form: Annual or short ming perennial herb
Morphology: Anthela large and sparse spreading; spike long round-shaped tubular, spikelets sparse arranged, linear, curved bent, rachilla with numerous joint, often lian same persistent scale one qi deciduous; nutlets three ridges-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (F.Muell.) Bauters
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Japan, Vietnam and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves wide 1–2 mm; spike 2–4 clustered appearing head-shaped, ovate, scale oblanceolate, spikelets with 2 segments small scale and 1 flowers bisexual flowers; nutlets three ridges-shaped, long about 1 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Tang & F.T.Wang) Bauters
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Zhejiang; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Spikelets with 2 segments small scale and 1 flowers bisexual flowers; nutlets three ridges-shaped, long about 0.5 mm, only is scale-like bract surrounding. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Mast.) B.G.Briggs & L.A.S.Johnson
Distribution: Distributed in Guangxi and Hainan; Southeast Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or forest interior wetland.
Morphology: Rhizome densely covered with gray yellow soft hairs; culms round column-shaped, leaf sheath leathery; spikelets rows forming spike-shaped panicle, flowers small, single -like, dioecious, outer whorls 2 tepals bent die appearing boat-shaped-shaped; fruit elliptic. Flowering period April–July, fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Taiwan; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore ditch banks or riversides open woodland.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate, two rows, apex gradually narrow forming one flattened, disc rolled rolled xu; panicle terminal, flowers small, two -like; drupe globose, at maturity band yellow red. Flowering period April–July, fruiting period September–November.

Author: (Honda) Honda
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, South China and Southwest China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Culms lower part appressed wo ground surface; panicle sparse pine, main axis coarse robust, branched without branchlets, spikelets long 5–6 mm, two lateral very compressed, smooth glabrous, apex without awn, containing 1 florets, stamens 6. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (L.) Sw.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Central China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in streams margins or marsh wetland.
Morphology: Plant rough; panicle branched mostly with branchlets, lower part often bare exposed, leaf sheath in often with yinflorescence and spikelets, stamens 3; caryopsis dao valley-shaped and flattened, rough. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Griseb.) Hance ex F.Muell.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in streams margins or marsh wetland.
Morphology: Rhizome well developed; leaf sheath thickened; leaf blade flattened wide large, wide 15–30 mm; panicle long 30–50 cm, spikelets single -like, monoecious, female spikelets tubular; caryopsis cylindrical. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Trin.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China, East China and Southwest China; Mongolia and Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, yellow soil hills or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Leaf blade longitudinal rolled resembling needle-like; culms base sheath inner with yin hidden spikelets; panicle long about 20 cm, spikelets containing 1 florets, two -like, shedding nodes on glumes of on; awn rough glabrous or with slender spiny hairs, two times geniculate curved. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Roshev.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy slopes or steppe.
Morphology: Culms base sheath inner without yin hidden spikelets; culms borne ligule long 1.5–2 mm; inflorescence often is terminal leaf sheath enclosing, not entire exserted; awn rough, awn needle-like up to 10 cm, often is arcuate shape or ring-shaped curved. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: P.A.Smirn.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy steppe.
Morphology: Culms borne ligule long 3–10 mm; glumes long 3–4.5 cm, lemma long 1.5–1.7 cm; awn rough, awn needle-like long 12–18 cm, often is arcuate shape or ring-shaped curved. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Roshev.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain front of proluvial fans, flat floodplain flats or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Culms borne ligule long 2–3 mm; glumes long 1.8–2.5 cm, lemma long about 1 cm; awn rough, first awn column long 1.5–2 cm, second awn column long about 1 cm, awn needle-like long 9–10 cm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Roshev.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky gravel slopes or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Culms borne ligule long 1–2 mm; lemma long 9 mm, apex rough, with 1 ring short hairs; awn one times geniculate curved, awn column glabrous, long 1–1.5 cm, awn needle-like long 6–7 cm, with up to 5 mm feathery. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: P.A.Smirn.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, Gobi gravel desert or river floodplains gravelly ground.
Morphology: Leaf blade lower surface rough se or with pubescent; inflorescence constricted, lemma long 7–12 mm, abaxial side hairs appearing strips-shaped; awn one times geniculate curved, long 4.5–7 cm, awn column and awn needle-like same with white pinnate-shaped hairs. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; in nan and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, dry slopes or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Ligule short and obtuse; inflorescence base often is terminal leaf sheath enclosing, glumes long 12–16 mm, lemma long 5–9 mm, abaxial side with strips-shaped hairs; awn two times geniculate curved, entire with pinnate-shaped hairs. Flowering period May–July.

Author: Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; the Himalayas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes meadow, mountain front of proluvial fans or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, branched long 3–6 cm, spikelets appearing purple, glumes long 13–17 mm, lemma long 8–10 mm, abaxial side spreading borne slender hairs; awn long 6–9 cm, two times geniculate curved, entire with pinnate-shaped hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Trin.) M.Nobis, P.D.Gudkova & A.Nowak
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized grass flat or sandy soil slopes.
Morphology: Culms dense tufted; ligule pointed lanceolate, long 5–10 mm; panicle large type spreading; spikelets long 4.5–7 mm, containing 1 flower; awn erect or slightly curved, long 5–12 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Uses: Forage, fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Huds.
Distribution: Native to Europe, Beijing and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores moist sandy land.
Morphology: Culms dense tufted; panicle constricted, erect, long 10–20 cm; spikelets long 15–25 mm, spreading needle-like oblong; lemma long 10–12 mm, with 7 veins, awn slender straight, long 2–6 mm. Flowering period May–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys or tidal wet grassland.
Morphology: Panicle long 12 cm, wide 2–4 cm; spikelets oblong, long 12–16 mm, internodes not easily outer exposed; lemma long 6–8 mm, awn long 3–8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Fisch.) Nevski
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground steppe.
Morphology: Spike compact, long 9–12 cm, rachis cui and easily broken, spikelets 2–3 borne on 1 nodes, containing 2–3 florets, lemma hairy, palea slightly short on lemma; awn long 1–2 mm. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Scribn. & Merr.) Á.Löve
Distribution: Native to Canada and United States, Shanxi introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides or barren wild sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant bright green; leaves wide 3–12 mm; spike coarse 8–17 mm, spikelets each nodes 2–7, containing 3–7 flower, glumes slightly short on spikelets, veins not conspicuous; lemma acute or with short awn. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Lam.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Spike coarse 10–20 mm, spikelets each nodes 4–6, long 15–22 mm, containing 3–5 florets, glumes long 15–20 mm, lemma hairy. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 551; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: C.Yen & J.L.Yang
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 15–42 mm; spikelets each nodes 3–5, containing 5–10 florets, glumes unequal long, with 3 veins, first glumes long 10–12 mm, lemma densely covered with long hairs, first lemma long 9–13 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Shanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in forest margins
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 4–6, containing 5–10 florets, glumes nearly etc. Long, with 1 vein, long 10–13 mm, lemma densely covered with long hairs, first lemma long 8–10 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: L.B.Cai & Tong L.Zhang
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland or river valleys.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 3–4, containing 8–12 florets, glumes nearly etc. Long, with 1 vein, long 10–13 mm, lemma abaxial side glabrous, first lemma long 10–14 mm. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September.

Author: L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai Xining.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 8–11 mm, containing 3–4 florets, glumes oblong-lanceolate, apex awn long 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: L.B.Cai & X.Su
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Shanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in grass slope or desert.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2, long 9–16 mm, containing 4–5 florets, glumes conical spiny shape, long 8–16 mm, several without awn, lemma with 2–3 mm long short awn, first lemma long 9–14 mm, palea apex pointed. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Trin.) Pilg.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in half desert salinized grassland.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2, long 10–14 mm, containing 2–3 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, with 1 vein, short on spikelets, covering first lemma base, toward on zhu gradually narrow forming awn, lemma with short awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.B.Cai & X.Su
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang leaves cheng.
Habitat: Occurs in grass slope.
Morphology: Spike long 6–13 cm, wide 5–7.5 mm; spikelets each nodes 1–2, containing 4–5 florets, glumes with 3–4 mm long short awn, first lemma long 7–8.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: N.R.Cui & D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow.
Morphology: Spike long 5–11 cm; spikelets each nodes 2, long 7–11 mm, containing 3–5 florets, glumes narrowly lanceolate, with 1 vein, long 5–9 mm, covering first lemma base, first lemma long 7–9 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys or forest margins.
Morphology: Spike long 23–32 cm, fully chui, pale brown; spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 11–15 mm, containing 5–9 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, with 1 vein, long 9–11 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Roshev.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Native to Turkmenistan, Inner Mongolia introduced cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized grassland.
Morphology: Culms glabrous; spikelets each nodes 2–3, appressed close to rachis, linear, long 10–15 mm, containing 3–5 florets, spikelets internodes covered with pubescent; glumes lanceolate, and spikelets etc. Long, with 3 veins, long 8–12 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: G.H.Zhu & S.L.Chen
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia and Shanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland or sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2, long 18–25 mm, containing 5–9 florets, glumes lanceolate, with 3–5 veins, long 11–16 mm, covering first lemma base, first lemma long 10–12 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: (L.) Hochst.
Distribution: Native to Europe, Beijing and elsewhere introduced cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open alkaline -like sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant tufted, conspicuous blue gray; spike coarse 15–25 mm, spikelets each nodes 2, long 12–30 mm, with 2–5 florets, glumes and spikelets nearly etc. Long, with 3 veins, lemma densely covered with long hairs. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Turcz.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2, long 11–15 mm, containing 3–4 florets, glumes and spikelets nearly etc. Long, fully entire covering glume segments, margin wide membranous, anthers long 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Trin.) Pilg.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Hebei and Shandong; Mongolia, Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores.
Morphology: Inflorescence dense hairy; spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 15–20 mm, containing 2–5 florets, glumes oblong-lanceolate, only covering first lemma base. Flowering period May, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Claus) Pilg.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline meadow, plains or river floodplains.
Morphology: Ligule long 0.5 mm; inflorescence densely covered with pubescent, internodes long 3–6 mm; spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 8–13 mm, containing 3–5 florets, glumes nearly subulate, and spikelets nearly etc. Long, not covering first lemma base. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Drobow) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes.
Morphology: Ligule long 2–3 mm; internodes long 6–20 mm; spikelets long 15–19 mm, glumes linear-lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: L.Zhi & L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 3–4, long 6–13 mm, containing 3–6 florets, glumes lanceolate, long 4–7 mm, lemma oblong-lanceolate, abaxial side covered with short-pubescent, with inconspicuous 3 veins, anthers long 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Buckley) Pilg.
Distribution: Native to North America, China ou with introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming grassland.
Morphology: Rhizome well developed; spike coarse 5–15 mm, each nodes spikelets 1–3, rachis glabrous, spikelets long 10–22 mm, with 3–7 florets, glumes short on spikelets, lemma glabrous, awn long 3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Kar. & Kir.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized desert meadow or plains oases.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 8–15 mm, containing 2–6 florets, glumes slender conical spiny shape, long 5–7 mm, with 1 vein, not covering first lemma base, first lemma long 5–8 mm, often smooth glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Georgi) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in northern China regions; northern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or saline meadow.
Morphology: Spikelets each nodes 2–3, long 10–20 mm, containing 4–7 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, long 8–15 mm, with 3 veins, lemma with membranous margin, without slender hairs, base disc with long hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Uses: Forage, fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use

Distribution: Distributed in northern China regions; northern Asia widely distributed.

Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Uses: Forage, fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use

Author: (L.B.Cai)L.B.Cai & X.Su
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland or wasteland.
Morphology: First lemma long 9–10 mm, lemma base disc with long hairs, inner glume nearly etc. On lemma. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: L.B.Cai & X.Su
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grass slope.
Morphology: Spike rou shun, lustrous, spikelets only containing 4–5 florets, glumes abaxial side with keel, margin membranous, lemma broadly lanceolate, abaxial side smooth glabrous, with 5 veins, palea conspicuous short on lemma, keel on without slender hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: (Trin.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, river floodplains or saline-alkaline low ground meadow.
Morphology: Rachis and spikelets glabrous; spikelets each nodes 1–2, long 10–22 mm, containing 5–10 florets, glumes conical shape, short on spikelets, long 6–8 mm, with 3 veins, first lemma long 7–9 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Uses: Forage, fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: H.X.Xiao
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Jilin.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland or forest margins.
Morphology: Rachis and spikelets conspicuous covered with long hairs; spikelets each nodes 1–2, long 10–20 mm, containing 4–8 florets, glumes lanceolate, with 1–3 veins, lemma lanceolate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: S.L.Lu & Y.H.Wu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas meadow.
Morphology: Spike sparse pine, slender and long; spikelets each nodes often 1, containing 2–3 florets, glumes lanceolate, etc. Long, long 9–11 mm, with 3 veins, inner glume conspicuous short on lemma. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang leaves cheng.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Rachis at nodes with long soft hairs; spikelets middle part each nodes often 1, containing 4–6 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, long 10–15 mm, with 3 veins, margin membranous with slender hairs, inner glume nearly etc. Long on lemma. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or forest margins.
Morphology: Rachis densely covered with pubescent; spikelets each nodes 1–2, containing 4–8 florets, conspicuous with short-stalked, glumes lanceolate, long 6–7.5 mm, with 1–3 veins, margin membranous without slender hairs, inner glume slightly short on lemma. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: S.L.Lu & Y.H.Wu
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1–3 mm; rachis at nodes hairy; spikelets each nodes often 1, containing 3–5 florets, glumes conical shape or narrowly lanceolate, long 7–10 mm, without veins or with 1 vein, lemma hairy, margin with slender hairs. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: L.B.Cai
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland or lake shores.
Morphology: Leaf sheath smooth, without membranous margin; spikelets middle part each nodes only 2, glumes smooth, lanceolate, with long 1–1.5 mm mucronate, inner glume short on lemma, upper part keel sparsely covered small spiny. Flowering and fruiting period August.

Author: D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas saline meadow.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 2–6 mm; spikelets each nodes often 1, containing 3–4 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, with 1 vein, lemma smooth glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Y.H.Wu
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, river floodplains saline-alkaline land or saline meadow.
