Morphology: Leaf blade involute like needle-like; rachis at nodes covered with stiff hairs; spikelets each nodes often 1, long 8–10 mm, containing 3 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, without veins or with 1 vein, lemma smooth glabrous, with 5 veins. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Nevski) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky slopes.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide reaching 25 mm; spikelets each nodes often 1, containing 3–5 florets, glumes conical-shaped linear, long 7–10 mm, without veins, glabrous, lemma broad lanceolate, glabrous, awn long 2–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (K.Richt.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 2–6 mm; spikelets each nodes often 1, long 11–17 mm, containing 4–7 florets, glumes linear-lanceolate, long 5–9 mm, without veins, lemma broad lanceolate, with 5–9 veins. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; bei temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, sandy soil or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spike soft, often slightly band purple, three lian spikelets glumes same type, slender and long curved soft, long 45–65 mm, long guo florets 10 times, lemma lanceolate, apex awn up to 70 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Wilensky
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist grassland.
Morphology: Culms at nodes covered with gray white soft hairs; spike often appearing gray-green, glumes long 6–8 mm, in among lemma long 6–7 mm, awn long 6–7 mm, abaxial side densely covered slender spiny hairs, anthers long 2–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Bowden
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides or sandy grassland.
Morphology: Culms at nodes glabrous; spike often appearing purple; in among lemma long about 5 mm, awn long 4–5 mm, abaxial side smooth glabrous; anthers long 1–1.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Trin.) Link
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline land or tidal wetland.
Morphology: Glumes short on lemma, in among florets lemma long 5–6 mm, abaxial side glabrous or covered with slender spiny hairs, awn long 1–2 mm; anthers long about 3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Schreb.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, northern China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland or fields.
Morphology: Leaf sheath dense hairy, auricle up to 1 mm; spike long 3–7 cm, glumes long 8–15 mm, covered with bristles, lemma apex awn long 5–15 mm; anthers long 3.5–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Throughout China widely cultivated; worldwide (tropical regions except) widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Auricle up to 8 mm; three lian spikelets entire sessile, jie can yu, rachis on at maturity jian ren not broken, lemma awn long 8–15 cm; caryopsis at maturity zhan attached on glume body, not shedding protruding. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition front matter

Author: Huds.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Qinghai, Shaanxi or Sichuan ceng with introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Culms slightly hairy; auricle often not persistent in; spike long 1.5–7 cm, glumes straight, oblique spreading, center spikelets glumes long 14–26 mm, lateral spikelets lemma lian same awn long 3–8 cm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, slopes or forest margins.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 10–20 cm, wide 5–10 mm; spike relatively sparse pine and pendulous, long 15–20 cm, spikelets arranged not oblique on one lateral, glumes conspicuous short on first florets, apex with short awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, slopes or forest margins.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 3–8 cm, wide 3–5 mm; spike relatively compact slightly pendulous, long 5–12 cm, spikelets arranged oblique on rachis one lateral, glumes oblong, long 4–5 mm, apex with awn pointed, lemma with awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Turcz. ex Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or slopes grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 5–9 mm; spike erect, wide 5–10 mm, spikelets each nodes 2, glumes lanceolate, long 8–10 mm, awn up to 5 mm, lemma entire densely covered short small rough hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes or grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 10–16 mm; spike coarse robust, wide 10–12 mm, spikelets each nodes 2–4, glumes narrowly lanceolate, long 10–13 mm, awn up to 7 mm, lemma abaxial side glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Trin.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Mongolia, Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, tidal wet grassland or slopes.
Morphology: Spike often is arcuate-shaped, spikelets solitary, glumes short on or etc. Long on first lemma, with 5–7 veins, lemma abaxial side covered with coarse hairs, margin with long slender hairs, awn long 10–30 mm, palea long only is lemma 1/2–2/3. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Honda
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wetland, slopes or forest margins.
Morphology: Spikelets solitary, glumes conspicuous short on first lemma, lemma abaxial side nearly glabrous, awn long 20–40 mm, palea about and lemma etc. Long. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: (L.) Gould
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys grassland or plains oases.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; culms sometimes covered with white shuang; leaf sheath often with pubescent; spikelets solitary, mature when shedding nodes on glumes of lower, glumes with 5–7 veins, margin membranous, lemma slightly long on palea, with 5–7 veins, awn long about 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (L.) Á.Löve
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Inner Mongolia introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores sandy land or dry sandy grassland.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; rachis on mature after easily breaking off, lateral ridges smooth glabrous, spikelets solitary, glumes apex obtuse, lemma obtuse, long 14–16 mm, first lemma long about 16 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: (Host) D.R.Dewey
Distribution: Native to Europe, Beijing and elsewhere introduced cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf sheath margin glabrous; rachis mature after not breaking off, lateral ridges with slender spiny hairs, glumes apex flat truncate, long 6–10 mm, lemma obtuse, first lemma long 10–12 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: (Roth) P.Candargy
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Hebei and Xinjiang; Russia, United States and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe.
Morphology: Spike wide 1–1.5 cm; spikelets long 15–20 mm, containing 9–11 florets, its lower often with small shape bract; glumes with 5 veins, without awn; lemma abaxial side glabrous, with 7–9 veins. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: (Fisch. ex Link) Schult.
Distribution: Distributed interior North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, sandy land or hilly ground.
Morphology: Spike slender, linear; spikelets arranged compact, not pectinate; glumes not exceeding half the length of the first lemma, apex conspicuous with short awn, lemma usually glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Gaertn.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid grassland, slopes, hills or sandy land.
Morphology: Without rhizome; spike coarse robust, wide flattened, oblong; spikelets arranged compact, appearing pectinate dentate; glumes not exceeding half the length of the first lemma, apex conspicuous with short awn, lemma densely covered with villous or stiff hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Oliv.) J.L.Yang, C.Yen & B.R.Baum
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in high elevations grassland, watersides sandy land or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Spike ovate orbicular, spikelets compact arranged and oblique on one lateral, glumes with 3–5 veins, covered with pubescent, lemma mucronate, densely covered pubescent, palea keel upper part with stiff long slender hairs. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Spreng.) Nevski
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or sandy land.
Morphology: Upper part leaf sheath not swollen; spike long 20–45 mm, spikelets long 12–15 mm, glumes smooth glabrous, lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: (Gaertn.) Nevski
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, sandy land or riverbeds gravel flat.
Morphology: Upper part leaf sheath swollen; spike ovate round-shaped elliptic, long 10–17 mm, spikelets compact arranged appearing pectinate dentate, long 6–10 mm, glumes smooth glabrous, lanceolate, abaxial side with keel, margin at maturity becoming thick or appearing angles textured. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: (L.) Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia, ground in sea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe, sandy land or dry slopes.
Morphology: Upper part leaf sheath slightly swollen; spikelets long 9–14 mm, glumes hairy, linear-lanceolate, abaxial side with keel, lemma covered with pubescent, inner glume keel extending long forming 2 short obtuse teeth. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: (K.Koch) Nevski
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or sandy land.
Morphology: Upper part leaf sheath slightly expanded large but not swollen; spikelets long 13–17 mm, glumes hairy, linear, abaxial side with keel, lemma covered with long hairs, inner glume keel extending long forming 2 long 0.5–1.5 mm awn. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China and worldwide introduced cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Spike long 5–15 cm, rachis not breaking off, spikelets sessile and solitary, with 2 can yu florets, glumes long about 10 mm, without awn, lemma long 12–15 mm, awn long 3–5 cm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to West Asia and South Asia, throughout China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Spike long 5–10 cm, at maturity not from base entire deciduous, rachis jian ren not breaking off, spikelets solitary, two lateral compressed, glumes abaxial side round, main veins on middle part above projections with keel. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: Coss.
Distribution: Native to Southwest Asia, Beijing, Henan, Shaanxi and Xinjiang and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Spike cylindrical, rachis coarse robust, spikelets xiang qian on depressed inner, at maturity zhu nodes breaking off, glumes leathery, apex truncate or with 2 teeth, its teeth all is rounded-obtuse projecting head, lemma lanceolate, apex with long about 1 cm awn. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv. ex J.Presl & C.Presl
Distribution: Native to Russia, northern China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle sparse pine, long 20–25 cm, spikelets containing 2 florets, rachilla extending extending on florets of after, first florets male, abaxial side with geniculate curved awn, second florets two -like, awn long 1–2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Spikelets containing 1–2 florets; rachilla not easily shedding nodes, lemma glabrous, second lemma without awn. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: L.
Distribution: Eurasia widely distributed, throughout China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or barren wild grassland.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, spikelets long 18–25 mm, containing 2–3 florets, pendulous, rachilla extending extending on florets after, easily shedding nodes, two glumes nearly on etc. Long, with 9 veins, lemma covered with stiff hairs or glabrous, second lemma with awn. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: (L.) K.Richt.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Northern Hemisphere very ground and high elevations regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland or meadow.
Morphology: Leaf sheath and leaves densely covered pubescent; panicle constricted appearing spike-shaped, spikelets ovate orbicular, long 4–6 mm, containing 2–3 florets, glumes long ovate, lemma apex with 2-lobed teeth, awn long 4–6 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; panicle during flowering spreading, after constricted forming spike-shaped, long 8–15 cm; spikelets two lateral compressed, containing 1 two -like florets and 2 subulate reduced lemma; glumes with very narrow wings. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region and Southwest Asia, Beijing, Hebei, Shanghai and Taiwan and elsewhere cultivated or naturalized
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Panicle compact appearing spike-shaped, ovate to oblong-ovate, long 1.5–4 cm, spikelets inverted ovate-shaped, reduced lemma 2, same shape, anthers long about 3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Trin.) Veldkamp
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or slopes grassland.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long; panicle spreading, long 3–5 cm, spikelets long 2.5–3 mm, lustrous, with 1 terminal two -like florets and 2 lateral male florets, glumes thin membranous, broadly ovate. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist meadow.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; leaf blade long lanceolate, wide 2–3 mm, ligule long 2–3.5 mm; panicle long about 6 cm, wide 1–3 cm, spikelets yellowish green, lemma without awn, palea is lemma of half. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; leaf blade narrowly linear, wide 1–1.5 mm, ligule long 0.5–1.5 mm; panicle elliptic, spreading, spikelets purple brown, lemma without awn, palea is lemma 2/3. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (L.) Desf.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or shallow water places.
Morphology: Panicle spike-shaped, spikelets containing 1 florets, from joint places deciduous, glumes apex 2 shallowly lobed, lobes apex slightly pointed, awn long 3–7 mm, lemma with short awn. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: (Haller f.) Koeler
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Eurasia temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Panicle sparse pine spreading, long 10–20 cm; spikelets linear, often containing 1 florets, lemma with 3 veins, long 3–4 mm, apex entire, awn from apex or slightly lower exserted, long 1–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Litv.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle compact, long 20–25 cm; spikelets long 9–11 mm, glumes unequal long, lemma long 4.5–5 mm, abaxial side smooth, apex often 2-lobed, awn from lobed teeth among or slightly lower exserted, long 3–4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Roth
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Eurasia temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle compact, rachilla not extending extending palea of after, spikelets long 5–7 mm, glumes nearly etc. Long, lemma long 3–3.5 mm, apex slightly 2 teeth lobed, awn from lemma abaxial middle part attached nearly exserted, long 2–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (Trin.) Trin.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland or gullies and valleys wetland.
Morphology: Ligule long 5–10 mm; panicle sparse pine spreading, spikelets long 2–5 mm, glumes long 2–5 mm, lemma base disc pubescent and glume body nearly etc. Long, awn from lemma abaxial middle part attached nearly exserted, long 1.5–4 mm. Flowering period July–September.

Author: (Schreb.) Darbysh.
Distribution: Eurasia temperate regions widely distributed, China Xinjiang with native to, other areas regions with cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, river-valley terraces or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Auricle falcate-shaped curved; panicle sparse pine spreading, spikelets long 10–15 mm, containing 4–5 florets, glumes lanceolate, lemma apex without awn or with mucronate. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Huds.) Darbysh.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, eastern China cultivated; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, river valleys or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Plant with short rhizome; auricle falcate-shaped curved; panicle sparse pine, spikelets long 10–20 mm, containing 4–9 florets, lemma apex without awn, abaxial side smooth or upper part slightly rough, anthers long about 4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Europe, Asia and Africa widely distributed, China widespread cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, wasteland or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spike shape spike long 10–20 cm, spikelets containing 4–20 florets, two lateral compressed, sessile, solitary, glumes long is spikelets 1/3, lemma oblong, apex without awn, caryopsis long about is wide 3 times. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Lam.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, worldwide widely introduced cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, wasteland or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spike shape spike, spikelets containing 10–15 florets, lateral borne on rachis on, lemma long 6–12 mm, awn long 5–15 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Gaudin
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China North China and Northwest China cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, fields or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spike shape spike, spikelets containing 3–10 florets, mostly few qian xian on rachis in, lemma long 5–8 mm, awn long 4–8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland or sandy land.
Morphology: Culms on inflorescence lower rough or hairy; leaf blade longitudinal rolled forming needle-like, wide 0.3–0.6 mm; panicle constricted appearing spike-shaped, spikelets often band purplish red, lemma abaxial side rough, apex awn long 1–1.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Hack. ex Wiesb.) Hegi
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Russia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, alluvial fans or grassland.
Morphology: Culms on inflorescence lower smooth; leaf blade wide 0.3–0.6 mm; spikelets long 4–6 mm, glumes long 1–2 mm, margin narrow membranous; lemma long 2.5–3.5 mm, anthers long 1.5 mm above. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Hack.) Schinz & R.Keller
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, Northwest China and Sichuan; the Mediterranean region, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland or rock crevices.
Morphology: Culms on inflorescence lower smooth; leaf blade often pairs bent, wide 0.5–1.1 mm; spikelets long 6–10 mm, glumes margin narrow membranous; lemma long 4.8–6 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland, river floodplains or wet meadow.
Morphology: With short rhizome; leaf blade wide 1–2 mm; panicle sparse pine, spikelets pale green or deep purple, lemma abaxial side smooth or rough, apex awn long 1–3 mm, anthers long 2–2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.) C.E.Hubb.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China Northeast China coastal areas naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline soil ground.
Morphology: Plant spreading spreading-shaped; spike cylindrical and slightly compressed, long 4–10 cm, mostly appearing xiang dao-shaped curved, spikelets containing 1 florets, single du qianborne on rachis in. Flowering period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 554; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain stony ground.
Morphology: Panicle hard erect, long about 5 cm, compact, branched coarse short, spikelets containing 3–5 florets, linear-lanceolate, up to 10 mm, first glumes with 3–5 veins. Flowering and fruiting period summer.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores sandy saline soil or grooves sides alluvial fans.
Morphology: Panicle long about 15 cm, spikelets large, long 8–11 mm, oblong-lanceolate, containing 6–9 flower, band purple brown, lemma long 3–3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Trin.) Scribn. & Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe, Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains grassland.
Morphology: Plant with lower extending rhizome; panicle long 2–5 cm, spikelets containing 3–4 florets, long 6–8 mm. Flowering period June.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) V.I.Krecz. ex Ovcz. & Czukav.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas meadow wetland.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, without rhizome; panicle long 2–5 cm, spikelets containing 4–7 florets, long 8–10 mm. Flowering period June–July.

Author: (Stapf ex Hook.f.) R.R.Stewart
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in basins gentle sloping ground.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, long 12–20 cm, wide 2–3 cm, spikelets long 6–9 mm, glumes long, apex pointed, lemma long 3.5–4.5 mm, anthers long 2.5–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: R.R.Stewart
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Pakistan and Kashmir also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland, salt-lake shores or marsh meadow.
Morphology: Panicle narrow, long 5–10 cm, wide about 1.5 cm, spikelets long 5–6 mm, glumes long, apex pointed, lemma long 3.2–3.5 mm, anthers long 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Griseb.) Scribn. & Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe salt becoming wetland, sandy soil or watersides grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide reaching 4–5 mm; panicle sparse spreading, long 20–30 cm, spikelets small, containing 2–4 florets, long 2.5–3.5 mm, glumes slightly short, lemma long 1.5–3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Trin.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant compact clustered; basal leaves very numerous, pairs bent, wide 0.3–0.7 mm; panicle long 4–15 cm, spreading, lemma smooth, anthers long 0.8–1.7 mm. Flowering period May–June.

Author: L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas riversides or marsh meadow.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, wide 2–3 mm; panicle ovate orbicular, long and wide all is 3–4 cm, spikelets long 3–4 mm, containing 2–3 florets. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Roshev.) V.I.Krecz. ex Ovcz. & Czukav.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Tibet and Xinjiang; Central Asia and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides sandy floodplain flats.
Morphology: Culms base is dense old sheath surrounding; panicle constricted, branched lower part densely covered spikelets, spikelets long about 4–5 mm, containing 3–6 florets, lemma long 2.8–3 mm, anthers long 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July.

Author: V.I.Krecz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks saline soil wetland.
Morphology: Panicle wide reaching 10 cm, spikelets band purple, long 4–5 mm, containing 3–4 florets, lemma long 2.8–3 mm, palea two keel densely covered small spiny-shaped rough, anthers long 1.5–2 mm. Flowering period June–July.

Author: (H.Hartmann) Dickore
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Nepal and Kashmir regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming rivers and lakes floodplain flat.
Morphology: Panicle relatively constricted, wide 1–2 cm, spikelets long 5–6 mm, second glumes long 2.5 mm, lemma long 3.2–3.5 mm, palea two keel smooth glabrous or upper part with few small spiny. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (Hack.) V.I.Krecz. ex Ovcz. & Czukav.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Mongolia, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river lake shores margins sandy land, alluvial fans or mountain ground saline soil ground.
Morphology: Panicle long 4–6 cm, broad forked kai, second glumes long 1.5–2 mm, lemma long 2.3–3.2 mm, palea two keel smooth glabrous or upper part with few small spiny. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes saline soil steppe.
Morphology: Panicle long and wide about 1–2 cm, with 10 remaining in number spikelets, lemma long 2.8–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus mountain gullies wetland.
Morphology: Panicle long 3–4 cm, wide about 0.5 cm, with numerous spikelets, lemma long 2 mm. Flowering period July–August.

Author: D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes gullies and valleys or marsh meadow.
Morphology: Panicle large type spreading, long 12–20 cm, wide 5–7 cm, about zhan plant of half, lemma long 3.5–4 mm, anthers long 0.3–0.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains watersides.
Morphology: Panicle relatively dense, long 8–12 cm, wide 2–3 cm, spikelets containing 6–9 florets, long 6–8 mm, lemma long 2–2.2 mm, anthers long about 0.6 mm. Flowering period May–July.

Author: Bor
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet; Pakistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas sandy lake shores or saline meadow.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, 1/3 below is dense dense withered sheath surrounding; panicle dense forming spike shape, erect, long about 2 cm, wide 5 mm, spikelets long about 3.5 mm, anthers long 0.5–0.8 mm. Flowering period summer.

Author: Bor
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; South Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas valleys sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Panicle very narrow, long 3–4 cm, wide about 5 mm, spikelets containing 4–7 florets, lemma long 3.2–3.5 mm, apex pointed, purple, inner glume narrow small, two keel smooth, anthers long 0.6–0.8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August.

Author: (Hack.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores salt flat meadow or high mountain gullies valley marshland.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–8 cm, wide 1–2 cm, spikelets containing 4 florets, long 5–5.5 mm, lemma long 2.5–2.8 mm, apex roundeded-obtuse, with margin hairs-shaped slender teeth lobed, palea two keel rough. Flowering and fruiting period July.

Author: L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Sichuan and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain gullies wetland or salinized and alkalized alpine areas meadow.
Morphology: Spikelets containing 5–7 florets, long 4–6 mm, lemma long 2–2.2 mm, smooth glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet and Xinjiang; South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores marshland, ditch banks wetland or river floodplains meadow.
Morphology: Culms sparse tufted, base inclined wo; leaf blade long 3–4 cm; spikelets long 3–3.5 mm, containing 2–3 florets, first glumes long 1 mm, second glumes long 1.5 mm, lemma long 1.6–2 mm, apex obtuse pointed. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Krecz.) Serg.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Ili; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys sandy land or wet grassland.
Morphology: Culms erect, dense tufted; leaf blade long 1–2 cm; first glumes long 0.5 mm, second glumes long 1 mm, outer cheng long 1.5 mm, apex orbicular, with densely covered margin hairs slender teeth. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Host) Parl.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe and Turkey also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming marshland.
Morphology: Ligule long 3–4 mm, leaf blade wide about 2 mm; panicle narrow, long 10–20 cm, spikelets containing 5–9 florets, long 6–8 mm, glumes longer, lemma long 3.5–4 mm, anthers long 2 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: (Macoun ex Vasey) Hitchc.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang; Korea, Japan, Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast or gravel meadow.
Morphology: Ligule long about 1 mm; panicle long 5–10 cm, constricted, anthers long 1 mm. Flowering period June–July.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: D.F.Cui
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Ruoqiang.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes valley floors watersides.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–10 cm, wide about 4 cm, spikelets often containing 3 florets, first glumes long 2–2.5 mm, lemma long 3.5–4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Schischk.) V.I.Krecz. ex Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain saline soil Gobi gravel desert or riversides flat flat gravelly ground.
Morphology: Culms base clustered red brown without leaves leaf sheath; leaf blade wide 2–4 mm, involute; panicle spreading, glumes longer, lemma long about 3.2 mm. Flowering period June–July.

Author: (Hornem.) Fourr.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Europe and Turkey also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river lake shores margins, halophytic meadow or marshland.
Morphology: Leaf blade involute, wide 0.5–1.5 mm. Panicle constricted, spikelets containing 4–7 florets, glumes lanceolate, lemma long 3–3.5 mm. Flowering period June–August.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 549; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: (K.Koch) Grossh.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Iran, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi desert watersides, arid steppe or salt-lake shores margins.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 3–6 cm, wide 2–3 mm; panicle constricted, base attached spikelets, spikelets containing 6–9 florets, long 6–8 mm, first glumes long about 1.5 mm, lemma long 2.5–3 mm. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (Schur) Janch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 8–15 cm, wide 2–3 mm; panicle spreading, wide 4–8 cm, spikelets containing 5–7 florets, long 7–8 mm, first glumes long 1.5–2 mm, lemma long 2.8–3 mm. Flowering period May–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 550; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Qinghai; Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand rocky saline soil wetland.
Morphology: Panicle narrow, attached few spikelets, spikelets long 5–6 mm, first glumes long about 1.5 mm, lemma long 3.2–3.8 mm, anthers long 2–2.4 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores sandy land or salt becoming low wet meadow.
Morphology: Leaf sheath smooth glabrous, leaf blade wide 2–3 mm; panicle spreading, spikelets long 5–6 mm, first glumes long 1.2 mm, lemma long 3–3.5 mm, anthers long about 1.8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) Norl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming low wetland.
Morphology: Leaf sheath band purple and rough, leaf blade wide 4–5 mm; panicle spreading, spikelets long 5–6 mm, first glumes long 1–1.5 mm, lemma long 2.3–2.8 mm, anthers long 1.6–2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: N.R.Ivanov ex Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, wide about 2 mm; panicle elliptic, long 5–8 cm, wide about 4 cm, branched long 3–4 cm, first glumes long 1.5–2 mm, lemma long about 3 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground saline meadow, gravel flat or salt-lake shores grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1–2 mm; panicle narrow, long 10–20 cm, branched long 1–2 cm, spikelets long about 6 mm, first glumes long 1.5–1.8 mm, lemma long 2.8–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sandy land or saline meadow.
Morphology: Leaf blade involute, wide about 1 mm; panicle slightly sparse pine, long 5–15 cm, wide 2–4 cm, spikelets containing 3–4 florets, long about 4.5 mm, first glumes long 1–1.2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert and steppe.
Morphology: Leaf blade involute, wide about 0.7 mm; panicle long 8–18 cm, very narrow, branched short and appressed borne, spikelets long about 5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) Pavlov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid steppe, zhong saline-alkaline marshland or sand gravel floodplain flats.
Morphology: Culms base is yellow-brown leaf sheath enclosing hidden; leaf blade involute forming needle-like; panicle narrow, spikelets long 4–7 mm, lemma long about 3.5 mm, anthers long about 1.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet; Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores, river valleys sandy land or moist salt flat grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade involute, wide about 1 mm; panicle sparse spreading, spikelets long 6–7 mm, first glumes long about 1.5 mm, lemma long about 3 mm, anthers long 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas riversides or meadow.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, each nodes with 1–2 branched, spikelets very purple, lemma long.5–2.2 mm, anthers long 0.9–1.3 mm. Flowering period June–July.

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning and Hebei; Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline-alkaline land or seashore saline land.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, long 4–9 cm, wide 1.5–3 mm; panicle sparse pine, wide 5–8 cm, branched long 6–8 cm, spikelets containing 5–7 florets, long 5–6 mm, first glumes long about 1 mm, lemma long 1.6–2 mm, anthers long 1.2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Grossh.) Grossh.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in arid steppe or salt becoming sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant base enlarged thick becoming coarse; leaf blade long 4–6 cm, wide 1–2 mm; panicle short narrow, lemma long 2–2.5 mm. Flowering period June–July.

Author: Melderis
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Afghanistan and Iran also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus saline soil wetland.
Morphology: Plant base with sheath inner and sheath outer two zhong divided nie and not enlarged thick, lemma long 1.5–2 mm. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (Litv. ex V.I.Krecz.) Pavlov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplain flats low-lying and moist places or salt becoming arid meadow.
Morphology: Culms slender slender, cu about 0.5 mm, leaf sheath persistent surrounding attached base; panicle with few spikelets, spikelets long 3–4 mm, containing 3–5 florets, lemma long about 2 mm, anthers long about 1.2 mm. Flowering period May–August.

Author: (Schur) Holmb.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks saline soil wetland.
Morphology: Panicle golden-shaped pyramidal shape, long 15–20 cm, spikelets containing 3–5 florets, long 4–4.5 mm, lemma long 2.2–2.5 mm, anthers long 1.2–1.5 mm. Flowering period June–July.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 549; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia, Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides wetland, sandy sea mudflat or saline soil meadow.
Morphology: Panicle long 10 cmzuo you, spikelets long 3.5–4 mm, lemma long about 2 mm, anthers long 1–1.2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Grossh.) Grossh.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in halophytic meadow and lake shores.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, wide 2–4 mm; panicle spreading, long 10–20 cm, branched long 6–10 cm, first glumes long 1.5–2 mm, second glumes long 2–2.5 mm, lemma long 2–2.5 mm. Flowering period June–August.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 550; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: (Grossh.) Grossh.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in halophytic meadow and lake shores.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, wide 2–4 mm; panicle spreading, long 10–20 cm, branched long 6–10 cm, first glumes long 1.5–2 mm, second glumes long 2–2.5 mm, lemma long 2–2.5 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: Honda
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Qinghai; Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hills, slopes or tidal wetland.
Morphology: Panicle long about 10 cm, branched long about 5 cm, first glumes long 1–1.2 mm, second glumes long about 1.5 mm, outer cheng long 2–2.2 mm, apex roundeded-obtuse. Flowering period June–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) V.I.Krecz. ex Drobov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid river floodplains gravel sandy land or saline-alkaline grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade short linear, wide 1–1.3 mm; panicle long 10–20 cm, branched rough, long 2–4 cm, first glumes long about 1.2 mm, lemma long 2–2.3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Holmb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Russia Siberia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or mountain ground meadow.
Morphology: Panicle long 15–30 cm, sparse pine spreading, spikelets containing 4–6 florets, first glumes long about 1.2 mm, second glumes long about 2 mm, lemma long about 3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning and Inner Mongolia; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sand rocky ground or beach saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Panicle long 10–30 cm, spikelets containing 3–4 florets, first glumes long 2–2.5 mm, second glumes long 3 mm, lemma long 3–3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (R.Br.) E.L.Rand & Redfield
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy beach or saline soil grassland.
Morphology: Panicle nearly racemose, mostly few oblique borne one lateral, long 3–5 cm, second glumes long 2.5–3 mm, lemma long 3.5–4 mm. Flowering period July–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) V.I.Krecz. ex Drobov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas desert steppe, saline meadow or gravel slopes.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–10 cm, branched each nodes 2–5, second glumes long 2–2.5 mm, lemma long 2.5–3 mm. Flowering period July–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) V.I.Krecz. ex Drobov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas desert steppe, saline meadow or gravel slopes.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–10 cm, branched each nodes 2–5, second glumes long 2–2.5 mm, lemma long 2.5–3 mm. Flowering period July–August.

Author: (Tzvelev) Ikonn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe or wet grassland.
Morphology: Panicle pine spreading, long 20 cm, wide 10 cm, spikelets long 3–4 mm, first glumes long 0.7–1 mm, second glumes long 1.5–1.8 mm, lemma long 1.8–2 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and East China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains, lakeshores marshland or low-lying and moist saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, long 15–20 cm, branched lower part bare exposed, spikelets containing 5–8 florets, long 4–5 mm, first glumes long 0.7–1 mm, lemma long 1.6–1.8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Keng) Keng f. & S.L.Chen
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Inner Mongolia, Hebei, Shanxi, Gansu and Jiangsu.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or moist grassland.
Morphology: Panicle broad spreading, long 5–8 cm, wide reaching 5 cm, spikelets containing 2–3 flower, long about 2.5 mm, first glumes long 0.6–1 mm, lemma long about 1.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Holmb.
Distribution: Distributed in Jilin; Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks tidal wetland.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–12 cm, spikelets long 6–7 mm, containing 6–9 florets, first glumes long 1–1.5 mm, second glumes long about 2 mm, lemma long 2 mm, palea long on its lemma and conspicuous outer exposed. Flowering period June.

Author: (V.I.Krecz.) V.I.Krecz. ex Drobov
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in streams and ditches shores or salt becoming sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle long 10 cm, spikelets long 5–7 mm, containing 5–8 florets, first glumes long 1.5–1.8 mm, lemma long 2–2.7 mm, palea etc. Long or slightly short on its lemma. Flowering period April–May or July–September.

Author: (Schult.) Hitchc.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland or riversides sandy beach wetland.
Morphology: Panicle wide large sparse pine, branched extending long spreading, spikelets long about 5 mm, containing 3–5 florets, first glumes about 1.5 mm, second glumes long about 2 mm, lemma long 2.5–3 mm, lower part densely covered pubescent. Flowering period June–August.

Author: (Jacq.) Parl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized meadow or sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, main axis and branched rough, mostly reflexed, pendulous, spikelets long 4–6 mm, lemma long 2–2.2 mm, base pubescent shorter few. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 17, 548; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Jacq.) Parl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized meadow or sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, main axis and branched rough, mostly reflexed, pendulous, spikelets long 4–6 mm, lemma long 2–2.2 mm, base pubescent shorter few. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Jacq.) Parl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized meadow or sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, main axis and branched rough, mostly reflexed, pendulous, spikelets long 4–6 mm, lemma long 2–2.2 mm, base pubescent shorter few. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Lange) Holmb.
Distribution: China not distributed in; Northern Hemisphere high latitudes regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in arid river floodplains or saline soil sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle long 4–7 cm, narrow, attached 1–3 spikelets, spikelets containing 3–6 florets, long 3–4.5 mm, first glumes long 1–1.5 mm, lemma long 2.2–2.5 mm. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Northwest China; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: With long stolon; leaf blade soft; panicle spreading, long 10–30 cm, spikelets containing 2 florets, glumes short small, without veins, lemma apex truncate, with raised 3 veins, smooth glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period April–August.

Author: Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Sichuan and Xinjiang; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert valleys saline-alkaline flats or slopes grassland.
Morphology: Ligule long 2.5–3 mm; leaf blade wide 1.5–3 mm; panicle wide reaching 10 cm, branched each nodes 4–5, spikelets numerous, long 6–8 mm, containing 3–5 florets, lemma long 5–7 mm. Flowering period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain front of plains, slopes gravel sandy land or desert steppe.
Morphology: Plant base with scales stem-shaped zhu ya; leaf blade wide 0.5–2 mm; panicle long 2–8 cm, constricted, spikelets small, glumes with 3 veins, lemma and margins veins lower part with short-pubescent, base disc with woolly hairs, spikelets often tai borne. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Roshev.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia to South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert mountain ground gullies and valleys or oases.
Morphology: Plant with scales stem; panicle long 2–10 cm, spikelets containing 2–4 florets, long 4–7 mm, green or apex purple, lemma long 2.5–3.5 mm, fully entire glabrous, anthers long 1.2–2 mm. Flowering period April–May.

Author: (Steud.) Fernald
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China (tropical regions except); northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marshland, waterside wetlands or shallow water places.
Morphology: Panicle long 10–30 cm, spikelets sessile, imbricate on spike stalk one lateral, flattened, orbicular, two lateral compressed, gray-green, often containing 1 florets, long about 3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: M.Bieb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, slopes or watersides tidal wetland.
Morphology: Panicle oblong or ovate-shaped oblong, long 1.5–4 cm, spikelets long 3.5–4 mm, glumes apex obtuse, two lateral densely covered pubescent, lemma several etc. Long, awn long 5–8 mm, geniculate curved. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Xinjiang, Beijing and elsewhere cultivated; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, valleys or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle round column-shaped, long 3–8 cm, spikelets long about 5 mm, glumes lateral veins with short hairs, awn long 6–8 mm, geniculate curved, nearly glume body of base zhong protruding, conspicuous outer exposed. Flowering and fruiting period April–August.

Author: Poir.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part, North China and Northwest China; Eurasia cold temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland.
Morphology: Panicle long round-shaped cylindrical, long 2.5–7 cm, spikelets long 4–5 mm, glumes apex pointed, two lateral glabrous or sparsely covered short hairs, awn long 1–5 mm, erect, nearly glume body of middle part exserted, yin hidden or slightly outer exposed. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Europe and the Mediterranean region, Jiangsu, mountain ground or Yunnan and elsewhere escaped from cultivation or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in shallow water, wetland or open wet meadow.
Morphology: Panicle long round column-shaped, long 1.5–7 cm, glumes covered with pubescent, awn long 3–5 mm, geniculate curved, long on lemma 2–4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, river floodplains sandy land or rocky gravel ground.
Morphology: Leaf blade longitudinal rolled; panicle narrow or sparse pine, spikelets gray-green or purple, awn rough, glumes with 1 vein, nearly etc. Long, lemma long 7–10 mm, conspicuous with 3 awn, main awn long 1–2 cm, two lateral awn slightly short. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Roshev.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sand dunes.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, spikelets long 2.5–3 cm, glumes unequal long, first glumes long 2.5–3 cm, lemma long 8–9 mm, apex 2-lobed glabrous, conspicuous with 3 awn, awn entire body covered with pinnate-shaped pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Trin.) De Winter
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Yunnan; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spikelets long 1.5–1.7 cm, glumes nearly etc. Long, first glumes with 3–5 veins, lemma long 5–7 mm, apex flat truncate and with one ring short hairs, awn entire body covered with pinnate-shaped pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaf blade flattened, wide 3–5 cm; panicle very large type, long 30–60 cm, spikelets long 10–12 mm, containing 2–4 florets, lemma abaxial surface middle part below densely covered villous, hairs long 5–7 mm. Flowering and fruiting period September–December.
Uses: Forage, ornamental, soil and water conservation, ecological use

Author: (L.) Moench
Distribution: Native to Europe, Beijing, Shanghai and Taiwan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle long 5–40 cm, often narrow, spikelets lateral flattened, with 2–5 florets, long 4–9 mm, glumes membranous, lemma glabrous, with 3 veins, long 2.5–4.5 mm, anthers purple. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Cav.) Trin. ex Steud.
Distribution: Throughout throughout China; worldwide distribution.
Habitat: Occurs in salt-lake shores marsh, strongly salinized marshland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Rhizome very well developed; leaf blade lanceolate-linear, leathery and hard; panicle long 10–30 cm, spikelets long about 12 mm, lemma glabrous, base disc extending long, densely covered with filiform pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Uses: Fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use, roots medicinal
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Retz.) Trin. ex Steud.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in river rivers and lakes coast wetland.
Morphology: Panicle large type, long 30–50 cm, lemma base disc slender and curved, bian borne short on its glume body of sparse pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.
Uses: Fiber, soil and water conservation, ecological use

Author: (Thunb.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, Central China and South China; Asia southeastern part and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gentle slopes or plains moist grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade lanceolate, wide 3–8 mm, base slightly cordate; panicle spreading, branched with yellow gland spots, spikelets elliptic round-shaped globose, long 2–2.5 mm, containing 2 florets; glumes fruit subglobose. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or salinized and alkalized sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle short spike-shaped, constricted appearing cylindrical, long 1–3.5 cm, spikelets usually containing 2–3 florets, lemma with 7–9 veins or to numerous veins, apex with 9 strips erect feathery-shaped awn, awn long 2–4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: (Desf.) Trin. ex Steud.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Asia and Africa warm regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, long 5–20 cm, wide 2–4 cm, spikelets narrow ju shape, long 5–10 mm, rachilla persistent, palea and lemma same when deciduous, caryopsis and glume body free. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Thunb.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, South China and Southwest China; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, slopes grassland or seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle large and spreading, branchlets and spikelets stalk middle part or middle part above with glands, spikelets oblong, each one florets lemma and palea not same when deciduous; caryopsis brown red. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or sandy land.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, long 6–15 cm, without glands, branched axil among often with villous, spikelets long 3–10 mm, first glumes without veins, long 1 mm below, palea than lemma chi luo or persistent. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: Keng
Distribution: Distributed in North China and East China.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland, grassland or saline-alkaline sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle spreading or constricted, long 20–40 cm, without glands, branched axil among ye glabrous, spikelets long 3–5 mm, containing 3–10 florets, first glumes with 1 vein, long about 1.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–November.

Author: (All.) Vignolo ex Janch.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland, sandy soil or grassland.
Morphology: Panicle oblong or pointed pyramidal shape, branched axil among with pubescent, branchlets and spikelets stalk on found in all glands, spikelets wide 2–3 mm, with 10–40 florets, first lemma long about 2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Host
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle spreading and sparse pine, branched axil among glabrous, branched and spikelets stalk on dou with glands, spikelets wide 1.5–2 mm, containing 3–16 florets, first lemma long about 2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Franch. & Sav.
Distribution: Distributed in Shandong, Anhui, Jiangsu, Zhejiang and Fujian; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Rhizome horizontally creeping; leaf blade linear-lanceolate, wide 1–4 mm; raceme constricted appearing spike-shaped, base often enclosing hidden on leaf sheath inner, spikelets wide about 2 mm, in main axis on arranged relatively compact, first glumes fully entire reduced. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Desv.
Distribution: Native to Australia, Jiangsu ceng introduced cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1–4 mm; raceme constricted appearing spike-shaped, long 1.5–5 cm, spikelets long 2–5 mm, inflorescence stalk short, apex expanded large, glumes base margin connate, lemma long 3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: Steud.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China and southern part coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains, slopes or seashore grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 2–4 mm; raceme appearing spike-shaped, base exserted leaf sheath outer, spikelets stalk curved, long up to 5 mm, spikelets long 2.5–3.5 mm, ovate, second glumes textured stiff and slightly lustrous. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade often involute, wide 1–2 mm; raceme appearing slender column shape, spikelets stalk robust straight, long about 1.5 mm, spikelets long 2–3 mm, wide about 1 mm, lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Loisel.) P.M.Peterson & Saarela
Distribution: Native to North America Atlantic Ocean coasts, eastern China and southern part coast band cultivated after naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in beach marshland.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 10–90 cm, wide 1–2 mm; spike long 5–20 cm, 10–20 racemose attached borne on main axis on, spikelets glabrous, at maturity entire deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 38; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: (C.E.Hubb.) P.M.Peterson & Saarela
Distribution: Native to United Kingdom, East China coast band cultivated after naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in beach marshland.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 10–20 cm, wide 7–10 mm; spike long 7–23 cm, 2–6 racemose attached borne on main axis on, spikelets covered with pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 38; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Kunth
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach salt ground on or fields sandy soil.
Morphology: With rhizome; leaf blade linear, involute appearing needle-like, long 3–11 cm, without warts hairs; panicle gray-green, constricted appearing spike-shaped, narrow forming linear, branched appressed borne; spikelets long 2.5–3 mm, first glumes long about 2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) P.M.Peterson
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and East China; Central Asia and the Mediterranean region also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil or saline-alkaline flats.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 2–6 cm; panicle constricted forming spike-shaped, round column-shaped or oblong, long 1–3 cm, its lower receptacle by one swollen large bract-shaped leaf sheath, spikelets long about 3 mm, stamens 3. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 29, 557; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) P.M.Peterson
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and East China; Central Asia and the Mediterranean region also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks, grooves sides or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Panicle short contracted forming head-shaped or ovate orbicular, long about 16 mm, wide 5–13 mm, lower surface tight receptacle two in number swollen large bract-shaped leaf sheath, spikelets long about 4 mm, stamens 2. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 29, 557; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Michx.) Torr.
Distribution: Native to North America, Beijing and Taiwan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Spike 10–50, long 8–18 mm, often pendulous and oblique borne on main axis of one lateral, mature after entire deciduous, spikelets not forming pectinate dentate arranged, fertile flower lemma only minutely mucronate. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Nutt.) Columbus
Distribution: Native to United States, China North China and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Rhizome long and horizontally creeping; leaf blade linear, wide 1–2 mm, covered with white pubescent; male inflorescence with 2–3 racemose arranged spike, long 5–15 mm, female inflorescence often appearing head-shaped, long 6–9 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (L.) Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; chu the Americas outer world nuan temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Spike 2–7 finger-like-shaped arranged on culms apex, long 1–4 cm, without terminal spikelets, spikelets sessile, two lateral compressed, forming two rows tight appressed ground imbricate on narrow and flattened rachis one lateral, awn long 1–2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: (L.) Gaertn.
Distribution: Native to Africa, southern China and Southwest China part introduced and cultivated, worldwide widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes dry land.
Morphology: Spike 5–8 appearing finger-like-shaped attached culms apex, at maturity often inner curved, long 5–10 cm, wide 8–10 mm; seeds subglobose. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (L.) Gaertn.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world hot band subtropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Spike 2–7 finger-like-shaped attached borne on culms apex, branched not curved, long 3–10 cm, wide 3–5 mm; seeds ovate. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv. ex Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wetland.
Morphology: Panicle long 15–25 cm, spikes numerous, appearing racemose arranged on extending long inflorescence main axis on, spikelets linear-oblong, lemma abaxial side slightly round, apex with 2 teeth lobed, teeth among with 1 short awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.) Nees
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, sandy soil or wetland.
Morphology: Leaf sheath and leaf blade all glabrous; panicle long 10–30 cm, inflorescence branched relatively coarse robust, long 5–10 cm, spikelets often band purple, long 2–4 mm, containing 3–7 florets. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: (Retz.) Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, South China and Southwest China; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaf sheath and leaf blade all with warts base villous; inflorescence branched very slender weak, long 2.5–6 cm, spikelets gray-green or band purple, long 1–2 mm, containing 2–4 florets. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Lag.) Keng
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan, Taiwan and Yunnan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or open wild grassland.
Morphology: Spike 4–5, long 10–25 cm, finger-like-shaped attached borne on culms apex, spikelets lanceolate, long 5–7 mm, lemma mostly few with xie abaxial adaxial compressed, its margin zhu veins on glabrous, awn long 8–16 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (F.Muell.) Clayton
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan, Southeast Asia and Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Spike single protruding terminal or 2–3 finger-like-shaped attached borne on culms apex, long about 7 cm, spikelets long 4–5 mm, awn slightly long on lemma. Flowering and fruiting period September.

Author: Kunth
Distribution: Native to Africa, southern China introduced and cultivated after naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in open grassland or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade apex acuminate; spike several to ten remaining in number clusters borne on culms apex, long about 10 cm, spikelets long 4–4.5 mm, first outer margins glume margin and keel two lateral hairy, not fertile lemma 2–3, narrow narrow, apex pointed and emarginate. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: Sw.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part, North China, Northwest China, East China and Southwest China; South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks sandy land, dry slopes or arid saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Spike 5–10 remaining in number, long 1.5–5 cm, finger-like-shaped attached borne on culms apex, often erect and long forming hairs shua-shaped, spikelets chu glumes outer with 2 awn, not fertile lemma apex wide broad and truncate. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Honda) Keng
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Spike 4–11, long 3–8 cm, spikelets chu glumes outer with 3 awn, long 2.5–3 mm, not fertile lemma long large on wide, not fertile florets sparse separate and its among rachilla xian exposed. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (G.Forst.) R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in xi sand archipelagos and Taiwan; Asia southeastern part and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore coral reefs.
Morphology: Leaf blade textured stiff, linear, often involute; spike erect, round column-shaped, long 5–10 cm, coarse about 1.5 mm, spikelets containing 2 florets, long about 12 mm, qianborne on rachis concave xue in, and rachis internodes one qi deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Pers.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, South China and Southwest China; world warm regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, dry slopes or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Rhizome long and creeping; spike 3–5 finger-like-shaped attached, long 2–6 cm, spikelets imbricate on rachis one lateral, without awn, long 2–2.5 mm, only containing 1 florets, lemma textured soft, relatively glumes is long. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 557; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: (Roshev.) Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, desert or half desert sandy soil.
Morphology: Culms base with dense withered leaf sheath; leaf blade wide 1.5–2.5 mm; panicle spreading, long 2–8 cm, spikelets long 4–8 mm, containing 3–6 florets, lemma without awn or with long 0.5 mm small pointed tip. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Trin.) Keng
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, hills sloping ground or sandy soil.
Morphology: Culms slender slender, dense tufted, often spreading spreading, dry after often forming wan yan-shaped curved, plant green; leaf blade wide 1–2 mm; panicle narrow, long 4–7 cm, spikelets few, long 5–7 mm, containing 2–3 florets. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Keng ex Keng f. & L.Liou
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China and elsewhere.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, grooves shore or sandy soil.
Morphology: Culms relatively coarse robust, erect, with mostly nodes; leaves relatively mostly, leaf blade textured stiff, oblique on, wide 2–4 mm; panicle narrow, base often is leaf sheath enclosing, spikelets long 8–13 mm, containing 3–7 florets. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Vahl) Panz.
Distribution: Native to Africa, Southwest Asia and South Asia, Fujian, Shandong and Yunnan naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Racemose panicle terminal, long 6–20 cm, spikes numerous irregularly arranged along the main axis, short, long 0.5–4 cm, spikelets containing 2–3 florets, glumes longer than the florets. Flowering and fruiting period November–December.

Author: (Debeaux) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy beach, saline-alkaline flats or salinized meadow.
Morphology: Plant with long creeping branches; leaf blade wide 3–6 mm; panicle spike shape, long 2–5 cm, branched arranged compact and zhong die, spikelets long 4–6 mm, with 4–6 florets, glumes glabrous, lemma glabrous ye without awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Uses: Forage, medicinal, sand stabilization, ecological use
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks sandy land, sand dunes or plains Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1–2 mm, both surfaces densely covered with slightly spiny hairs; panicle long 2–5 cm, spikelets ovate, long 2–3 mm, containing 2–6 florets, anthers long about 0.6–0.8 mm. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: K.Koch
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide about 1.5 mm, glabrous; panicle spike-shaped, long 2–7 cm, branched each other sparse separate and not zhong die, spikelets long 2–4 mm, containing 4–8 florets, glumes and glume segments margin densely covered with long slender hairs. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (H.L.Yang) S.L.Chen & X.L.Yang
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Leaf sheath and leaf blade both surfaces all dense hairy; panicle spike-shaped, long 3–4 cm, branched not zhong die, spikelets long 3–4 mm, containing 3–4 florets, glumes and glume segments entire body densely covered pubescent. Flowering period July.

Author: (Retz.) Pers.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; world tropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in field margins grassland.
Morphology: With long stolon; raceme 2–3, rachis with narrow wings, spikelets three in number clustered, covered with pubescent, long 1.2–1.4 mm, second lemma cartilaginous textured, without awn. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: (Kunth) Henrard
Distribution: Distributed in Anhui, Zhejiang, Jiangxi, Fujian and Taiwan; Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid sand dunes or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Entire plant (culms, nodes, leaf sheath and leaf blade both surfaces) densely covered pubescent; raceme 2–4, huborne on long about 2 cm main axis on, appearing umbel locule-shaped, spikelets long 2–2.2 mm, second glumes with 3–5 veins. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Retz.) Koeler
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, barren slopes or sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 3–10 mm; raceme 4–10, appearing finger-like-shaped arranged on culms apex, paired borne spikelets same type, lanceolate, long 3–3.5 mm, first lemma veins among and margin with pubescent and warts base long bristles. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Lam.) Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Hainan and Yunnan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks or beach sandy land.
Morphology: Raceme 5–7, long 4–10 cm, paired borne spikelets different type, short-stalked spikelets nearly glabrous, long about 3 mm, first lemma with 5–7 veins, long-stalked spikelets with filiform pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Hook.f.) Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Raceme coarse stiff ting straight, 2 or 3, long up to 20 cm, paired borne spikelets different -like, spikelets long about 4 mm, first lemma with 7–9 strips conspicuous raised coarse veins. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Link
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 3–7 mm; panicle narrow erect, inflorescence axis on without warts base long bristles, spikelets ovate orbicular, long 2–2.5 mm, with small stiff hairs, without awn, first glumes long about is spikelets 1/2. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Throughout throughout China; worldwide warm-temperate zone and subtropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 5–20 mm; panicle nearly pointed pyramidal shape, rachis rough or with warts base long spiny hairs, spikelets ovate, long 3–4 mm, second glumes etc. Long on spikelets, second lemma herbaceous, awn long 0.5–1.5 cm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Kunth) Hitchc.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, throughout China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 4–12 mm; panicle erect, long 11–19 cm, branched on not again branched; spikelets ovate-elliptic, long 3–4 mm, apex minutely mucronate and without awn, veins on without warts base hairs, but sparsely covered stiff spiny hairs. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: Roshev.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China, East China and Southwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open waterside wetlands or marshland.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 1–2 cm; panicle slightly pendulous, long 10–25 cm, spikelets ovate-elliptic, often band purple, long 3–4 mm, awn long 1.5–5 cm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: Link
Distribution: Native to Africa, throughout China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 10–24 mm; panicle erect, branched slightly appearing arcuate-shaped curved, spikelets broad ovate, long 3–5 mm, green white, glabrous, without awn, second glumes slightly short on spikelets, valley grains not easily deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Walter) Kuntze
