Distribution: Native to the Americas and Africa, southern China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, sandy beach or woodland.
Morphology: Stolon well developed; leaf blade apex obtuse round; spike-shaped panicle main axis round column-shaped, spikelets qianborne on flattened main axis one lateral concave xue inner, solitary, long 4–5 mm; fruit smooth. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: (L.) P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland, fields or sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle constricted appearing cylindrical, spikelets 2–5 clusters borne on main axis on, long 2–2.5 mm, base long bristles green to purple brown, first glumes long about is spikelets 1/3, apex obtuse, second glumes several and spikelets etc. Long. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: (Poir.) Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, saline-alkaline sandy land or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Inflorescence main axis on each branchlets only with 1 mature spikelets, spikelets long 3–4 mm, base bristles golden yellow, first glumes apex pointed, second glumes long is spikelets 1/2–2/3, first florets often with stamens, first palea etc. Wide on second florets. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Hochst. ex Chiov.) Morrone
Distribution: Native to Africa, Yunnan and Taiwan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground borne long, with long stolon; leaf blade long 4–5 cm; inflorescence consisting of 2–4 spikelets gou forming, enclosing hidden in upper part leaf sheath in, only stigma, anthers exserted sheath outer, bristles short on spikelets, spikelets up to 15 mm, stigma feathery-shaped. Flowering period March–April.

Author: (L.) Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, South China and Southwest China and elsewhere; Asia southeastern part and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, wasteland or sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted; leaf blade long up to 80 cm; panicle erect, constricted appearing spike-shaped cylindrical, spikelets peduncle long 1–3 mm, bristles rough, often band purple, long 1.5–3 cm, spikelets linear-lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Griseb.) Morrone
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, dry grassland or sandy soil.
Morphology: With horizontally creeping rhizome; leaf blade long 10–25 cm; panicle compact, wide about 10 mm, spikelets peduncle inconspicuous, long 0.5–1 mm, bristles soft, long 8–15 mm, gray-green or purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, China Hainan, Yunnan and Taiwan and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in hot band seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Racemose panicle erect, rachis internodes long 2–4 mm, spiny bracts appearing subglobose cup-shaped, only consisting of 1 whorls inner layers bristles connate one half above and forming, outer layers free bristles 10–20, round column-shaped, large mostly long not exceeding inner layers flattened becoming bristles one half. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.

Author: (Hack.) Fern.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, China Northeast China and North China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, riversides or seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Spiny bracts slightly long subglobose, long 8–10 mm, consisting of several whorls inner layers bristles connate one half above and forming, bristles 45–75, outer layers bristles several, than inner layers bristles short and slender, inner layers bristles base wide 0.5–0.9 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to United States, Beijing and Hebei and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores or riversides sandy land.
Morphology: Spiny bracts ovate orbicular, long 9–16 mm, dense hairy, bristles 15–43, outer layers bristles often persistent in, flattened becoming or cylindrical, inner layers bristles base wide 1.2–3 mm, one half above connate. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Burm.f.) Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy beach.
Morphology: Culms textured hard, prostrate and broad spreading, up to several m; leaf blade linear, margin bent rolled appearing needle-like; flower single -like, dioecious, spikelets lanceolate, male inflorescence umbel-shaped, with bract, female inflorescence star-shaped awn-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Thwaites) S.M.Phillips & S.L.Chen
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, South China and Southwest China; East Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides saline-alkaline wetland.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground man extending, much-branched; leaf blade short small, long 1–5 cm, wide 2–4 mm; panicle base enclosing hidden on leaf sheath inner, long 2–5 cm, branched mostly erect, only with 1 spikelets, first florets with stamens 2. Flowering and fruiting period autumn.

Author: (G.Forst.) R.Br. ex Sm.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy beach.
Morphology: Spike single one terminal, its lower receptacle by with sheath spathe, spikelets lanceolate, sessile, single borne on flattened rachis one lateral, mature after rachis used as bell-shaped biao developing strips-shaped rolled curved and forming one hard tubercles-shaped gou zao. Flowering and fruiting period April–December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Sm.) Veldkamp
Distribution: Distributed in Guizhou, Fujian and Yunnan; the Mediterranean region, Africa and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland or dry fields.
Morphology: Leaf blade linear-lanceolate, densely covered with slender hairs; panicle consisting of 4–5 raceme composed of, spikelets solitary, ovate, long about 2 mm, first glumes long 0.2–0.3 mm, without veins, second lemma apex obtuse. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Willd.) Zizka
Distribution: Native to Africa, Fujian, Guangdong and Taiwan and elsewhere naturalized or cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Panicle spreading, long 10–15 cm, spikelets stalk sparsely covered villous, spikelets two lateral compressed, long about 5 mm, often covered with pink silky hairs, second glumes and first lemma apex slightly lobed, lobes among borne 1 short awn. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.

Author: P.Beauv.
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan and Yunnan; Bhutan and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes roadsides meadow.
Morphology: Panicle consisting of 3–10 raceme composed of, spikelets abaxial adaxial compressed, ovate-elliptic, long 4–5 mm, glabrous, solitary or base paired borne, first glumes persistent in, second lemma abaxial side is toward axis -like. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: Michx.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, China North China, East China and South China and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Without rhizome; culms often curved bent; panicle spreading, long about 30 cm, spikelets sparsely covered, long about 2.3 mm, smooth, first glumes long is spikelets 1/4–1/5, scales covered with papery, mostly veins. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; world warm regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, stream banks or watersides tidal wetland.
Morphology: Rhizome coarse robust well developed; culms erect, jian ting; panicle spreading, long 5–20 cm, spikelets oblong, long about 3 mm, glabrous, apex pointed, first glumes long about is spikelets 1/4, second florets smooth. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, southeastern China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, wasteland or ditches and channels.
Morphology: Stolon long and horizontally creeping; raceme 2 opposite, long 2–6 cm, rachis stiff straight, spikelets obovate-oblong, long about 3 mm, sparsely covered slightly pubescent, first glumes reduced, second lemma abaxial side is toward axis -like. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Sw.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, Hainan, Hong Kong, Taiwan and Yunnan and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Stolon herbaceous, very long; raceme large mostly 2, opposite, long 2–5 cm, spikelets ovate-lanceolate, long about 3.5 mm, apex pointed, second glumes without midrib, second lemma apex pointed, conspicuous short on spikelets. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Roberty
Distribution: Native to South Asia and Southeast Asia, China East China, South China and Southwest China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sparse pine clay ground.
Morphology: Panicle large type terminal, long 20–30 cm, consisting of numerous whorled slender and long raceme composed of, spikelets paired borne, sessile spikelets two -like, slightly two lateral compressed, with stalk spikelets abaxial side flattened. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (L.) H.Scholz
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia, South Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, Gobi gravel desert or fields sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade wide 0.5–1.5 cm; panicle erect, long 30–60 cm, spikelets paired borne, one sessile, one with stalk, base disc with etc. Long on spikelets filiform pubescent, first glumes of two keel rough, second lemma with long 4–8 mm conspicuous outer exposed of awn. Flowering and fruiting period autumn.

Author: (Steud.) Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rivers or seashore moist shore flats.
Morphology: Leaf sheath glabrous; ligule long about 3 mm, leaf blade wide 1.5–3 cm, glabrous; raceme 4–10, finger-like-shaped arranged on culms apex, spikelets paired borne, same shape, all without awn, sessile spikelets containing 2 florets. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Steud.) Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rivers or seashore moist shore flats.
Morphology: Leaf sheath glabrous; ligule long about 3 mm, leaf blade wide 1.5–3 cm, glabrous; raceme 4–10, finger-like-shaped arranged on culms apex, spikelets paired borne, same shape, all without awn, sessile spikelets containing 2 florets. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Steud.) Ohwi
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China coastal areas; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rivers or seashore moist shore flats.
Morphology: Leaf sheath glabrous; ligule long about 3 mm, leaf blade wide 1.5–3 cm, glabrous; raceme 4–10, finger-like-shaped arranged on culms apex, spikelets paired borne, same shape, all without awn, sessile spikelets containing 2 florets. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Steud.) Miq.
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Shandong and Zhejiang; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach sandy land or nearly sea riverbanks.
Morphology: Leaves sessile, leaf blade both surfaces densely covered with villous; raceme 2 clustered borne, and hu xiang tight appressed forming one cylindrical, overall covered with white villous, sessile spikelets long about 1 cm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Pers.
Distribution: Native to Central Asia, West Asia, South Asia and the Mediterranean region, southern China and western part naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys, riversides, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Rhizome well developed; leaf blade wide 0.5–2.5 cm; panicle wide 5–10 cm, sessile spikelets elliptic, apex slightly obtuse, with conspicuous 3 small teeth, first glumes conspicuous abaxial adaxial compressed, with stalk spikelets at maturity deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (Nees ex Steud.) Millsp. & Chase
Distribution: Native to Africa, northern China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Panicle branched relatively slender shou, at maturity easily broken; sessile spikelets long elliptic-lanceolate to long elliptic; caryopsis relatively small, at maturity fully entire is glumes enclosing. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Moench
Distribution: Native to Africa, northern China widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Panicle branched relatively coarse robust, at maturity not easily broken, sessile spikelets obovate or inverted ovate-elliptic, hairs relatively few, abaxial side usually glabrous; caryopsis at maturity is glumes enclosing, or only apex bare exposed. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Retz.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, sandy soil or water wetland.
Morphology: Culms in not containing zhe tang, without tian wei; panicle large type, each nodes attached 2–4 branched, sessile and with stalk spikelets narrowly lanceolate, abaxial side with villous, base disc with long about 1 mm short-pubescent, second lemma apex with short awn pointed. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; Asia, Oceania and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rivers or lakes margins water wetland.
Morphology: Culms in containing with zhe tang, wei slightly tian; culms diameter 4–10 mm, with 5–10 nodes, internodes in hollow; leaf blade linear, wide 2–8 mm; spikelets abaxial side without pubescent, base disc with long on its spikelets 3–4 times filiform hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (L.) Raeusch.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in sand soil wasteland, riverbanks grassland, desert or seashore.
Morphology: Rhizome long and horizontally creeping; leaves large parts basal, wide about 1 cm; panicle dense dense, long 20 cm, wide reaching 3 cm, spikelets long 4.5–6 mm, base disc with long 12–16 mm filiform pubescent, stigma purple black. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: (Forssk.) Stapf
Distribution: Distributed in Central China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland.
Morphology: Leaf sheath round column-shaped; ligule longer, on margin lacerate lobed-shaped; raceme 2–8 finger-like-shaped attached borne on culms apex, inflorescence stalk smooth glabrous, spikelets paired borne, sessile spikelets first glumes oblong, keel wingless, midrib conspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.

Author: (L.) Keng
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world subtropical zone and temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Borne slopes grassland, wasteland or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Raceme 4 to numerous attached borne on culms apex appearing finger-like-shaped, long 3–7 cm, rachis with white filiform hairs, sessile spikelets long 4–5 mm, base disc with ran hairs, awn long 10–15 mm. Flowering and fruiting period autumn.

Author: (Michx.) Nash
Distribution: Native to North America, Beijing and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Raceme numerous, rachis often on apex enlarged coarse and with dentate attached genus structure, spikelets paired borne on each nodes, with stalk spikelets long 7–10 mm, often short on sessile spikelets, not fertile, awn up to 6 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; India, Pakistan, Nepal also found.
Habitat: Occurs in high elevations slopes grassland.
Morphology: Raceme often 4–8 attached borne on main culms or branched apex, base cylindrical, sheath-shaped spathe narrow long, spikelets paired borne, sessile zhe neng fertile, with stalk zhe not fertile, second glumes without awn, sessile spikelets first glumes abaxial side 2 keel among with deep longitudinal grooves. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.

Author: (Hochst.) Stapf
Distribution: Distributed in Yunnan; Southeast Asia, South Asia, Oceania and southern Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hills slopes or grassland.
Morphology: Wei panicle large type, sparse, spathe narrow linear, wide 1–3 mm, each paired borne raceme with 2 awn, with slender and long inflorescence stalk, not reflexed, upper part raceme base cylindrical, spikelets covered with white hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–December.

Author: Fisch. & C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, low mountain rocky slopes or river floodplains.
Morphology: Plant with red ye sap; stem two forked-shaped branched; basal leaves pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed; flower buds fusiform, with milky projecting-shaped prickles, petals orange yellow, base band conspicuous red spots; capsule linear-shaped cylindrical, sparsely covered with nearly round conical-shaped prickles, fruiting pedicel long 0.5–1 cm. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem unbranched; basal leaves narrow oblanceolate, large head deeply pinnatifid; bract with coarse teeth, flower buds fusiform, smooth, petals orange yellow, without spots dots; capsule sparsely covered with milky projecting-shaped prickles, at maturity from apex toward base dehiscent, fruiting pedicel very long. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes gravel rock crevices, desert or river floodplains.
Morphology: Bract pinnate-shaped 3–5 deeply lobed; flower buds ovate orbicular, covered with scale-like prickles, petals golden yellow, base with deep colored spots; capsule covered with spiny-shaped scale, at maturity from base toward apex dehiscent, fruiting pedicel long. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Crantz
Distribution: Distributed in Europe and the Mediterranean region, Beijing ceng jing introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaves pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed; flowers solitary stem apex, sepals mostly few covered with pubescent, petals yellow, ovary overall covered with nearly round conical-shaped prickles; capsule long 15–30 cm, glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: (Pall.) B.Fedtsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slightly containing salt clayey sand loam meadow, shrubland or steppe.
Morphology: Tuber subglobose or oblong; leaf blade ultimate lobes not lobed; raceme with 5–25 flower, flower red purple, on petals long 3–4 cm, spur long 2.1–2.8 cm; capsule linear. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert low mountain slopes, fixed sandy land or saxaul woodland.
Morphology: Tuberous roots diameter 2–5 cm; cauline leaves 2 alternate, 2–3 times three protruding deeply lobed; raceme terminal, flower yellow; achene large, subglobose, diameter 2.5–4.5 cm, bang guang-shaped inflated, indehiscent, inner containing seeds 2. Flowering period April, fruiting period May.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in northern China regions; Mongolia, Korea peninsula and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland or sandy steppe.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped, stamens numerous, filaments upper part oblanceolate, than anthers wide; achene ovate, with 8 strips longitudinal ribs, persistent style long about 1 mm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Stephan ex Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia, Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open terrace terraces, sandy soil or dry grass slope.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; leaflet textured slightly stiff; inflorescence round conical-shaped, nearly two forked-shaped branched, pedicel slender and fan mostly, long 1.5–3 cm; achene long 4–5.2 mm, with 8 strips coarse longitudinal ribs, stigma long about 1.6 mm. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in ditch banks or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaflet long 2–4 cm, upper part three lobed, jian head-shaped; panicle narrow pyramidal shape, flowers small, yellow; achene long about 2 mm, with 8 strips longitudinal ribs. Flowering period July, fruiting period September.

Author: (L.) Rchb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil or dry grassland.
Morphology: Plant structure blue gray; leaves slender lobed, lobes obovate; flowers small, pale yellow, diameter 3–5 mm, carpels 6–20; follicle erect, linear-shaped long elliptic, long 8–10 mm, seeds 4–14 grains. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground grass slope or open woodland.
Morphology: Tuberous roots luo bo-shaped; plant jian ting and tight cou, often unbranched, covered with appressed appressed broken pubescent; leaves slender lobed, ultimate lobes linear; terminal raceme narrow long, flower yellow band green, or dark purple, on sepals kui-shaped chuan shape; follicle long 1.1–1.7 cm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: N.Busch
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas grassland or salt-lake shores sandy land.
Morphology: Tuberous roots inverted conical; plant glabrous; flower yellow, often band green, sometimes blue, on sepals chuan-shaped falcate or falcate; follicle long 1.1–1.4 cm. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Maxim.) Wei Wang & Z.D.Chen
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet, Sichuan, Qinghai and Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground grass slope, fields wasteland or riversides sandy land.
Morphology: Straight roots nearly cylindrical; stem often branched; leaves palmate-shaped entire lobed, one times lobes slender lobed; raceme, flowers bluish purple, sepals with long zhao, on sepals chuan shape, petals without spur, lip large; follicle long 0.8–1.2 cm. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground grass slope or hills sandy land.
Morphology: Leaf blade three entire lobed, lobes linear-lanceolate to linear; raceme with 3–15 flower, flower purple blue or blue, spur subulate, long 1.7–2.3 cm, reduced stamens blue, with yellow ran hairs, carpels 3; follicle straight. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or watersides sandy soil.
Life Form: Perennial herbaceous climbing plant structure, chamaephyte
Morphology: Leaf blade pinnate-shaped slender lobed, ultimate lobes linear, wide 2–3 mm; flower often single flowers axillary, bell-shaped pendulous, sepals 4, pale yellow, filaments slightly covered with pubescent; persistent style long 2–2.5 cm, densely covered with white pubescent. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains wasteland or watersides sandy soil.
Life Form: Deciduous woody climbing plant structure, chamaephyte
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, small leaf blade or lobes entire or base you 1–2 shallowly lobed; round conical-shaped cyme or single cyme, sepals yellow, both surfaces with pubescent; persistent style long up to 10 cm, covered with villous. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or watersides sandy soil.
Life Form: Deciduous woody climbing plant structure, chamaephyte
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet lobes linear-lanceolate, lanceolate or narrowly ovate; cyme axillary, often is 3 flower, sepals both surfaces glabrous; persistent style long 3.5–5 cm, covered with villous. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Maxim.) Korsh.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus grassland, sandy soil ground or barren slopes.
Life Form: Deciduous woody climbing plant structure, chamaephyte
Morphology: Once-pinnate leaves, with 5–7 leaflet, in lobes ovate-shaped oblong or lanceolate, margin with not regular incised-serrate; flowers solitary, sepals inner surface glabrous; persistent style up to 4 cm. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period September–October.

Author: Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan and Tibet; India and Nepal also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert slopes, grassland or gravel floodplain flats.
Life Form: Deciduous woody climbing plant structure, chamaephyte
Morphology: Bipinnate leaves, leaf blade margin entire or with 1–2 slender teeth; sepals yellow, yellow-brown or he purple, inner surface glabrous; persistent style long 0.6–1.5 cm, densely covered with villous. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period September–October.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Gansu and Shaanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 0.5–1 cm, wide 3–8 mm, pinnatisect, lobes 2–4 pairs; sepals 4, oblique on spreading appearing bell-shaped, yellow, apex pointed; persistent style long about 2 cm, with yellow silky-shaped hairs. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, gravelly ground or rock crevices crevices.
Morphology: Leaf blade long 1.5–6 cm, wide 0.5–3 cm, leaf margin sparsely covered serrate-shaped teeth; sepals 4, yellow; persistent style up to 3 cm, with yellow villous. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period October.

Author: (Maxim.) W.T.Wang & L.Q.Li
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi, Ningxia and Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, sandy land or sand dunes low-lying ground.
Morphology: Leaf blade leathery, gray-green, entire, occasionally base with teeth or small lobes; cyme with 3 flower or flowers solitary, pedicel long 0.6–2.5 cm, sepals 4, yellow; achene densely covered white villous. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alluvial fans, rocky gravel ground, sandy soil ground or barren slopes.
Life Form: Deciduous shrub or perennial herb, chamaephyte
Morphology: Simple leaves opposite or clustered, leaf blade thinly leathery, gray-green, linear to lanceolate, entire or with teeth; cyme or round conical-shaped cyme terminal, sepals white, spreading; persistent style long 2–3 cm. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes, grass slope or sandy steppe.
Morphology: Leaves one to twice deeply pinnatifid, lobes entire; flower buds mian spherical-shaped, flower diameter 2.5–5 cm, sepals 4–8, white, outer surface densely covered woolly; persistent style long 1.5–3 cm. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Adams) Kom.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore or riversides saline-alkaline -like marshland.
Morphology: Stolon well developed; leaves basal, leaf blade round cordate to broadly ovate, long 0.4–2.5 cm, margin with 3–10 round teeth; flower diameter about 7 mm, petals 5, yellow; aggregate fruit ovoid, up to 6 mm, achene very mostly, up to 100, beak short. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Jacq.) Ovcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline marshland or wet grassland.
Morphology: Leaves leathery, oblong to ovate appressed elliptic, long 1.2–4.2 cm, apex with 3–5 obtuse teeth; flower diameter about 2 cm, petals 7–13; aggregate fruit ovoid, long about 1 cm. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Maxim.) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in western part and Southwest China; western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline -like wet grassland.
Morphology: Stolon slender slender, horizontally creeping; leaves is simple leaves, leaf blade rhombic appressed cuneate to broadly ovate, long 1–2 cm, wide 0.5–1 cm, 3 deeply lobed; flower diameter 7–10 mm; clustered united fruit subglobose, diameter about 6 mm, achene 20 mostly in number. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Bertol.) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Nepal and Kashmir regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores wet grassland or marshland.
Morphology: Leaves is simple leaves, leaf blade linear to lanceolate, long about 1 cm, wide 1.5–3 mm, entire; petals 5, yellow; aggregate fruit globose, diameter about 5 mm, achene ovate, beak outer curved appearing hooked. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: L.Liou
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet Ge'gyai County.
Habitat: Occurs in halophytic meadow or salt-lake shores.
Morphology: Leaves 2–3 times 2–3 slender lobed, ultimate lobes short linear; petals 5, yellow; aggregate fruit with 20 remaining in number achene, fruit beak straight, apex with small hooked. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Crantz) Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, wasteland or gravel floodplain flats.
Morphology: Plant covered with silky-shaped short-pubescent; leaves oblanceolate or linear, long 3–6 mm; flowers solitary flower scape apex, diameter about 5 mm; aggregate fruit oblong to cylindrical, achene flattened ovate, with long beak. Flowering and fruiting period March–May.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland or shallow water.
Morphology: Plant smooth glabrous; basal leaves numerous, 3 deeply lobed; cyme with mostly flower, flower diameter 4–8 mm; aggregate fruit oblong, long 8–12 mm, achene very numerous, nearly bai in number, with slender wrinkled striations, beak short to nearly without. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to South China and Southwest China each ground; Mongolia, Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland.
Morphology: Leaf blade round cordate or five angles shape, long and wide is 3–10 cm; flower arranged sparse spreading, diameter 1.5–2.2 cm; clustered united fruit subglobose, diameter 6–8 mm, achene flattened, beak short straight or outer curved. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Xinjiang; ou ya bei wen regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in ditch banks grassland.
Morphology: Stem lower part creeping ground surface, nodes places borne roots; trifoliolate leaves, lobes rhombic-shaped cuneate, glabrous; flower diameter 2–2.5 cm; achene flattened, beak straight or outer curved. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China (Northwest China except); Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or watersides marshland.
Morphology: Plant covered with rough hairs; trifoliolate leaves, lobes inverted spreading needle-like cuneate; flower diameter 6–12 mm; aggregate fruit oblong, achene flattened, beak very short, appearing dots-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Pursh
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to South China and Southwest China each ground; Asia Northeast China part and Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, lanceolate, margin with slender zhong serrate; luo-shaped cyme with mostly flower, flowers small, two -like, yellowish white, sepals 5, apetalous, stamens 10, carpels 5, lower part connate; capsule red purple, diameter 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Rupr.) J.Léonard
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Afghanistan, Iran, Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in with white spiny or red sand borne long saline-alkaline land or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant succulent, red brown colored; scale spirally arranged; spadix terminal, flowers small and mixed -like, male flowers with 1 stamens and 1 dense gland, female flowers with 1 pistil, bisexual flowers with 1 stamens and 1 pistil. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–July.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Uses: Stem edible, forage, medicinal, tanning material

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; even-pinnate leaves, leaflet opposite; flowers axillary, pedicel conspicuous short on leaves, flower yellow, sepals 5, petals 5, stamens 10; fruit with divided valves 5, margin with acute spiny. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Yunnan, Hainan and Taiwan; world tropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores, seashore open woodland or hot and dry river valleys.
Morphology: Flowers solitary leaf axils, diameter about 3 cm, pedicel and leaves nearly etc. Long. Flowering period May–June.

Author: (Bunge) Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Kazakhstan and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sand gravel ground or mountain front of plains.
Morphology: Branches hard, apex with spiny pointed; leaves clustered or opposite, leaflet 1 pairs, long spatulate or linear, succulent; flower borne on old branches leaf axils, yellowish green; capsule fruit subglobose, long 18–40 mm, wings wide 5–9 mm. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low slopes, desert, alluvial fan or salinized ground.
Morphology: Branches curved, apex spiny needle-like; leaflet 1 pairs, succulent, linear; capsule pendulous, narrowly ovate or obovate, long 10–24 mm, wide 8–16 mm, wings wide about 2 mm, apex with pointed tip. Fruiting period July.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravel Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Roots coarse; plant spreading spreading; leaflet 1 pairs, stipule white membranous, rachis apex without membranous pointed; pedicel 2 and borne on leaf axils, flower pale green or ju red, stamens long on petals; capsule elliptic, berry-shaped, pendulous. Flowering period June, fruiting period August.

Author: Y.X.Liou
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or mountain front of plains desert.
Morphology: Roots woody; stem and petiole covered with slender milky dots-shaped projections, rough; leaflet 1 pairs, stipule membranous, rachis apex with membranous pointed; petals white, stamens short on petals; capsule narrowly ovate, dry, dehiscent, apex acuminate. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravel low slopes or proluvial gravel scree.
Morphology: Stem and petiole smooth; leaflet 1 pairs; petals white, lower part ju red, stamens long on petals; capsule strips-shaped lanceolate, long 18–25 mm, slightly curved, pendulous. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: (Schrenk) Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in stony or gravelly sandy land.
Morphology: Leaflet 1 pairs, wide obovate, long 15–30 mm, wide 5–20 mm; capsule cylindrical, long 30–50 mm, wide 5–7 mm, curved. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: C.Y.Yang & ZH.J.Li
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Uqturpan.
Habitat: Occurs in desert Gobi desert.
Morphology: Petiole with narrow wings, leaflet 1 pairs, textured slightly thick; flower 1–2 flowers borne on leaf axils, pedicel long 3–4 mm, calyx pale green, stamens short on petals; capsule non- linear-lanceolate, straight, toward on erect. Flowering period June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: J.H.Huang ex C.Y.Yang & J.H.Fan
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Hami.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Leaflet 1 pairs; flower 2–4 flowers borne on leaf axils, pedicel long 5–10 mm, calyx and petals orange red, stamens long on petals; capsule non- linear-lanceolate, straight, toward on erect. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in arid stony desert.
Morphology: Leaflet 1 pairs, narrow obovate, long 5–13 mm, wide 6–10 mm, oblique, margin with sparse stiff hairs; capsule linear-lanceolate, long 25–35 mm, ma dao-shaped curved, with 5-ridged. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Asia, Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized desert or lakeshores sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaflet 1 pairs, textured thick; petals apex nearly white, lower part ju red, stamens long on petals; capsule falcate-shaped curved, oblong or cylindrical, long 2–4 cm, 5-ridged, pendulous. Flowering period 5–6, fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain slopes or river floodplains sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant spreading spreading; stipule herbaceous, leaflet 1 pairs, oblique ovate, gray blue, apex acute pointed; capsule linear-oblong, long about 3 cm, with 5-ridged, toward on shu li. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert slopes lower part or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Plant erect; leaflet 1 pairs, long 1.5–2.5 cm, green, textured thin, apex roundeded-obtuse; capsule short cylindrical, long 10–18 mm, toward lower oblique spreading. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July.

Author: Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile dunes, sandy land or desert riverbanks.
Morphology: Stipule herbaceous, leaflet 1 pairs, apex obtuse; capsule fruit subglobose, pendulous, long about 10 mm, with narrow wings. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Asia, Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alluvial plains, desert oases, moist sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Stipule herbaceous, green, leaflet 1 pairs, textured thick, apex roundeded; capsule oblong or cylindrical, long 1–4 cm, with 5-ridged, pendulous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–September.

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu; Afghanistan, Iran and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Stipule herbaceous, leaflet 1 pairs, oblique ovate, long 2.5–4 cm, wide 1.5–3.5 cm; capsule short round column-shaped, base narrow, long 1.5–2 cm, wide 5–7 mm, with 5-ridged. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, field margins or salinized sandy land.
Morphology: Petiole with narrow wings, leaflet 1 pairs, oblique ovate, apex slightly orbicular, long 3–4 cm; flower 2 flowers and borne on leaf axils, flower lobes white; capsule long cylindrical, long 2–4 cm, two end obtuse, with 5-ridged, shu li. Flowering period May, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Y.X.Liou
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain slopes or ditch banks sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Leaflet 1 pairs, long 2–4 cm, wide 1.5–3 cm, stipule herbaceous; stamens long on petals; capsule shu li, oblanceolate or oblong, lower part gradually narrow, long 2.5–4 cm, slightly with ridges, wingless. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly desert or stony low slopes.
Morphology: Rachis with wings, leaflet 1–2 pairs, long 1–2.5 cm, wide 0.5–2 cm, thickened, stipule herbaceous; stamens long on sepals; capsule pendulous, subglobose, long 15–30 mm, with wide wings. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Plant rough, covered with small milky-shaped projections; leaflet often 2 pairs; capsule elliptic, long 10–12 mm, berry-shaped, mostly sap.

Author: Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert low slopes or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant smooth; leaflet 1–3 pairs, long 10–12 mm; capsule cylindrical, oblong or long ovate, up to 20 mm, wide about 6 mm, two end obtuse, with ridges or narrow wings, style persistent. Flowering period May, fruiting period July–August.

Author: C.Y.Yang & J.H.Fan
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Fukang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Plant smooth; rachis with narrow wings, apex with white membranous pointed, leaflet 2–3 pairs, strips-shaped lanceolate, long 10–25 mm, wide 2–3 mm; capsule slender cylindrical, long 20–25 mm, wide 2–4 mm, with ridges and wingless. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan, Russia and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, proluvial fans, salt becoming sand soil or saxaul woodland.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground; petiole flattened with wings, leaflet 2–3 pairs, linear-oblong, wide 2–5 mm; capsule oblong-ovate, long 10–30 mm, wide 6–8 mm, winged, wings wide 2–3 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.Y.Yang & J.H.Fan
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang wu lu plant qi.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant erect; rachis apex with membranous filiform pointed, leaflet 3 pairs, spatulate, long 10–12 mm; capsule broadly elliptic or oblong-ovate, with narrow wings, toward on spreading. Flowering period July, fruiting period August.

Author: Popov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly ground or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Plant shrubland-shaped; leaflet 2–3 pairs, strips-shaped oblong, wide 1–3 mm; capsule membranous, lanceolate, toward on gradually narrow, long 2–3 cm, base wide 7–10 mm, with 5 narrow wings, wings wide 2–3 mm. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period May–July.

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant cushion-forming; stipule with white membranous margins, leaflet 2–3 pairs, linear-oblong, long 4–8 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm; capsule round conical-shaped, long-acuminate, long 10–17 mm, base diameter 5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period summer.

Author: C.Y.Yang & Y.Feng
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Altay and fu sea.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Roots coarse; rachis with narrow membrane wings, leaflet 2–3 pairs, long 4–5 mm, wide 2–3 mm, stipule membranous, free; capsule broadly ovate, long 8–10 mm, wide 6–7 mm, wings wide 2–3 mm. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: C.Y.Yang & J.H.Fan
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Yiwu.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain gullies valley.
Morphology: Rachis with narrow wings, leaflet lower part 2–3 pairs, upper part 4 pairs, long 8–10 mm, wide 3–5 mm; capsule oblong-obovate, long about 20 mm, wide about 1 mm, with 5 narrow wings, toward on spreading. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert zone riversides sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Without main stem; leaflet 2–3 pairs, long 10–15 mm, wide 5–10 mm; capsule subglobose or ovate-shaped globose, diameter 16–30 mm, with 5 wide wings. Flowering period May, fruiting period June.

Author: Cham.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; West Asia, Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert low mountain or river terraces.
Morphology: Plant rough, covered with small papillae or slender spiny; leaflet 3–5 pairs; capsule fruit subglobose or ovate-shaped globose, long 2–4.5 cm, wings wide 5–12 mm, membranous. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–August.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain front of plains, alluvial fans or yellow earthen slopes.
Morphology: Plant prostrate, grooved ridges and rough prickles; rachis with wings, leaflet 2–3 pairs, linear, with spiny pointed; stamens long on petals; capsule lanceolate or cylindrical, slightly with 5-ridged, apex acuminate or acute pointed, pendulous. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: C.Y.Yang
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Jinghe.
Habitat: Occurs in shifting sands.
Morphology: Roots slender and succulent; stem of-shaped shape curved; rachis with narrow wings, leaflet 1 pairs, long about 15 mm, wide 7–8 mm; capsule cylindrical, long about 25 mm, wingless. Flowering period June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil ground or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Roots slender; plant creeping; leaflet 1 pairs, obovate, long 7–15 mm, wide 5–7 mm; flower yellow, axillary; capsule pendulous, broad elliptic, long 15–18 mm, with wide wings. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Kanitz
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in proluvial plains, gravelly Gobi gravel desert or salt becoming sandy land.
Morphology: Roots slender; leaflet 1–3 pairs, long 6–25 mm; petals nearly ovate, ju red, margin white, stamens short on petals; capsule cylindrical, long 16–25 mm, wingless, apex acute pointed or obtuse. Flowering period April–July, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Ningxia.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes, riversides terraces or dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaves clustered or opposite, oblanceolate, long 5–7 mm, wide 2–3 mm, gray-green, entire; flower 4 several, petals white; fruit 4 lobes lobed, valves long ovate or crescent-shaped, long 5–6 mm, gray-green. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: D.Gordon ex Y.T.Lee
Distribution: Distributed in Hebei, Shanxi, Shandong, Henan, Shaanxi, Anhui and Jiangsu.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes.
Morphology: Branches spiny long acicular; pinnate leaves, leaflet 5–12 pairs, long 6–24 mm, wide 3–10 mm, entire; flowers small, mixed -like, green white; legume flattened and thin, long 3–6 cm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–October.

Author: Lam.
Distribution: Distributed in North China south of each ground.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, valleys or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Branches spiny coarse robust, often branched; once-pinnate leaves, leaflet 3–9 pairs, long 2–10 cm, wide 1–6 cm, margin with finely serrate; legume band-shaped, long 12–37 cm, both surfaces thickened gu qi. Flowering period March–May, fruiting period May–December.

Author: Miq.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, East China, Central China and Southwest China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in toward sunny slopes, valleys or seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Branches spiny slightly flattened; legume band shape, long 20–35 cm, flattened, irregular xuan niu or curved used as falcate-shaped, often with bladder-like-shaped raised. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period June–November.

Author: (Makino) H.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, East China, Central China and Southwest China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, fields or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 8–28 pairs, petiole with glands 1, leaflet long 5–9 mm, band-shaped lanceolate; inflorescence axillary, flower yellow; legume flattened, hairy, dehiscent, long 3–8 cm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; also found in southern Asia and Australia.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore shrubland.
Life Form: Evergreen woody climbing plant structure, phanerophyte
Morphology: Plant with inverted hooked spiny; bipinnate leaves, leaflet leathery, long 3–6 cm; raceme rows forming panicle, flower yellow; legume oblique broad ovate, leathery, long 3–4 cm, swollen, with beak. Flowering period April–July, fruiting period July–December.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Taiwan; world tropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in beach sandy land.
Life Form: Evergreen woody climbing plant structure, phanerophyte
Morphology: Plant densely covered with spiny; bipinnate leaves large type, leaflet oblong; raceme axillary, flower yellow; legume leathery, long 5–7 cm, apex with beak, inflated, densely covered with spiny. Flowering period August–October, fruiting period October–March.

Author: Link
Distribution: Native to Australia, South China and Southwest China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in hills mountain ground or sandy soil.
Morphology: Bipinnate leaves, often appearing yin gray, rachis with conspicuous glands, leaflet linear; capitulum pale yellow or orange yellow, compound rows forming panicle; legume long 3–8 cm, flattened, covered with glaucous bloom. Flowering period April, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (L.) Schott
Distribution: Native to northern China, each ground often with cultivated; Asia, Europe and the Americas also found cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in plains sandy soil.
Morphology: Current-year branches green; pinnate leaves, leaflet long 2.5–6 cm, entire; large type panicle terminal, flower white or pale yellow; legume moniliform, with succulent pericarp, mature after indehiscent. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–October.

Author: R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sand dunes, riverbanks sandy land or salt-lake shores gravel flat.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with pubescent; 3 leaflet, leaflet long 2.5–7.5 cm; raceme terminal, flower whorled, yellow, wings lobes and keel lobes etc. Wide or narrow; legume linear, flattened, apex with pointed beak. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–October.

Author: Czefr.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, gravelly desert and salinized sand flat.
Morphology: Leaflet linear-lanceolate to linear, long 3–7 cm, long is wide 5 times above; raceme terminal, flower whorled, yellow, ji lobes than keel lobes narrow; legume linear, flattened, apex acute. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Maxim. ex Kom.) S.H.Cheng
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or river floodplains tablelands.
Morphology: Tree surface yellow; 3 leaflet, leaflet long 2–3.5 cm, both surfaces densely covered with silvery white soft hairs, entire; raceme terminal, flower yellow; legume flattened, linear, long 5–8 cm, margin straight. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Popov) S.H.Cheng
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang Kashgar, Kyrgyzstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly slopes.
Morphology: Often is simple leaves, leaflet entire, broad elliptic to ovate, long 1.5–4 cm; legume linear, long 3–5 cm, margin convex concave not flat. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Pall.) Yakovlev
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in relatively arid sand stones band or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with silvery white short-pubescent; compound leaves, only with 2 leaflet, terminal leaflet and stipule all becoming is spiny; raceme terminal, flower deep purple; legume flattened, oblong-lanceolate, with narrow wings. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, gravelly ground or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with grayish white villous; pinnate leaves, leaflet long 15–30 mm; raceme terminal, flower white or pale yellow; legume moniliform, long 8–13 cm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Franch.) Skeels
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China, East China, Central China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys sand dunes or slopes shrubland.
Morphology: Plant with spiny; pinnate leaves, leaflet long 10–15 mm; raceme, flowers small, calyx bluish purple, corolla white or pale yellow; legume moniliform, slightly compressed, long 6–8 cm. Flowering period March–August, fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Benth.) Benth. ex Baker
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Bhutan, India and Nepal also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides sandy land or dry slopes shrubland.
Morphology: Branchlets densely covered with soft hairs; pinnate leaves; raceme, flower relatively large, calyx blue, corolla bluish purple; legume appearing inconspicuous moniliform, slightly compressed, long about 6 cm. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan, Hong Kong and Taiwan; world tropical regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or shrubland.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white short soft hairs; pinnate leaves, leaflet nearly leathery; raceme terminal, flower pale yellow or nearly white; legume moniliform, long 7–10 cm, covered with short soft hairs. Flowering period August–October, fruiting period September–December.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, China from Northeast to Southwest China each ground cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or sandy soil.
Morphology: Odd-pinnate leaves, leaflet ovate or elliptic, long 1–4 cm; spike terminal and axillary, flower arranged compact, purple, without wings lobes and keel lobes; legume pendulous, long 6–10 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, soil and water conservation, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: DC.
Distribution: Native to India, Fujian, Guangdong and Hainan and elsewhere cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 1–2 pairs, suborbicular or rhombic-shaped obovate, long 3.5–6 cm; panicle nearly umbel locule-shaped, axillary, flower pale yellow or white; legume linear-oblong to band-shaped, long 4–8 cm. Flowering period March–April, fruiting period September–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia, large yang zhou and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; even-pinnate leaves, leaflet 20–30 pairs, leaflet linear-oblong, long 5–15 mm; raceme than leaves short, flower pale yellow, with purple striations; legume linear-oblong, with 4–8 nodes, mature when zhu nodes deciduous. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, Hainan, Taiwan and Hong Kong and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, fields or waterside wetlands.
Life Form: Annual or short ming perennial herb, chamaephyte
Morphology: Plant covered with long glandular hairs; leaflet oblong, wide 1–3 mm, long 9–16 mm; inflorescence axillary, flower white, with purple spots; legume linear, containing 6–8 nodes, mature when zhu nodes deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Hong Kong; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open places saline-alkaline soil wetland.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 1–2 pairs, long 15–20 mm; short xie tail-shaped cyme, flower 5–6 flowers, corolla yellow, long is calyx long 1.5 times, calyx lobes membranous; legume with 10–12 jia nodes. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Taiwan and Yunnan; South Asia, Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 3–5 pairs, leathery, ovate, long 6–11 cm, wide 4–5 cm; panicle mostly flower, flower yellow; legume orbicular, flattened, wide about 5 cm, with wide wings. Flowering period spring, fruiting period autumn.

Author: Baker
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Yunnan; South Asia, Southeast Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant prostrate; pinnate leaves, leaflet 2–5 pairs, long 3–8 mm, wide 1–3.5 mm; raceme short contracted, long 4–10 mm, flower purplish red; legume oblong, long 2.5–5 mm, with 2 grains seeds. Flowering period August, fruiting period November.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Walp.
Distribution: Distributed in North China to Southwest China each ground; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 2–4 pairs, long 5–1.5 mm, wide 3–10 mm; raceme axillary, short on leaves, flower purple or purplish red; legume linear-shaped cylindrical. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Thunb.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Japan, Korea and Southeast Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach, riverbanks or open wasteland.
Morphology: Pinnate-shaped 3 leaflet, apex rounded or minutely mucronate; raceme axillary, flower pale purplish red, calyx on lip lobed teeth apex abaxial surface minutely mucronate; legume long elliptic, flattened, long 6–10 cm, seeds large, ovate, long about 1.7 cm. Flowering and fruiting period March–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Thouars
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; tropical Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore or riverbanks.
Morphology: Leaf blade apex acute or round, but not emarginate; calyx on lip lobed teeth apex abaxial surface without small pointed tip; legume oblong, long 7–9 cm, wide 3.5–4.5 cm, seeds he black, long 1.8 cm. Flowering and fruiting period March–October.

Author: (Sw.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Taiwan; widely distributed on tropical coasts worldwide.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores.
Morphology: Leaf blade apex rounded or truncate, often emarginate, rarely acuminate; legume linear-oblong, long 8–12 cm, wide 2–2.5 cm; seeds brown, long 1.3–1.5 cm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Pers.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland slopes.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 4–8 pairs, long 15–35 mm, wide 4–14 mm; raceme, flower sparse spreading, pale purple; legume linear, long 4–5 cm, with short beak, covered with sparse prostrate pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period March–October.

Author: (L.) Pierre
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Asia, Africa, Oceania and the Americas also found.
Habitat: Occurs in stream banks, pond margins or seashore mangrove forest.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 2–3 pairs, nearly leathery, ovate to broadly elliptic; raceme axillary, flower often 2 flowers clustered, flower white or pale red; legume oblong, flattened, with 1 grains seeds. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August–October.
Uses: Timber, oilseed, medicinal, windbreak, soil and water conservation, street tree
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Lour.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in nearly sea riverbanks shrubland or mangrove forest.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or woody climbing plant structure, phanerophyte
Morphology: Entire plant glabrous; odd-pinnate leaves, leaflet 3–7, thick papery or thinly leathery, long 5–10 cm; raceme axillary, flower white or pink; legume oblique ovate orbicular, flattened, with seeds 1–2 grains. Flowering period April–August, fruiting period August–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Maxim.) Makino
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; eastern Asia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, grassland, slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Three protruding pinnate leaves, leaflet long 5–18 mm, wide 3–12 mm, lateral veins mostly and dense; flower often 1–2 flowers axillary, flower purple; legume relatively calyx long 1.5–3 times. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Laxm.) Schindl.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Korea, Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, grassland, river floodplains ground or sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Three protruding pinnate leaves, leaflet long 2–5 cm, wide 5–16 mm; raceme axillary, often short on leaves, flower white or yellowish white, often with bi suo flower; legume small, long 3–4 mm, enclosing on persistent calyx inner. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (L.f.) Pers.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes shrubland or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaflet long 1.5–3.5 cm, wide 3–7 mm, apex slightly pointed or rounded-obtuse; raceme slightly exceeding protruding leaves, flower white or pale yellow, with bi suo flower; legume broadly ovate, slightly exceeding protruding persistent calyx. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Lour.) H.Ohashi & K.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; Cambodia and Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Single leaflet, sparse is trifoliolate leaves, leaflet elliptic; raceme terminal, flower 2 flowers and borne, calyx red, corolla blue or pink; legume narrow oblong, flattened, jia nodes 2–7, nearly square shape. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China, large mostly cultivated; South Asia, Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in nearly sea sandy soil or plains.
Morphology: Spiny coarse robust; pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, long wide 15–30 cm; raceme terminal, calyx spathe-shaped, corolla red; legume black, thickened. Flowering period March, fruiting period August.

Author: (Burm.) Merr.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan, Hong Kong and Taiwan; world tropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, leaflet nearly leathery, long 3.5–9.5 cm, wide 2.5–9.5 cm; raceme long 2–4 cm, flower yellow; legume linear-oblong, slightly curved, swollen, long 3.5–6 cm. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (L.) A.Rich.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in barren wild, shrubland or forest margins.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, leaflet membranous, long 4–9 cm, wide 2–2.5 cm; inflorescence axillary, nearly umbel shape, standard petal yellow, powdery red or purple, wings lobes purple; legume erect, linear-shaped cylindrical, covered with bristles. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Roxb.) Ohwi & H.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, East China and South China; Japan, the Philippines and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, sandy soil or river floodplains rocky gravel ground.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, leaflet long 2.5–7 cm, wide 0.8–3 cm; raceme rou weak, flower 3–4 flowers, yellow; legume cylindrical, long 3.5–6.5 cm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (DC.) Urb.
Distribution: Native to tropical America; Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan and elsewhere cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, stipule ovate, leaflet ovate to rhombic, lower surface covered with yin colored soft hairs; flower deep purple; legume linear, long 5–9 cm. Flowering and fruiting period March–October.

Author: (L.) Urb.
Distribution: Native to tropical America; Fujian, Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan and elsewhere cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves with 3 leaflet, stipule lanceolate, leaflet narrow elliptic to ovate-lanceolate, lower surface densely covered with short-pubescent or thin covered with villous; flower red; legume linear, long 5.5–10 cm. Flowering period July, fruiting period September–November.

Author: Siebold & Zucc.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Afghanistan, Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil, waterside wetlands or salinized and alkalized wetland.
Morphology: Plant dense hairy; leaves with 3 leaflet; raceme short, flowers small, pale red purple or white; legume oblong, long 17–23 mm, wide 4–5 mm, densely covered with long stiff hairs. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Retz.) Poir.
Distribution: Possibly native to Oceania, East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 20–30 pairs, wide 2.5–4 mm; raceme with 2–6 flowers flower, flower yellow, standard petal wide large on long; legume long cylindrical, long 12–22 cm, wide 2.5–3.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Jacq.) W.Wight
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides moist places.
Morphology: Branchlets, rachis, inflorescence axis mostly few with prickles-shaped small projections; leaflet usually glabrous; standard petal long large on wide. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.

Author: (L.) Merr.
Distribution: Native to ground unclear, Guangdong, Hainan, Taiwan and Yunnan cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in stream banks, marsh margins or moist river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Branchlets internodes long often short on 3 cm, at nodes raised; leaflet 10–20 pairs, rachis long 4–10 cm; standard petal long 11–13 mm, pian zhi body S type curved, with conspicuous free pointed tip. Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.

Author: (L.) Poir.
Distribution: Possibly native to Indonesia and Malaysia, South China, Southwest China and Taiwan cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in warm and humid low ground.
Life Form: Short ming evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet long 2–5 cm, wide 8–16 mm; raceme pendulous, with 2–4 flower, flower buds falcate-shaped curved, flowers large, long 7–10 cm, white to rose red; legume linear, pendulous, long 20–60 cm, wide 8–9 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Central China and Southwest China; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist slopes, grassland or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 5, oblique ovate, long 5–15 mm, wide 4–10 mm; umbel, flower 3–7 flowers, yellow or golden yellow, long 10–15 mm; legume straight, linear-shaped cylindrical. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 21; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Waldst. & Kit. ex Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist marsh margin or lake sides grassland.
Morphology: Leaflet linear, long 12–25 mm, wide 2–4 mm; peduncle slender and long, long 3–8 cm, with flower 1–5 flowers, flower long 6–11 mm, bract long on calyx, calyx teeth long etc. On calyx tube. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 29, 506; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Freyn
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; western Asia and Europe southeastern part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline-alkaline grass flat or marsh margin.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; leaflet obovate to inverted ovate-elliptic, long 7–13 mm, wide 4–6 mm; peduncle slender slender, long 2–5 cm, bract and calyx etc. Long. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period July–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to North America, northern China regions widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: With stipule spiny; pinnate leaves, leaflet elliptic or ovate, long 2–5 cm, wide 1.5–2.2 cm; raceme axillary, pendulous, flower white, fang xiang; legume linear-oblong, flattened. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Fisch. ex DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, watersides sandy land or salinized floodplain flats.
Morphology: Odd-pinnate leaves, leaflet ovate to suborbicular; raceme axillary, flower purple, white or yellow; legume curved appearing falcate-shaped or appearing ring-shaped, dense forming spherical, densely covered tuberculate projections and spiny hairs-shaped glands. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–October.
Uses: Medicinal, fiber, aromatic use, sweetener, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Fisch. ex DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, watersides sandy land or salinized floodplain flats.
Morphology: Odd-pinnate leaves, leaflet ovate to suborbicular; raceme axillary, flower purple, white or yellow; legume curved appearing falcate-shaped or appearing ring-shaped, dense forming spherical, densely covered tuberculate projections and spiny hairs-shaped glands. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–October.
Uses: Medicinal, fiber, aromatic use, sweetener, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert zone riverbanks terraces or salinized floodplain flats.
Morphology: Leaflet lanceolate or oblong-lanceolate; legume straight or slightly curved, smooth or with spiny hairs-shaped glands. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Batalin
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks terraces, sandy wasteland or salinized floodplain flats.
Morphology: Leaflet 3–7, nearly leathery, long 2–6 cm, wide 0.8–3 cm; legume ovate orbicular or oblong, swollen, pericarp leathery and hard, without spiny hairs, with seeds 1–4 grains. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period 6–l Oyue.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in field margins, ditch banks or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground borne long, covered with spiny hairs-shaped glands; leaflet 5–9; raceme axillary, high protruding leaves tuft; legume nian zhu-shaped, long 15–25 mm, glabrous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Franch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Hebei, Shanxi, Henan, Shaanxi, Ningxia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks terraces, watersides sandy soil or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaflet 9–13, oblong or lanceolate, apex emarginate or obtuse; raceme axillary, flower white; legume suborbicular or orbicular-reniform, long 5–10 mm, with tubercles projecting and glandular dots, with 2 seeds. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Southwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains ground or seashore sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaflet 9–15, lanceolate, densely covered with scale-like glands, margin with slightly small hooked slender teeth; flower dense forming spherical-shaped, pale purple; legume ovate orbicular, densely covered with long stiff spiny, with seeds 2. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Shap. ex Keller & Shap.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, riverbanks margins floodplain flats or saline-alkaline flats.
Morphology: Simple leaves alternate, long 0.8–1.5 cm, apex rounded, entire, glabrous; raceme axillary, inflorescence axis becoming forming hard acute spiny, flower deep purplish red; legume moniliform, internodes elliptic round body shape, indehiscent. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Uses: Forage, medicinal, sweetener, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Maxim.) B.H.Choi & H.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravel alluvial fans, gravel river floodplains and dry slopes.
Morphology: Half shrub; odd-pinnate leaves, leaflet 15–29, ovate orbicular; raceme long on leaves, flower rose red, wings lobes long not exceeding standard petal 1/3, calyx tube oblique bell-shaped, in on calyx teeth between dehiscent, on calyx teeth among deeply lobed reaching calyx tube 2/3; legume with needle-like spiny. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (B.Fedtsch.) B.H.Choi & H.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert slopes.
Morphology: Calyx tube two bilabiate, in on calyx teeth and lateral calyx teeth between dehiscent. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July.

Author: (Fisch. & C.A.Mey.) Fisch. & Basiner
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
