Morphology: Leaflet 7–11, narrowly lanceolate, upper part rachis without or only with 1 leaflet; raceme axillary, flower few, purplish red, wings lobes long is standard petal 2/3; legume with zhan-shaped hairs. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Pall.) B.H.Choi & H.Ohashi
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Leaflet 11–19, oblong; raceme and leaves nearly etc. Long, flower purplish red, standard petal inverted ovate orbicular, on calyx teeth among not divided; legume with 2–3 jia nodes, covered with short-pubescent, with needle-like spiny. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Turcz.) Turcz. ex Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Plant lower part and upper part leaflet arranged and shape-shaped not same; legume without needle-like spiny. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Trautv.) L.R.Xu & B.H.Choi
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Shanxi and Shaanxi; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Leaflet relatively narrow, wide 2–4 mm; inflorescence often exceeding protruding leaves, flower purple, calyx teeth triangular, long is calyx tube one half, wings lobes long about is standard petal 1/3; legume glabrous, without needle-like spiny. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Maxim.) L.R.Xu & B.H.Choi
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Shanxi and Shaanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Calyx teeth short triangular, acute pointed, long only is calyx tube 1/3, wings lobes short and pointed, long about is standard petal 1/4; ovary and legume glabrous and spiny. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Scop.
Distribution: Native to Europe, northern China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant covered with short-pubescent; leaflet 13–19, lanceolate; raceme long on leaves, in flowers in front of with tufted hairs, flower rose purple, calyx teeth spreading needle-like subulate; legume with 1 half orbicular nodes jia, margin with spiny. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Pall.) Dum.Cours.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Northwest China ya, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming desert sandy land, riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Entire plant glabrous; even-pinnate leaves, leaflet 2–4, oblanceolate, rachis and stipule persistent, needle-like la-shaped; raceme with 2–5 flower, peduncle long 1.5–3 cm, flower pale purple or purplish red; legume inflated, thickly leathery. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Kom.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, mountain gullies, yellow soil hills or sandy land.
Morphology: Young branches densely covered with white pubescent; pinnate leaves, leaflet 3–6 pairs, rachis persistent, entire stiff becoming forming needle-like spiny; pedicel solitary, flower yellow, wings lobes auricles relatively lobes stalk slightly short; legume round tube-shaped, covered with white villous. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Lam.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, rachis deciduous, leaflet 5–10 pairs, long 3–10 mm; calyx tubular campanulate, corolla yellow, wings lobes lobes stalk long is lobes segments 1/2; legume tubular. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Kom.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi, Ningxia and Gansu; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with pubescent; pinnate leaves, stipule in long branches zhe stiff becoming forming needle-like spiny, leaflet 6–8 pairs; legume flattened, lanceolate, long 2–2.5 cm, wide 6–7 mm. Flowering period May, fruiting period June.

Author: Pojark.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, mountain front of plains or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Tree surface yellow; accessory palmately compound leaves with 4 segments leaflet, stipule and petiole in long branches on stiff becoming forming needle-like spiny, leaflet clustered, narrow oblanceolate, wide 1–3 mm; flower yellow, ovary glabrous; legume tubular, glabrous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (L.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Ovary covered with dense hairs; legume long 1–2 cm, with pubescent. Flowering period May, fruiting period June.

Author: Pojark.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shanxi, Shaanxi, Ningxia and Gansu; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in half desert zone mountain front of plains, low slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaflet oblanceolate, apex acute pointed; pedicel short coarse, long 2–5 mm, calyx tubular, base oblique forming sac-shaped projections, long 9–11 mm, corolla yellow; legume lanceolate, flattened, glabrous. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Central China and Southwest China; Korea, Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land salinized and alkalized wetland.
Morphology: Without ground on stem; pinnate leaves, leaflet 7–19 segments, both surfaces covered with sparse pubescent, sometimes upper surface glabrous; umbel with flower 2–4 flowers, flower red purple; legume long round tube-shaped, covered with villous. Flowering period May, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly ground, dry river floodplains or hills sloping ground.
Morphology: Even-pinnate leaves, rachis persistent, stiff spiny-shaped, long 2–6 cm, leaflet 4–6 pairs; raceme axillary, flower 1–2 flowers, red purple; legume stiff leathery, oblong. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Tajikistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem well developed, covered with spreading sparse pubescent; pinnate leaves long 5–10 cm; stipule connate at the base, leaflet 11–15, wide 2–7 mm; raceme, flower purple, corolla long 6–8 mm; legume covered with spreading pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, river-valley terraces or saline soil grass flat.
Morphology: Stem well developed, erect or spreading flat; leaflet 9–13, wide 2–6 mm; flower sparse, corolla purple, long about 7 mm; legume pendulous, long elliptic, covered with appressed short-pubescent. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, river-valley terraces or saline soil grass flat.
Morphology: Stem well developed, erect or spreading flat; leaflet 9–13, wide 2–6 mm; flower sparse, corolla purple, long about 7 mm; legume pendulous, long elliptic, covered with appressed short-pubescent. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period July–September.

Author: S.S.Cheng ex C.W.Chang
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or salinized meadow.
Morphology: Stem erect or half creeping-shaped; stipule free, leaflet 7–13, wide 2–6 mm; corolla yellow; legume pendulous, oblong, bladder-like-shaped inflated, covered with half spreading white short-pubescent. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Freyn
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides meadow or in alpine areas lake shores floodplain flats.
Morphology: Plant covered with white short stiff hairs; stipule base each other connate very high, leaflet 15–21, wide 1.5–3 mm; flower purple, standard petal long 6–9 mm, keel lobes beak long 0.3–0.5 mm; legume covered with appressed black short-pubescent. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Boriss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands, river valleys floodplain flats or salt saline meadow.
Morphology: Plant covered with sparse pubescent; stem erect, not very well developed; leaflet 17–31, wide 2–3 mm; raceme short, peduncle and leaves etc. Long, flowers bluish purple, keel lobes beak long 0.3–0.5 mm; legume covered with appressed black and white short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Vassilcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, gravel floodplain flats or salt saline meadow.
Morphology: Plant covered with appressed sparse pubescent; stem short contracted, prostrate; leaflet 11–19, wide 1–2 mm; raceme head-shaped, flower deep red, keel lobes beak long 1.5–2 mm; legume covered with appressed slender pubescent. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Krylov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe valley floors, riverbanks saline-alkaline land or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Stem short contracted; raceme short, bract small, calyx teeth than calyx tube short 2–4 times, corolla purple, standard petal apex slightly notched, keel lobes beak long 1–1.5 mm; legume covered with appressed pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Hebei; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, hills rocky sloping ground or sand stones grassland.
Morphology: Plant slightly covered with short-pubescent, green; leaflet both surfaces glabrous, leaf margin sparsely covered with villous; 3–7 flower composed of short raceme, corolla white or pale yellow; legume nearly papery, inflated, glabrous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland, watersides sandy soil or forest margins.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with yellow villous; leaflet 4 whorled or opposite, non- orbicular; mostly flower composed of dense round tube-shaped raceme, peduncle and leaves nearly etc. Long, corolla white or pale yellow, standard petal apex rounded; legume ovoid. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, sandy land or open wasteland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with spreading villous; leaflet 4 whorled or opposite; calyx long 9–12 mm, corolla bluish purple, standard petal middle part with conspicuous yellowish green spots, keel lobes beak long 2–2.5 mm; legume ovate-shaped oblong, wide about 5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: (Pall.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry streams and ditches, slightly salinized sandy land or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with yellowish white villous; whorled pinnate leaves long 10–30 cm, leaflet 25–32 whorls, each whorls 4–8 segments; corolla pale red purple, standard petal long about 18 mm; legume spreading needle-like elliptic. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Korea and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land, watersides rocky gravel ground or hills dry slopes.
Morphology: Stipule large part and petiole appressed borne, leaflet 4–6 whorled, wide 1–2 mm; raceme head-shaped, flower red purple, long 12 mm, calyx campanulate; legume membranous, ovoid, inflated. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–October.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in China long river north of regions; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, watersides sandy land or sandy steppe.
Morphology: Stem green, much-branched; leaflet 5–7, oblong; raceme distant long on leaves, flowers small, arranged sparse, white or band pink; legume ovate spherical-shaped, long 2.5–3.5 mm. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: L.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides moist sandy land, saline-alkaline land or forest margins wet grassland.
Morphology: Leaflet 17–25, wide 4–9 mm, upper surface glabrous; peduncle relatively leaves short, raceme borne numerous flower, flower yellow; legume elliptic, pendulous, base fruit jing long 6–9 mm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline land or salinized meadow.
Morphology: Plant covered with pale yellow villous; stem conspicuous; leaflet ovate orbicular, stipule leathery; raceme borne 4–7 flower, corolla yellow, standard petal outer surface densely covered with pubescent, keel lobes glabrous; legume ovate, valves leathery, densely covered with pubescent. Flowering period April–May; fruiting period May–June.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant covered with white villous; stem short contracted; leaflet elliptic round-shaped ovate, stipule membranous; raceme borne 8–14 flower, corolla yellow, standard petal outer surface upper part densely covered with pubescent, keel lobes apex covered with pubescent; legume densely covered with white pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Fisch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Stem short contracted; stipule membranous, leaflet opposite, suborbicular or obovate; raceme borne 10–15 flower, corolla yellow, up to 30–35 mm, ovary stalk long 6–7 mm; legume ovate-shaped oblong. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Pallas) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China and elsewhere; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in open slopes or river floodplains grassland.
Morphology: Plant covered with spreading white pubescent; stem conspicuous; leaflet 11–23, oblong, stipule free; raceme nearly head-shaped, after extending long, flower dense, purple, keel lobes distant relatively wings lobes long; legume linear, curved. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wasteland.
Morphology: Stem prostrate or ascending; pinnate leaves long 5–15 cm, leaflet 13–21; raceme dense appearing head-shaped, flower 5–10 flowers, yellow, keel lobes relatively wings lobes long; legume dense, curved forming falcate-shaped or ring shape. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil or slopes grassland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with white villous; leaflet long 5–20 mm, middle part above wider, lower surface white; raceme compact appearing round column-shaped or ovate, calyx bell-shaped, inflated, long 15–18 mm, calyx teeth and calyx tube nearly etc. Long, corolla pale yellow; legume ovate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, Russia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks or ditch banks slopes.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with golden yellow villous; leaflet relatively large, middle part below wider, long 2–4.5 cm, lower surface sparsely covered with golden yellow pubescent; calyx long 12–18 mm, calyx teeth than calyx tube short, standard petal narrow inverted ovate-shaped spatulate. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wetland sand dunes, Gobi gravel desert or rocky dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaflet 25–31, spreading borne villous; inflorescence head-shaped racemose or ovate-shaped racemose, long 4–6 cm, peduncle long 4–6 cm, standard petal long 2.8–3 cm, keel lobes and standard petal nearly etc. Long. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, Central Asia and high jia suo regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaflet 15–23 segments; inflorescence long 7–12 cm, peduncle up to 2 cm, calyx in flowering period when tubular, flowering period after bang guang-shaped, densely covered with villous, ovary with stalk; ovary and legume densely covered with villous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Bunge ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Qinghai; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry river floodplains or alluvial sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Stem well developed; leaflet 11–19; raceme borne 7–9 flower, flower arranged sparse pine, calyx tubular campanulate, long 5–6 mm, corolla pale bluish purple, glabrous, wings lobes apex slightly notched; legume linear-oblong, long 10–20 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaflet 15–23; flower arranged sparse, flower pale purple, wings lobes apex entire or emarginate; legume without fruit jing, base enclosing covered with in calyx of inner, oblique li, linear-oblong, long 9–11 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: S.B.Ho
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or nearly water sandy land.
Morphology: Plant sparsely covered with grayish white appressed hairs; leaflet 5–9, leaflet ovate orbicular; raceme borne 6–15 flower, flower white or pale red, wings lobes apex entire; legume slender cylindrical, slightly curved, long 3–4 cm. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Northeast China ya, North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy steppe, watersides sandy land or moist slopes.
Morphology: Leaflet 19–29, oblong-elliptic, covered with sparse appressed appressed hairs; raceme spike-shaped, flowers numerous, arranged compact, calyx teeth short, long is calyx tube 1/2, corolla bluish purple; legume long 7–18 mm. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: S.B.Ho
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert Gobi gravel desert or dry river valleys sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with white short hairs; stem short contracted; compound leaves with long on rachis 1–2 times petiole, leaflet ovate orbicular; raceme borne 7–10 flower, peduncle relatively leaves short, flower pale purplish red, wings lobes apex entire; legume bang guang-shaped swollen large. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in flooded river floodplains or sandy land.
Morphology: Stem creeping or ascending; leaflet 11–27, ovate orbicular; raceme short contracted forming head-shaped, corolla pink, outer surface sparsely covered with spreading woolly hairs; legume ovate orbicular, densely covered with spreading woolly hairs. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in central China, Northeast China part and Northwest China part; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky gravelly slopes, dry steppe or watersides sandy land.
Morphology: Plant creeping spreading ground borne long, densely covered with appressed appressed hairs; leaflet 7–15, elliptic; raceme borne 3–5 flower, calyx covered with appressed appressed hairs, corolla pale yellow or white; legume spreading needle-like oblong, slightly curved. Flowering period April–August, fruiting period May–September.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy beach or Gobi desert saline-alkaline grassland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with long stiff hairs; stem short contracted; leaflet 13–31, elliptic; raceme borne 6–9 flower, calyx covered with spreading long hairs, corolla pale purple or band pink; legume oblong. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy soil ground or toward sunny slopes.
Morphology: Stem very short contracted; leaflet 9–37, upper surface glabrous; flower clusters borne on base leaf axils, corolla milky white or slightly band yellow; legume small, ovate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Sanchir
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Inner Mongolia and Ningxia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Stem very short contracted; leaflet 11–27; raceme short contracted, borne mostly flower, borne on base leaf axils,-shaped like roots borne, corolla pink or purplish red; legume ovate or ovate-shaped oblong, slightly inflated. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert stony dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaflet 5–13, linear, covered with appressed appressed hairs; peduncle than leaves long 2–3 times, raceme appearing head-shaped, flower 8–20 flowers, arranged compact, corolla pale red purple; legume linear-shaped, robust straight. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Fisch. ex Bong. & C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shifting sands.
Morphology: Rachis hard, persistent, spiny-shaped, leaflet 3–5, linear or linear-lanceolate; raceme borne 5–6 flower, calyx tubular campanulate, corolla purplish red; legume ovate orbicular. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July.

Author: Fisch.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil, dry valley or dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; leaflet 9–25, upper surface glabrous, stipule free; raceme borne 3–7 flower, corolla purplish red; legume abaxial adaxial compressed, inflated, narrow oblong. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Pall.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil, Gobi desert oases, salt-pond margins or saline meadow.
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white T-shaped hairs; leaflet 11–21, obovate; raceme often relatively leaves long, corolla bright red; legume inflated, valves membranous. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Litv.) Vassilcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile or half fixed sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves reduced, scale-like; flowers solitary leaf axils, in branches on forming long raceme, flower purple; legume slightly inflated, ovate orbicular. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in northern China, Northeast China part and Northwest China part; northern Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil or seashore sandy land.
Life Form: One, biennial or perennial herb, geophyte
Morphology: Leaflet obovate or obcordate, long 5–20 mm; inflorescence small head-shaped, flower long 2–2.2 mm, corolla yellow; legume reniform, long 3 mm, wide 2 mm. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 20; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy dry slopes, sandy dry steppe or riverbanks sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate-shaped trifoliolate leaves, leaflet obovate to linear-shaped oblanceolate, with acute serrate, upper surface glabrous, lower surface covered with appressed hairs; inflorescence short racemose, corolla yellow; legume falcate, long 10–15 mm. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Prodán) O.Schwartz & Klink.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Eastern Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or dry steppe.
Morphology: Stem erect, densely covered with yellow soft hairs; leaflet linear, apex acute or truncate, leaf margin only with 2–3 serrate, leaves upper surface covered with sparse appressed hairs, lower surface covered with dense hairs, lateral veins 5–7 pairs; legume ting straight. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, throughout China cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland, fields or wetland.
Morphology: Leaflet obovate, long 7–20 mm; inflorescence head-shaped umbel shape, flower long 3–4 mm, corolla yellow; legume spiral zhuan curved forming disc shape, with spines spiny or tubercles projecting. Flowering period March–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: Boiss. & Blanche
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks or slopes alkaline -like sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Pinnate-shaped trifoliolate leaves; inflorescence axillary, peduncle not well developed, long not to 5 mm, flower 4–6 flowers, corolla yellow, style very short; legume linear-shaped cylindrical, long 15–20 mm, arcuate shape curved. Flowering period March–June, fruiting period May–July.

Author: Forssk.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains or slopes alkaline -like sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Peduncle well developed, long 10–18 mm. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.) Ser.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China Northeast China, North China and Northwest China cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in fields or wasteland.
Morphology: Leaflet long 15–35 mm, wide 4–15 mm; raceme dense appearing head-shaped, peduncle long about 6 cm, corolla blue; legume ovate orbicular, long 2.5–5 mm, jing about 2.5 mm, apex with long pointed beak. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Waldst. & Kit.) Desf.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China, northern part and Northeast China part; northern Asia, western part and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slightly salinized low wetland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Pinnate-shaped trifoliolate leaves, stipule lanceolate, base half hastate, with 2–3 pointed teeth or notched lobed; leaflet margin each lateral each with acute teeth 15–20; raceme long 3–5 cm, flower yellow; legume adaxial feng appearing conspicuous keel-shaped enlarged thick. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 502; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Medik.
Distribution: Throughout China wild borne or cultivated; Asia, Europe and Africa widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, slopes, river floodplains or moist sandy land.
Morphology: Stipule pointed spiny-shaped, very long (6–10 mm), entire, leaflet margin sparsely covered shallow serrate; raceme long 9–20 cm, flower white; legume apex acute pointed. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–September.
Uses: Forage, fiber, soil and water conservation
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Pall.
Distribution: Native to western Asia and Europe, throughout China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, riverbanks, wasteland, sandy grassland or forest margins.
Morphology: Stipule falcate-shaped linear, long 3–5 mm, leaflet margin with not regular sparse shallow teeth; raceme long 6–15 cm, flower yellow; legume ovate, apex obtuse round. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period June–October.
Uses: Forage, fiber, aromatic use, ecological use

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, throughout China introduced.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Stem creeping trailing; palmate-shaped trifoliolate leaves, petiole long 10–30 cm; peduncle long 6–20 cm, inflorescence globose, terminal, flower long 7–12 mm, white, calyx teeth than calyx tube short, veins striations 10 strips; legume oblong. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Europe, China Northeast China and worldwide widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, forest margins or riversides grassland.
Morphology: Stem erect or on ascending, not in at nodes borne roots; peduncle long about 5 cm, inflorescence globose, flower long 7–9 mm, calyx teeth than calyx tube long or etc. Long, veins striations 5 strips, corolla pale red to white. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Asia middle part and Europe, eastern China, northern part and Northeast China part cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized wet meadow or wasteland.
Morphology: Petiole long 5–10 cm; leaflet wide 5–15 mm; inflorescence half globose to ovate, corolla pale red or yellow, calyx on lip in fruiting period swollen large forming bladder-like sac-shaped, on square 2 calyx teeth than lower square 3 teeth slightly long; legume enclosing on su calyx inner. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 34, 503; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Hebei, Shandong and Zhejiang; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores.
Morphology: Stem wo appressed, apex ascending; stipule sagittate, rachis tip end with rolled xu, leaflet 3–5 pairs, long elliptic or long obovate, with pinnate-shaped tun; raceme, flower 2–5 flowers, purple; legume long about 5 cm. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in northeastern China, Northwest China part and Southwest China part; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe moist sandy soil or wet meadow.
Morphology: Stem climbing, winged; stipule half sagittate, rachis apex rolled xu divided forked, leaflet 2–4 pairs, linear or linear-lanceolate, herbaceous, veins usually in lower surface projections; flower 2–5 flowers, purple; legume long 3–4 cm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Taiwan; widely distributed on tropical coasts worldwide.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land rock crevices.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Young branches dense hairy; simple leaves, alternate, entire, veins inconspicuous; cyme axillary, bract persistent, leaves-shaped, flower two -like, 5 base several, petals yellow; fruit drupe-shaped, 3–5 clustered borne, subglobose, covered with persistent calyx enclosing. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Moench
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, Beijing and elsewhere with cultivated; Europe, North Africa and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland, stream sides grass tuft or forest margins.
Morphology: Rhizome coarse robust; odd-pinnate leaves, apex leaflet large, lateral small leaf blade irregular divided, unequal large, both surfaces green, lower surface along veins covered with sparse pubescent; panicle, flower dense, white or pale pink. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Michaut ex Juss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Iran and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand stones dry slopes or lakeshores sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with slender spiny; simple leaves, elliptic or oblong, long 1–2 cm, with serrate, without stipule; flowers solitary, diameter 2–2.5 cm, yellow; fruit subglobose, sepals persistent. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Lindl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes.
Morphology: Branchlets with spreading borne prickles; pinnate leaves, leaflet 7–13, broadly ovate or suborbicular, with round crenate-serrate; flowers solitary leaf axils, yellow, sepals lanceolate, entire; fruit ovoid, flower after sepals reflexed. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Retz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert spring watersides, dry slopes or river valleys sides.
Morphology: Prickles curved, falcate-shaped; leaflet 7-9, long 1.5-4 cm, lower surface glabrous or with short-pubescent, stipule large part appressed borne on petiole, persistent, with prickles; flower several flowers, petals white, pedicel with gland; fruit ovoid, sepals erect persistent. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Yu.A.Kotukhov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry river valleys saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground; palmately compound leaves, small leaf blade 5, margin deeply lobed, abaxial surface silvery white; inflorescence terminal, flower sparse, petals yellow. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Th.Wolf
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Mongolia, Central Asia and xi the Himalayas also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry river valleys saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground; pinnate leaves, small leaf blade 7, base one pairs most small, toward on zhu gradually becoming large, margin deeply lobed, abaxial surface silvery white; inflorescence several from basal leaves tuft chou protruding, flower several flowers, arranged sparse, petals yellow. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid gravelly gray calcareous soil, river floodplains or shaded wet grassland.
Morphology: Plant much-branched, entire plant densely covered with gray soft hairs-shaped pubescent; pinnate leaves, leaflet 5–8 pairs, alternate like covering wa-shaped die gai, apex often 2-lobed; umbel locule-shaped cyme, flower diameter 6–8 mm, style clavate shape; achene with veins striations. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; world bei temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, riverbanks sandy land or salinized and alkalized wet grassland.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, small leaf blade oblong, both surfaces green; single flower lateral or terminal, pedicel long 0.8–1.5 cm, flower diameter 0.6–0.8 cm, petals yellow, style conical-shaped; achene oblong. Flowering and fruiting period March–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy soil, yellow earthen slopes or mostly gravel dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground borne long, entire plant covered with stellate hairs; palmate-shaped 3 protruding compound leaves, small leaf blade on half part margin with numerous serrate; flower 1–2 flowers, diameter 10–15 mm, petals yellow, style ding-shaped; achene nearly reniform. Flowering and fruiting period April–August.

Author: (L.) Rydb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or plateaus sandy wet meadow.
Morphology: Plant creeping spreading ground, sometimes with tuberous roots; pinnate leaves among broken, leaflet 6–11 pairs, upper surface green, lower surface densely covered with silvery white silky hairs; single flowers axillary, with long pedicel, flower diameter 1.5–2 cm, yellow, style lateral appearing short branches-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or sandy desert.
Morphology: Compound leaves with 3 or 5 small leaf blade, long 2 mm, wide about 0.5 mm, entire, petiole hard, persistent forming spiny-shaped; flowers solitary leaf axils, petals 3, white or pale pink; achene oblong, outer with persistent calyx tube. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Bunge) Juz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, dry slopes, gravelly ground or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Plant spreading ground borne long; pinnate leaves, leaflet 3 or 4, abaxial surface covered with silky-shaped strigose hairs, terminal leaflet with 2–3 teeth; single flower or cyme, flower 5 base several, diameter 0.6–1 cm, yellow or white; achene nearly ovate. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Kurtto & T.Erikss.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky slopes, plateaus steppe or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Pinnate leaves, leaflet 5–8 pairs, small leaf blade apex often 2-lobed, both surfaces green, appressed borne sparse pubescent; nearly umbel locule-shaped cyme, flower yellow; achene smooth. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Batalin
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, dry valley or yellow soil hilly ground.
Morphology: Branches spiny subulate; leaves alternate or tufted, oblong, entire, glabrous; flowers solitary or 2–3 flowers clustered, 5 base several, with short pedicel, flower white; drupe globose, stone flattened ovate orbicular, with grooves striations. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Thunb.) Makino
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and East China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes or valleys open woodland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, ovate, with pointed acute zhong serrate; single flowers terminal, flower diameter 3–5 cm, petals 4, white; drupe 1–4, black or brown, smooth. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or dry steppe sandy soil.
Morphology: Branchlets cylindrical; leaf blade flabellate or obovate, entire or apex 3–5 shallow round lobed, lower surface gray-green, densely covered with short-pubescent; umbel without peduncle, flower white; follicle covered with short-pubescent. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Rousi) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, yellow soil hills or watersides sand stones ground.
Life Form: Deciduous shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Spines spiny terminal or lateral; leaves often nearly opposite, narrowly lanceolate, long 30–80 mm, lower surface silvery white or pale white scale; fruit subglobose, diameter 4–6 mm, fruiting pedicel long 1–2.5 mm. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period September–October.

Author: Rousi
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains.
Morphology: Branchlets gray or brown, lateral spines spiny longer and slender slender, often unbranched; leaves alternate, long 4–6 cm, middle part above most wide; fruit suborbicular, long 6–9 mm, fruiting pedicel long 1–3.5 mm. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period September–October.

Author: Rousi
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river-valley terraces, dry slopes or floodplains.
Morphology: Branchlets biao surface white, guang bright, spiny relatively mostly and branched; simple leaves alternate, linear, long 15–45 mm, both surfaces silvery white, without rust-colored colored scale; fruit broad elliptic, long 5–9 mm, fruiting pedicel long 3–4 mm. Flowering period May, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Rousi) Y.S.Lian
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky gravelly riverbanks terraces or dry riverbeds.
Morphology: Branchlets gray or brown; leaves alternate, narrowly lanceolate, long 30–55 mm, lower surface grayish white, mostly few covered with white soft hairs; fruit elliptic, long 5–7 mm, fruiting pedicel long about 1 mm. Fruiting period September–November.

Author: Y.S.Lian, & al.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Sichuan.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks or terraces.
Life Form: Deciduous shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves alternate or opposite, lower surface white, only with scale; fruit cylindrical, long 6–10 mm, not curved, with 3–5 slender ribs. Fruiting period September–October.

Author: S.W.Liu & T.N.He
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Sichuan and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys, terraces or floodplains.
Life Form: Deciduous shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves alternate, long 2–8 cm, young when both surfaces covered with silvery white scale and stellate hairs; fruit cylindrical, curved, brown, densely covered with silvery white or pale white scale, with 5–7 longitudinal ribs, apex depressed, long 6–8 mm. Fruiting period September–October.

Author: Schltdl.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Tibet; Bhutan, Nepal and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sand stones ground.
Morphology: Spines spiny terminal; 3 leaves whorled or 2 leaves opposite, long 1–2.5 cm, lower surface densely covered with grayish white and brown scale; fruit elliptic or subglobose, long 8–12 mm, mostly sap, fruit stalk long 1–2 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, plains sandy soil or desert.
Morphology: Branches head without spiny or with spiny; leaves alternate, lanceolate, entire, lower surface grayish white, densely covered with white scale, lustrous; flowers clustered leaf axils, silvery white, disc apex usually glabrous, calyx tube campanulate; fruit relatively large, elliptic, long 12–26 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period September.
Uses: Medicinal, edible, oilseed, aromatic use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, timber, fiber, gum, sweetener
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, plains sandy soil or desert.
Morphology: Branches head without spiny or with spiny; leaves alternate, lanceolate, entire, lower surface grayish white, densely covered with white scale, lustrous; flowers clustered leaf axils, silvery white, disc apex usually glabrous, calyx tube campanulate; fruit relatively large, elliptic, long 12–26 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period September.
Uses: Medicinal, edible, oilseed, aromatic use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, timber, fiber, gum, sweetener

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, plains sandy soil or desert.
Morphology: Branches head without spiny or with spiny; leaves alternate, lanceolate, entire, lower surface grayish white, densely covered with white scale, lustrous; flowers clustered leaf axils, silvery white, disc apex usually glabrous, calyx tube campanulate; fruit relatively large, elliptic, long 12–26 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period September.
Uses: Medicinal, edible, oilseed, aromatic use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, timber, fiber, gum, sweetener

Author: Schltdl.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi desert sandy beach, sand dunes low-lying ground or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Branches with conspicuous spines spiny; disc apex hairy, calyx tube funnel-shaped or campanulate; fruit relatively small, ovate orbicular or suborbicular, long 8–10 mm, milky yellow or orange yellow. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period September–October.
Uses: Medicinal, edible, oilseed, aromatic use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, timber, fiber, gum, sweetener

Author: (L.) Brongn.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, nearly membranous or thin papery, ovate, margin with coarse round teeth, glabrous; axillary cyme, flower yellow, 5 base several; capsule-shaped drupe, subglobose, persistent calyx tube surrounding fruit base, fruiting pedicel long 4–6 mm. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period September–December.

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, North China and East China; Korea, Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land, yellow soil hills or rock crevices.
Morphology: Branches often with flattened plant shuan wings; leaves large, ovate orbicular, thickly leathery, both surfaces very rough; flowers small, clustered, purplish red, xian leaves kai fang; samara large, wide up to 3 cm, dense hairy, fruit stone parts position on samara middle part. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe band sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves small, nearly elliptic round-shaped ovate, papery; samara suborbicular, diameter 1.2–2 cm, nearly glabrous, fruit stone parts position on samara middle part. Flowering and fruiting period March–June.

Author: Franch.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, dry valley or sandy soil.
Morphology: Tree surface shallow longitudinal lobed; leaves small, ovate to elliptic-lanceolate, long 2.5–5 cm, both surfaces smooth glabrous; samara elliptic, wide 1.5–2 cm, nearly glabrous, fruit stone position on samara in upper part. Flowering and fruiting period March–May.

Author: (W.C.Shieh) J.C.Liao
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore rock crevices.
Morphology: Plant with white milky sap; branches with ring-shaped stipule hen; leaves alternate, broadly elliptic, thickly leathery, entire, smooth, lustrous; rong fruit borne on leaf axils, oblate. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Oceania, East China and South China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Tree surface inner surface bright red; branchlets slender slender, diameter 0.8–0.9 mm, soft, easily chou separate broken nodes; scale-like leaves each whorls usually 7 (6–8) in number, pale green, nearly transparent, tight appressed; infructescence spherical fruit-shaped, elliptic, long 1.5–2.5 cm. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period July–October.

Author: Miq.
Distribution: Native to Australia, East China and South China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Inner surface pale red; branchlets diameter 0.5–0.7 mm, slightly stiff, not easily chou separate broken nodes; scale-like leaves each whorls usually 8 (9–10) in number, upper part brown, not transparent; infructescence long 7–12 mm. Flowering period April, fruiting period June–September.
