Habitat: Occurs in desert zone moist saline-alkaline land, floodplains or oases saline soil ground.
Morphology: Branchlets slightly tight close to; leaves narrowly ovate, long 1–6 mm; raceme slender and long, long 4–12 cm, wide 2–3 mm, each other tight close to, composed of compact panicle, flower 5 several, small, flower after petals entire deciduous. Flowering period June–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert zone moist saline-alkaline land, sand dunes margin or floodplains.
Life Form: Deciduous shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Young young branches leaves slightly with papillae-shaped hairs; leaves ovate, long 1–1.5 mm; raceme outer inclined, long 4–7 cm, flower dense, 1 cm inner with flower 22 flowers, clustered forming spreading panicle, flower 5 several, flower after petals parts deciduous. Flowering period June–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: P.Y.Zhang & M.T.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile dunes margin, sand dunes or riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Branches gai without papillae-shaped hairs; leaves ovate, long about 1 mm; raceme outer inclined, long 3–5 cm, flower sparse, 1 cm inner with flower about 5 flowers, clustered forming sparse panicle, flower 5 several, flower after petals parts deciduous. Flowering period June–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal

Author: (L.) H.Karst.
Distribution: Native to Southwest Asia and northern Africa, Taiwan Penghu and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Life Form: Deciduous tree or large shrub, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves reduced, in annual branchlets on fully entire amplexicaul appearing sheath appressed, long about 2 mm; raceme long 3–6 cm, rows forming panicle, flower several without stalk, 5 several, white or pale pink, filaments attached disc lobes among, petals flower after deciduous. Flowering period spring to summer.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal

Author: P.Y.Zhang & M.T.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in large desert hill among zhong saline-alkaline sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves reduced, amplexicaul appearing sheath-shaped, but margin not entire bao united; raceme long 3–5 cm, clustered forming terminal small type sparse spreading panicle, flowers small, 5 several, pale purple or purplish red, diameter not exceeding 4 mm, flower after petals persistent. Flowering period June–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: M.T.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in away from riverbeds or lake basins sand dunes.
Morphology: Leaves reduced, fully entire amplexicaul forming sheath, shi branchlets like divided nodes one yang; raceme long 7–15 cm, clustered forming terminal large type sparse spreading panicle, attached flower sparse, 1 cm inner only with flower 3 flowers, flowers large, 5 several, white, diameter 4–7 mm, flower after petals deciduous. Flowering period July–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation, medicinal

Author: (Royle) Ovcz. & Kinzik.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; India, Pakistan and Kashmir also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks, lakeshores sand gravel ground or river floodplains.
Morphology: Leaves relatively large, in branches on sparsely covered, lanceolate or oblong-lanceolate, long 5–15 mm, wide 2–3 mm, base attenuate contracted; raceme lateral, stamens filaments only in connate at the base. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia and Shaanxi.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sandy land, sand slope and mobile dunes among depressions.
Morphology: Leaves large, sparsely covered, spreading, broadly ovate or elliptic, long 7–12 mm, wide 3–8 mm, base expanded spreading appearing orbicular or wide cuneate, not amplexicaul; raceme lateral, stamens filaments 2/3 parts connate. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period July–August.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: Batalin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in coasts river floodplains sandy land mountain among basins low ground.
Morphology: Leaves large, sparsely covered, cordate or broadly ovate, long 5–18 mm, wide 6–7 mm, base expanded spreading appearing deep cordate, amplexicaul; raceme terminal, stamens filaments 1/2 parts connate. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: C.Marquand
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Nepal also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sandy land or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Leaves small, densely covered, long 1.5–3 mm, wide 0.5–1 mm; bract lanceolate, long 3–4 mm, flowers small, long not exceeding 5 mm, sepals long 1.5–2 mm, petals long 3–4.5 mm, pale purple. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: Desv.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; Nepal, India, Pakistan and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground river floodplains or lakeshores sandy land.
Morphology: Branches usually with surface membrane; leaves wide 0.5–2 mm; inflorescence lateral or several inflorescence clusters borne on branches axil, inflorescence base persistent with numerous imbricate scale, bract broadly ovate, wide 3–4 mm, sepals long 2–4 mm, petals long 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: P.Y.Zhang & Y.J.Zhang
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in gravel stony valleys, riverbeds sandy land or floodplains.
Morphology: Leaves densely covered, long 2–6 mm, wide 0.5–1 mm; with two zhong inflorescence, spring raceme lateral, summer to autumn borne terminal panicle, relatively sparse spreading, bract wide 3–3.5 mm, petals long 4–5 mm. Flowering period March–September, fruiting period May–October.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: Royle
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia to Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy floodplains, lakeshores sandy land or mountain front of alluvial fans.
Morphology: Only with 1 zhong inflorescence, raceme apex borne on current-year branches strips on, dense appearing spike-shaped, bract broadly ovate or elliptic, long 7–8 mm, wide 4–5 mm, sepals long about 4 mm, petals long 5–6 mm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: (L.) Desv.
Distribution: Native to Europe to Pakistan, China ou with introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves densely covered, linear, long 1.5–4.5 mm, densely covered with glands; raceme apex borne on current-year branches strips on, dense appearing spike-shaped, long 7–20 cm, flower long about 6 mm, petals narrow, pink or white slightly powdery red, stamens 10. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak and sand stabilization, soil and water conservation

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Shanxi, Henan, Jiangsu and Zhejiang.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, plains or sea island.
Morphology: Ground on stem slender weak, without ya scales; leaves broadly ovate or obovate, glabrous; inflorescence appearing head-shaped, with bract and small bracts, calyx tubular, corolla long 25–28 mm, tube part purplish red, lobes blue. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–October.

Author: Stapf ex Prain
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Sichuan, Tibet and Yunnan.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, hot and dry river valleys or rock crevices.
Morphology: Branches densely covered with grayish white or pale yellowish white long stiff hairs, ya scales small, scale-like; leaves on surface glabrous or slightly hairy, sometimes dense hairy; inflorescence small, corolla long 15–19 mm, tube part purple, corolla lobes blue. Flowering period July–October, fruiting period July–November.

Author: (Ledeb.) Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain ground or stream banks sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant often covered with slender small calcareous granules; branches with strips ridges, along ridges with sparse slender small prickles; leaves often auricles-shaped amplexicaul; spike, calyx green, in fruit with ji crown-shaped projections, corolla pale bluish purple. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Lam.
Distribution: Native to South Africa, China and worldwide widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Petiole base often with auricles; spike, rachis densely covered with grayish white to pale yellow-brown short soft hairs, calyx lower half part or 2/5 without gland, corolla pale blue to blue white, corolla tube long 3.2–3.4 cm. Flowering period June–September and December–April.

Author: (Girard) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Japan and Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves basal, lotus base-shaped; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped or round conical-shaped, spikelets containing 2–4 flower, calyx long 5–7 mm, funnel-shaped-shaped, along veins covered with long hairs, calyx rim white parts not to calyx middle part, kai zhang fu jing xian ran small on calyx long degrees, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period April–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Bunge) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes hills, steppe sandy land or seashore containing salt sandy land.
Morphology: Inflorescence tip ji branchlets nearly flattened, two ridges shape, spikelets containing 2–5 flower, calyx long 6–7 mm, diameter about 1 mm, calyx rim white (or pale purple red) colored parts reaching calyx middle part, kai zhang fu jing and calyx long degrees equal, corolla yellow. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ikonn.-Gal. ex Lincz.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sparse pine saline soil or salt becoming riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Roots surface dark red; basal leaves small, long 1–2 cm, wide 4–7 mm; stem numerous, two dichotomously branched; calyx long 5–8 mm, white, sometimes dry after becoming is pale yellow, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Debeaux) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Shandong and Jiangsu.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, nearly sea slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Inflorescence tip ji branchlets round column-shaped or slightly angular, spikelets containing 2–3 flower, calyx long 7–8 mm, diameter about 1.5 mm, corolla pale purple. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Hance) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast or islands rock crevices crevices.
Morphology: Leaves clustered on branches upper part, thickened, in ribs inconspicuous; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped, calyx long about 5 mm, funnel-shaped-shaped, tube part along veins covered with long hairs, calyx rim white, usually zhe die and slightly kai zhang, corolla pale purplish red. Flowering period August–September, fruiting period September–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (H.Hara) H.Hara
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast or islands rock crevices crevices.
Morphology: Corolla bright yellow, calyx rim band pale yellow. Flowering period August–September, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Rupr.) Ikonn.-Gal.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kyrgyzstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem 2–8, branched curved bent; leaves basal, covered with glaucous bloom; inflorescence axis round column-shaped, spikelets with 4–7 flower, calyx white to pale yellow, funnel-shaped-shaped, long 5–7 mm, dense hairy, lobes apex with awn; corolla yellow. Flowering period July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (L.) Chaz.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe.
Morphology: Inflorescence axis often solitary and forming of-shaped shape curved bent, spike in each one branches end clustered forming one compact subglobose compound inflorescence, calyx long 5–6 mm, calyx rim nearly white, lobes small and inconspicuous, corolla pale purplish red. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Ledeb.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly slopes or sand dunes.
Morphology: Stem often prostrate; inflorescence 5–25, base forked places often with conspicuous bract, spike compact, calyx long 3–4.5 mm, calyx rim white, pink or powdery purple, lobes acute, corolla pale yellow. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or mostly rocky barren wild.
Morphology: Petiole wide about 0.5–2 mm; spike short and sparse pine, calyx long 6.5–8 mm, calyx rim pink or pale purplish red, lobes apex acuminate, with persistent awn pointed, corolla yellow. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Turcz.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Ningxia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky desert or salinized floodplain flats.
Morphology: Roots surface po lobed forming brown colored fiber; stem base woody, fleshy large and with mostly head, with xu mostly white membranous ya scales; leaves small, long about 5–15 mm, wide 1–3.5 mm; first inner bracts densely covered with long hairs or becoming glabrous, calyx and corolla purplish red. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Rupr.) Ikonn.-Gal.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kyrgyzstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert stony dry slopes or foothills.
Morphology: Roots surface not po lobed; leaves long 1–2.5 cm, wide 1–6 mm; first inner bracts without or slightly covered with short hairs. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Danguy) Kamelin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Pakistan and Kashmir also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly desert or mostly rocky slopes in front of alluvial fans.
Morphology: Stem base woody branched apex with white membranous scale; leaves clusters clustered on branches end, small and often early diao; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped, inflorescence axis dichotomously branched, gray-green, calyx long 8.5–10.5 mm, calyx rim bright yellow, corolla orange yellow. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (L.) Chaz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in containing salt gravel flat, yellow earthen slopes or sandy soil ground.
Morphology: Stem mostly shi, base without woody branched and white scale; basal leaves long 1–4 cm, wide 0.5–1 cm; umbel locule-shaped panicle, inflorescence axis two forked branched and curved bent, calyx long 5–8 mm, lobes 5, golden yellow, petals orange yellow. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Y.H.Wu & Y.C.Yang
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai ma mostly.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized soil in.
Morphology: Inflorescence axis flower scape-shaped, dichotomously branched clustered in terminal, forming mostly flower round spherical-shaped capitulum, numerous capitulum clustered borne forming terminal corymb, calyx rim lobes is undulate and non- triangular, without awn pointed. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Lincz.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem few, single one or 2–3, erect, not curved bent, on half part dichotomously branched; calyx long 5–6 mm, calyx rim golden yellow, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Ikonn.-Gal.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Sichuan.
Habitat: Occurs in yellow soil slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Inflorescence axis on xian ran covered with clusters hairs or stellate hairs. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Wangerin) Kamelin
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in dry gravel flat.
Morphology: Inflorescence axis usually low dwarf, coarse robust, gray-green, lower part smooth, dichotomously branched on ascending appearing broom-shaped-shaped, calyx rim band liu yellow, calyx tube lower part not xian oblique. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Lincz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in stony dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem woody, coarse thick, with mostly head, densely covered with persistent petiole base; leaves basal and mostly; inflorescence sterile branches mostly and long, much-branched, spike nearly head-shaped, with 5–7 spikelets, e long about 1 cm, calyx rim ning meng yellow, corolla orange yellow. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Kar. & Kir.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly Gobi gravel desert or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Inflorescence appearing single terminal head-shaped clusters clusters, inflorescence axis not appearing dichotomously branched-shaped, sterile branches 2–5 clusters borne on inflorescence axis each nodes white membranous scale axil part, calyx long 9–12 mm, calyx rim bright yellow, corolla orange yellow. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Herder) Kamelin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly Gobi gravel desert or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Inflorescence axis without warts, sterile branches long 2–4 cm, kai zhang, outer bracts and first inner bracts herbaceous part densely covered with longer hairs and short hairs, calyx long 8.5–11 mm, lobes apex usually with mucronate. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Ikonn.-Gal.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil.
Morphology: Inflorescence round conical-shaped, inflorescence axis often solitary, branched relatively slender and long, spike terminal, first inner bracts densely covered with long hairs, calyx tubular, long 4.5–6 mm, calyx rim white purple or nearly white, corolla bluish purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (T.X.Peng) Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain base.
Morphology: Inflorescence round conical-shaped, inflorescence axis often solitary, branched relatively slender and long, spike terminal, first inner bracts glabrous or slightly hairy, calyx tubular, long 6.5–7 mm, calyx rim white purple or nearly white, corolla bluish purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Schrenk) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains saline soil ground or salinized sandy soil.
Morphology: Inflorescence main axis lower part 5–7 at nodes with amplexicaul leaves, deciduous after liu with ring hen; inflorescence round conical-shaped, calyx long about 2.2–2.5 mm, calyx rim white, corolla pale bluish purple. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Tausch) Lincz.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline flats margins sandy land or riverbanks terraces.
Morphology: Leaves often early diao, small, up to 3.5 cm, oblong-obovate; inflorescence in often with branched fan mostly green sterile branches, first inner bracts on half part is membranous, calyx long about 2.5–3 mm, calyx tube veins on and veins among covered with dense hairs, calyx rim white, corolla bluish purple. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Willd.) P.Fourn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores salinized and alkalized sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves often early diao, small; stem from base much-branched, upper part branched dense; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped, with sterile branches; calyx and corolla pale purple. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: (Schrenk) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized barren flats or lakeshores terraces.
Morphology: Leaves in flowering period persistent in, long 5 cm above, spatulate; calyx long 2.5–3 mm, calyx tube often zhi one lateral veins on hairy or fully entire glabrous, calyx rim white, corolla bluish purple. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Willd.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized wasteland or low-lying saline soil ground.
Morphology: Leaves wide large, long 10–30 cm, wide 3–8 cm; inflorescence in without or with bie simple single sterile branches, first inner bracts first inner bracts xian ran herbaceous, apex only with narrow membranous margin, calyx long 3–3.5 mm, calyx rim pale purple to white, corolla bluish purple. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 17, 500; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Afghanistan, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline soil or salinized soil.
Morphology: Leaves in branches on alternate or clusters clustered, thickened, petiole base expanded zhang forming with membranous margin and amplexicaul sheath, sheath end with 2 auricles-shaped membrane segments; calyx long 3–4 mm, calyx rim white, among borne lobes usually conspicuous, corolla pale purple to bluish purple. Flowering period August–October, fruiting period September–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Mill.) Willd.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, eastern China with introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in coast saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves basal, linear; flower scape slender slender, from leaves tuft in chou protruding, inflorescence head-shaped, flower white, pink or rose red. Flowering period May–June.

Author: (L.) Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, river floodplains terraces or plains sandy soil.
Morphology: Basal leaves long 2.5–6 cm, wide 1–3 cm, both surfaces covered with calcareous granules, petiole lower part with wide green margins band; inflorescence axis with narrow wings, spikelets arranged compact, outer bracts long about 7–8 mm, calyx rim lobes without teeth teeth, corolla purplish red. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (C.A.Mey.) Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry sand stones floodplain flats.
Morphology: Spike spikelets usually arranged sparse, outer bracts long 3–5 mm, not used as covering wa-shaped yan gai, corolla pale purplish red. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Gray
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Europe, Africa and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Plant sparsely covered pubescent or nearly glabrous; leaves lanceolate or elliptic, wide 1–2.5 cm, receptacle leaf sheath tube-shaped, margin hairs long 1–3 mm; raceme appearing spike-shaped, inflorescence stalk with glandular hairs, flower purplish red; achene paired lenticular-shaped. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period July–October.

Author: (L.) Delarbre
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or barren wild tidal wetland.
Morphology: Plant glabrous, nodes part swollen large; leaves lanceolate, upper surface often with one large blackish brown crescent-shaped spots dots, receptacle leaf sheath tube-shaped, without margin hairs; inflorescence stalk covered with glands, perianth pale red or white, often is 4 deeply lobed; achene broadly ovate, paired concave. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Spach
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; also found in southern Asia and Oceania.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or barren wild sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem coarse robust, densely covered with spreading villous; leaves large, long 10–20 cm, wide 5–12 cm, receptacle leaf sheath apex usually with green wings; raceme appearing spike-shaped, flower pale red or white; achene suborbicular, paired concave. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period August–October.

Author: (L.) Delarbre
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wetland, marshland or river floodplains.
Morphology: Stem glabrous, nodes part swollen large; leaves lanceolate, long 4–8 cm, with xin la wei, receptacle leaf sheath apex with short margin hairs; leaf axils with bi flower shou jing flower; perianth upper part white or pale red, covered with yellow-brown transparent glandular dots; achene paired lenticular-shaped or 3-ridged. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period June–October.

Author: (A.J.Li) Adr.Sanchez & Jan.M.Burke
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in dry river valleys margins sandy soil or gravel rocky slopes.
Morphology: Branched mostly, apex spiny-shaped; leaves small and clustered, oblong, nearly leathery; inflorescence racemose terminal, tepals yellowish green, unequal large, inner surface 3 relatively large, in fruit enlarged large, tight appressed fruit; achene broadly ovate, 3-ridged, along ridges borne wings. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.
Uses: Soil and water conservation, ecological use

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Old branches appearing " of "-shaped shape guai curved; leaves linear, long 3–6 mm; flowers clustered leaf axils, white; fruit with thin membrane appearing bladder-like-shaped fruit. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Schrenk) Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes, fixed dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Old branches colored pale, gray or pale yellow gray; fruit along ribs winged, 4 strips ribs each with 2 wings, wings nearly membranous, relatively soft, pale yellow or yellow-brown, with slender veins striations. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Pall.) Gürke
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes or mobile dunes.
Morphology: Old branches gray brown or band purple brown; perianth white; fruit along ribs winged, 4 strips ribs each with 2 wings, wings nearly membranous, relatively soft, usually surface smooth, entire, nearly entire or with slender teeth. Flowering period May, fruiting period June.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes, fixed dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Old branches dark red or purple brown; perianth pink or red; fruit along ribs winged, 4 strips ribs each with 2 wings, wings nearly leathery, relatively thick, textured stiff, ribbed striations, margin with teeth or entire. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Korovin ex Pavlov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Tajikistan and Turkmenistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes.
Morphology: Fruit center-shaped ovate or ovate orbicular, ribs projecting protruding and acute, winged, wings relatively soft, nearly membranous, margin regular, spiny relatively soft, unbranched or on end 2 forked branched, tip branches slender, spiny hairs-shaped. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May.

Author: I.G.Borshch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Iran also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Fruit subglobose forming subglobose, each ribs borne 2 wings, wings relatively stiff, wings margin not regular, borne spiny, spiny flattened, relatively stiff, dense dense, nearly middle part 2 secondary dichotomously branched, tip branches slender, extending spreading jiao zhi, yan hidden achene. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Pavlov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes.
Morphology: Tepals fruiting period water flat extending spreading; fruit small, broadly ovate, diameter small on 13 mm, achene apex guang bare without spiny, only achene base ribs on borne wings, wings stiff, leathery, narrow, spiny short on or etc. Long on achene wide, very sparse, tip branches very short appearing star-shaped-shaped. Flowering period June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: I.G.Borshch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes or semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Tepals in fruit reflexed; fruit deep brown, subglobose, diameter 14–20 mm, entire ribs winged, wings relatively stiff, narrow, wide 1–2 mm, spiny etc. Long or slightly long on achene wide, sparse, tip branches longer appearing needle-like spiny-shaped. Fruiting period June–July.

Author: Losinsk.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes.
Morphology: Tepals in fruit reflexed; fruit usually yellow or yellow-brown, few red brown, broadly ovate, diameter 12–20 mm, entire ribs winged, wings relatively stiff, narrow, wide 2–3 mm, spiny etc. Long or slightly long on achene wide, sparse, tip branches longer appearing needle-like spiny-shaped. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, sand gravelly desert or gravelly desert.
Morphology: Pedicel long 1–2 mm, lower part with joint; tepals ovate orbicular, in fruit water flat extending spreading; fruit broadly elliptic, diameter 7–11 mm, each ribs with spiny 1–3 rows, spiny slender weak, hairs developing-shaped, easily broken. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August, second secondary flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Drobow
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy land, sand gravelly desert or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Fruit broadly ovate, diameter 10–15 mm, achene oblong, not twisted or slightly twisted, ribs rounded-obtuse, wider, grooves deep, 2 rows spiny rows on fruit ribs margin, textured cui, easily broken. Fruiting period June–July.

Author: Losinsk.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Fruit large, broadly ovate, diameter 17–25 mm, achene long ovate, twisted, ribs very projections, each ribs borne spiny 2–3 rows, dense dense or relatively sparse, base slightly flattened. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Z.M.Mao
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Pedicel lower part with joint; fruit broadly ovate, diameter 8–18 mm, achene long ovate, twisted, ribs inconspicuous or rounded-obtuse, nearly without grooves or with shallow grooves, each ribs with spiny 3 rows, dense dense, relatively stiff, free, base not expanded large. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July, second secondary flowering and fruiting period August.

Author: N.A.Ivanova ex Soskov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes or sandy desert.
Morphology: Plant appearing pyramidal shape, old branches curved bent; tepals pale red; fruit diameter 1–2 cm, achene with 2–4 mm long beak, ribs and grooves usually inconspicuous, spiny sparse, slender slender, soft, fruit beak spiny fascicled-shaped. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–July, second secondary flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: B.R.Pan & K.M.Shen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Plant appearing pyramidal shape; old branches straight; fruit diameter 0.8–1 cm, achene without long beak, slightly twisted, ribs and grooves usually inconspicuous, spiny each ribs 2 rows, sparse, free, slender slender, soft. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July, second secondary flowering and fruiting period September–November.

Author: Z.M.Mao
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Pedicel long 2–4 mm, middle part with joint; tepals broadly elliptic, in fruit reflexed; fruit subglobose, diameter 10–12 mm, achene slightly twisted, ribs and grooves inconspicuous, each ribs borne spiny 2 rows. Flowering period June, fruiting period July.

Author: Litv.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Fruit large, ovate orbicular, diameter 10–20 mm, achene elliptic, each ribs borne spiny 2 rows, spiny base slightly flattened, free, sparse, relatively slender, textured cui, not yan hidden achene. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June, second secondary flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Ningxia and Xinjiang; Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Fruit subglobose, diameter 10–30 mm, achene elliptic, twisted, ribs projections, spiny each ribs 2 rows, very dense or relatively dense, extending spreading jiao zhi, mostly few yan hidden achene. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June, second secondary flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia and Gansu; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes rocky gravel ground or sandy grassland.
Morphology: Basal leaves 2–5, nearly leathery, ovate orbicular, long 10–20 cm, long slightly large on wide or nearly equal, both surfaces all smooth glabrous; panicle, pedicel joint in middle part below; fruit elliptic, long 12 mm, wide 8.5–9.5 mm. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Siev. ex Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, floodplains or sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant without stem; leaves basal, leathery, shen-shaped orbicular or suborbicular, long 6 14 cm, apex broad round, veins palmate-shaped, base protruding veins 3–5 strips; inflorescence several consisting of rhizome terminal protruding; fruit shen-shaped orbicular, red, wide large on long. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China, Central China and Southwest China; North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wasteland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Basal leaves oblong or oblong-lanceolate, base rounded or nearly cordate, margin undulate; flower two -like, inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, wide cordate, base deep cordate, margin nearly entire, with reticulate veins, entire or one part with small tubercles; achene with 3 acute ridges. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains or ditch banks wetland.
Morphology: Basal leaves lanceolate or narrowly lanceolate, base cuneate, margin wrinkled undulate; flower two -like, inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, broadly ovate, reticulate veins conspicuous, base nearly truncate, margin nearly entire, entire with small tubercles. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Basal leaves lanceolate or narrowly lanceolate, base cuneate, margin wrinkled undulate; flower two -like, inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, triangular, base truncate, margin with small teeth, teeth long 0.5–1 mm, entire with long ovate small tubercles. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China, East China and Central China; Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in field margins wasteland or ditch banks wetland.
Morphology: Basal leaves oblong-ovate, wide 6–15 cm, base cordate; flower two -like, inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, narrow triangular-ovate, base truncate, margin each lateral with 2–3 spiny-shaped teeth, teeth long 0.8–1.5 mm, usually 1 segments with small tubercles. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in ditch banks wetland or open wild wasteland.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 1.5–3 cm; pedicel in lower part with joint; inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, triangular-ovate, long 3.5–4 mm, apex acute, entire with small tubercles, margin each lateral with 2–4 spiny-shaped teeth, teeth long 1.5–2 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Fisch. ex Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan, Russia, Ukraine and Poland also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or riversides wetland.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 0.8–2 cm; pedicel base with joint; inner tepals in fruit enlarged large, triangular-ovate, long 2.5–3 mm, apex narrow acute, entire with small tubercles, each margins with 3–4 spiny-shaped teeth, teeth long 1–1.5 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Stokes
Distribution: Distributed in East China, Central China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wetland, waterside wetlands or slopes grassland.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 2–5 cm; inner tepals in fruit narrow triangular-ovate, wide 1.5–2 mm, entire with small tubercles, margin each lateral with 1 needle-like spiny, needle-like spiny long 3–4 mm, straight or slightly curved. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides wetland or moist fields wasteland.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 1–4 cm; inner tepals in fruit narrow triangular-ovate, wide about 1.5 mm, margin each lateral with 2–4 needle-like spiny, needle-like spiny long 2–2.5 mm, entire with oblong small tubercles. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Rchb.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides, lakeshores or wasteland moist places.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 0.7–1.5 cm; inner tepals ovate-shaped triangular, only 1 segments with small tubercles, with small tubercles tepals margin each lateral with 2–3 needle-like spiny, needle-like spiny long 4–5 mm, its ta 2 segments spiny-shaped teeth shorter. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: F.Schm. ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and East China; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in water grooves wetland or rivers lakes coasts.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves wide 0.3–1.2 cm; inner tepals in fruit triangular-ovate, entire with small tubercles, its in 1 segments margin each lateral with 2 needle-like spiny, spiny apex straight or slightly curved, long 3–4 mm, ling 2 segments margin each lateral with 2 small teeth. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: (Wall. ex Meisn.) T.M.Schust. & Reveal
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan, Yunnan and Tibet; India, Pakistan and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus desert dry slopes or valleys wetland.
Morphology: Stem branched not is forked-shaped; leaves broadly lanceolate or oblong-lanceolate, long 6–16 cm, wide 3–7 cm, base ji-shaped cordate or nearly truncate; panicle spreading, perianth 5 deeply lobed, white or pale red; achene ovate, 3-ridged. Flowering period August–September, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Laxm.) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains, salt-lake shores wetland or sandy saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long; leaves long elliptic or lanceolate, long 5–13 cm, wide 0.5–1.5 cm, base hastate or cuneate, entire; inflorescence round conical-shaped, flower two -like, perianth 5 deeply lobed, yellowish green; achene ovate, 3-ridged. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Meisn.) S.P.Hong
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia to Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains, salt-lake shores wetland or sandy saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves very narrow, linear, wide 1.5–2.5 mm; inflorescence relatively small. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, desert sandy land or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Woody branches apex forming spiny-shaped, current-year branches slender and erect; leaves alternate, gray-green or blue green, leathery, orbicular to broadly ovate; current-year branches and leaves without papillae; tepals 4, stamens 6, stigma 2; achene paired lenticular-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, poplar forest understory saline-alkaline land or alluvial fans.
Morphology: Woody branches with spiny, current-year branches strips short contracted; leaves clustered, gray-green to blue green, orbicular or broadly ovate, both surfaces all glabrous; tepals 4, pink, stamens 6, stigma 2; achene paired lenticular-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid sandy land.
Morphology: Woody branches apex spiny-shaped, current-year branches slender weak, densely covered papillae; leaves gray to gray-green, ovate or round-shaped elliptic, both surfaces all covered with papillae; tepals 4, stamens 6, stigma 2; achene paired lenticular-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Ledeb.) Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky dry slopes or desert.
Morphology: Woody branch apices unarmed; current-year branches, leaves and midrib papillose; leaves elliptic or broadly elliptic, green, leathery; 5 tepals, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: (M.Bieb.) Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia to Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid gravel sloping ground or floodplains.
Morphology: Woody branches apex spiny-shaped; leaves broadly elliptic or obovate, blue green or gray-green, apex rounded or emarginate; raceme short, long about 1.5 cm, flower arranged compact; 5 tepals, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged. Flowering period May–August.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky dry slopes or gravel wasteland.
Morphology: Branches apex with spiny; leaves elliptic or obovate, green, papery, long 1.5–2.5 cm, wide 1–1.3 cm, with conspicuous reticulate veins; pedicel long about 1 cm, joint position on in lower part; 5 tepals, pale yellow or yellow red, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Chang Y.Yang & Y.L.Han
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile sand dunes.
Morphology: Branches apex with spiny; leaves linear or lanceolate, mostly appearing falcate-shaped curved, long 1.5–3 cm, wide 0.5–3 mm, midrib projections; pedicel long 0.5–1 cm, joint position on in upper part; tepals pale green. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (L.) K.Koch
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly ground, Gobi gravel desert, floodplains or mountain ground.
Morphology: Branch apices unarmed; leaves blue green to gray-green, narrowly lanceolate to oblong, apex acuminate or obtuse; raceme long 4–6 cm, flower arranged sparse, 5 tepals, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: Y.L.Liu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, mostly rocky floodplains or mountain front of proluvial fans.
Morphology: Leaves and current-year branches densely covered papillae. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: (Regel) Krasn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river-valley terraces or rocky dry slopes.
Morphology: Branch apices unarmed, current-year branches slender and long, conspicuously projecting from the clump; leaves gray-green, oblong-elliptic or oblong-obovate, apex acuminate; inflorescence branched, pedicel joint below the middle, 5 tepals, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid steppe, Gobi gravel desert or dry slopes.
Morphology: Branches short coarse, curved guai, apex without spiny; leaves green, leathery, narrowly lanceolate to linear, wide 1–3 mm; raceme short, perianth 5 segments, outer whorls tepals 2, oblong-ovate, toward lower reflexed; achene smooth guang bright. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Borodina) Yurtseva
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Current-year branches densely covered with small projections; leaves alternate, oblong or elliptic, wide 3–4 mm; raceme terminal, 5 tepals, elliptic, 8 stamens, 3 stigmas; achene 3-ridged, smooth, lustrous. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Inner Mongolia, Hebei, Shaanxi and Ningxia.
Habitat: Occurs in arid sandy slopes or sandy land.
Morphology: Branch apices unarmed; leaves green, leathery, inverted spreading needle-like oblong or linear, wide 2–12 mm; outer whorls tepals oblong, in fruit toward lower reflexed; achene densely covered with granules-shaped small dots. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Losinsk.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile dunes or semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Branch apices unarmed; leaves green, leathery, oblong or elliptic, long 1.5–3.5 cm, wide 0.8–2 cm, with conspicuous reticulate veins; outer whorls tepals shen-shaped orbicular, in fruit spreading, not reflexed. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Uses: Windbreak and sand stabilization, ecological use

Author: Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Iran and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in barren wild.
Morphology: Stem short contracted, branched curved bent; young branches and leaves covered with papillae-shaped hairs; leaves pale green, oblanceolate to elliptic, wide 2–6 mm, receptacle leaf sheath apex lacerate lobed forming fringed; flower 1–3 flowers clustered leaf axils, nearly without stalk; achene ovate, 3-ridged, rough or with small dots, lustrous. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Stem prostrate or on ascending; leaves linear, wide 0.7–1.5 mm, green, apex with spiny-shaped pointed, receptacle leaf sheath hyaline-membranous, apex with filiform slender lobes; flowers solitary leaf axils, pale purplish red; achene enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Ivanova ex Borodina
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand gravelly desert or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves elliptic or oblong, wide 3–6 mm, leathery, gray-green, apex without spiny-shaped pointed, receptacle leaf sheath base brown, in upper part white membranous, 2-lobed; flower 1–2 flowers axillary, green white; achene enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Fu & Y.Z.Zhao
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or low mountain hills.
Morphology: Leaves orbicular or broadly elliptic, wide 1–1.3 cm, nearly leathery, apex roundeded-obtuse, receptacle leaf sheath oblique, apex pointed, brown; flower 2–3 flowers clustered; achene densely covered with granules-shaped small dots, slightly lustrous, enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Meisn.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravel slopes, river floodplains sand gravel ground or river valleys grassland.
Morphology: Roots woody becoming; stem prostrate; leaves elliptic, long 1–2 cm, wide 5–13 mm, petiole long 2–5 mm, receptacle leaf sheath hyaline-membranous, with brown veins; flower several bianborne on plant, green; achene lustrous, enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world bei temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Leaves large small not equal; receptacle leaf sheath lower part brown, upper part white; flower throughout on plant, pedicel apex with joint, perianth dehiscent to 2/3–3/4; achene densely covered with consisting of small dots composed of slender strips striations, dull, and persistent perianth nearly etc. Long or slightly exceeding. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Kom.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Jilin and Liaoning; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wasteland.
Morphology: Receptacle leaf sheath entire is brown; stamens 5; achene surface with spreading luan tubercles-shaped granules. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: F.Z.Li, Y.T.Hou & F.J.Lu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves long elliptic or oblanceolate, wide 2–5 mm; flower in branches upper part dense forming spike; achene two type, long type fruit exserted tepals outer, densely covered with small dots, dull, short type fruit and tepals nearly etc. Long, densely covered with forming rows small dots. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: F.Z.Li, Y.T.Hou & C.Y.Qu
Distribution: Distributed in Jilin.
Habitat: Occurs in barren wild or wetland.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; leaves elliptic round-shaped ovate, wide 2–4 mm, receptacle leaf sheath brown, upper part lacerate lobed; achene two type, short fruit type type enclosing on persistent perianth inner, covered with small dots, dull, long fruit type type long on persistent perianth, surface smooth, lustrous. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: C.Merck ex K.Koch
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Jilin and Liaoning; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wasteland or riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; leaves elliptic or oblanceolate, upper surface midrib conspicuous, lateral veins inconspicuous, receptacle leaf sheath with 3–4 veins; flower 2–5 flowers axillary, perianth dehiscent to 2/3; achene densely covered with small dots, slightly lustrous, slightly projecting protruding on perianth. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Boreau
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang; northern Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks sandy beach or forest openings grassland.
Morphology: Stem yang wo; leaves elliptic or oblanceolate, large small nearly equal, receptacle leaf sheath white membranous, with 5–7 veins; pedicel apex with joint, perianth dehiscent to 1/2; achene densely covered with small dots composed of slender strips striations, dull, enclosing on persistent perianth inner or slightly projecting protruding. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; Asia each ground and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains wetland, sandy land or rocky gravel ground.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; leaves narrow elliptic or oblanceolate, wide 2–4 mm, receptacle leaf sheath hyaline-membranous, white; pedicel middle part with joint; achene smooth, lustrous, enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period June–September.

Author: Jaub. & Spach
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, stony desert or river floodplains grassland.
Morphology: Stem coarse robust, with small projections; leaves linear or awl-shaped-shaped linear, wide 1–1.5 mm, slightly succulent, receptacle leaf sheath white transparent, without veins; perianth herbaceous, dehiscent to 1/3; achene 3-ridged, densely covered small dots, slightly lustrous. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: F.Z.Li, Y.T.Hou & F.J.Lu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves strips-shaped lanceolate, wide 2–3 mm, abaxial surface midrib and lateral veins very its raised, receptacle leaf sheath up to 1.1 cm, with 15–20 strips longitudinal veins, lower part brown, upper part white, lacerate lobed; flower clusters borne on branches upper part leaf axils, not forming raceme; achene with forming rows small dots. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: F.Z.Li, Y.T.Hou & F.J.Lu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in plains wasteland.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves strips-shaped lanceolate, wide 2–3 mm, abaxial surface midrib and lateral veins very its raised, receptacle leaf sheath up to 1.1 cm, with 15–20 strips longitudinal veins, upper part white, lacerate lobed; flower clusters borne on branches upper part leaf axils, not forming raceme; achene smooth, slightly with lustrous. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: M.Bieb.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Shandong and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves lanceolate or narrowly lanceolate, wide 3–8 mm, receptacle leaf sheath with 6–7 strips veins; flower attached borne on branches upper part leaf axils, composed of slender and long spike; achene densely covered with small dots, slightly lustrous. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 23, 498; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition (front matter alkaline knotweed Polygonum gracilius)

Author: Steud. ex Kunze
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Borne watersides or river valleys wetland.
Morphology: Stem erect, much-branched, appearing broom-shaped-shaped; leaves usually caducous, lanceolate or linear-lanceolate, wide 6–8 mm, receptacle leaf sheath with 6–8 strips veins; flower 1–3 flowers borne on stem branches upper part leaf axils, forming spike; achene smooth, lustrous. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Waldst. & Kit.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; eastern Europe to high jia suo regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, sandy land or fields.
Morphology: Stem erect, branched appearing broom-shaped-shaped; leaves often caducous, oblong, wide 1–4 mm, receptacle leaf sheath long 7–10 mm, strongly lacerate lobed; flower in stem branches apex forming without leaves raceme, pedicel short on perianth; achene smooth, lustrous, exserted persistent perianth outer. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains sandy land or dry slopes.

Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: M.Bieb.

Habitat: Occurs in field embankments or water ditch banks.
Morphology: Plant gray-green; leaves long round-shaped linear, wide 2.5–5 mm, margin with small projections, not recurved, receptacle leaf sheath silvery white, without veins; flower 3–7 flowers clusters borne on leaf axils; achene densely covered with small dots, slightly lustrous, enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Jaub. & Spach

Habitat: Occurs in salinized low ground or poplar forest understory.
Morphology: Stem creeping, blue or pale purplish red; leaves succulent, linear-shaped spatulate, wide 1–1.5 mm, receptacle leaf sheath apex with teeth or lacerate lobed; flower 4–6 flowers clustered leaf axils, pedicel long, exceeding perianth nearly 3 times, perianth deeply lobed several reaching base, anthers purple; achene lustrous. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb. ex Meisn.

Morphology: Plant green; leaves linear-lanceolate or lanceolate, wide 2–4 mm, margin without small projections, slightly recurved, receptacle leaf sheath silvery white, without veins; flower single borne on leaf axils; achene densely covered with small dots, slightly lustrous, enclosing on persistent perianth inner. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Skvortsov
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields wasteland or valleys wetland.
Morphology: Stem erect or on ascending; leaves elliptic or long elliptic, upper surface midrib, lateral veins jie conspicuous, receptacle leaf sheath with 5–9 veins; achene densely covered with small dots, slightly lustrous, apex with long pointed, projecting protruding on persistent perianth. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–August.

Flowering period May–July, fruiting period August.

Author: (Forssk.) Asch. & Schweinf.

Flowering period February–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: (L.) J.Presl & C.Presl
