Author: (Schrenk) Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or dry slopes.
Morphology: Tubercles-shaped stem base brown to dark brown, with dense soft hairs; when year branches with branched, with 8–12 internodes; leaves scale-like, without spiny-shaped pointed; wings broadly elliptic to suborbicular, slightly spreading; disc lobes linear. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Schrenk) Paulsen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, Gobi gravel desert or dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant often appearing half globose, stem base densely covered white villous; stem with branched, blue green; leaves awl-shaped-shaped or three angles-shaped, apex with spiny-shaped pointed; tepals in fruit not winged-shaped attached genus structure, disc lobes half orbicular; bao fruit subglobose, yellow or cheng yellow. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: (L.) R.Br. ex Wight
Distribution: Distributed in Guangxi and Hainan; Asia tropical regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores or open wild sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, obovate to linear, long 1.5–6.5 cm, glabrous; flower two -like, several flower forming cyme, again forming capitulum, filaments base connate forming cup-shaped, style filiform, stigma slightly 2-lobed; ovules or seeds 1, with accessory seed coat. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.f.) Bennet
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant from base branched, with white woolly hairs; leaves opposite or nearly whorled, awl-shaped-shaped acicular, wide not and 1 mm; spike terminal, tepals 4, spreading needle-like subulate; utricle apex transverse gai lobed, seeds ovate, dry when in zhong qi pairs surface with ke-shaped depressed. Flowering period April–August, fruiting period August–November.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: P.Beauv.
Distribution: Native to South America and Africa, China with introduced recorded.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant prostrate, glabrous; leaves opposite, sessile, oblong to ovate orbicular, succulent, apex obtuse or acute; inflorescence axillary, without stalk, nearly head-shaped, white to dao grass colored, tepals lanceolate, stamens 5, accessory reduced stamens lanceolate, margin fringed. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.

Author: (Mart.) Griseb.
Distribution: Native to South America, North China south of each ground widely naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides, fields or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant creeping, stem tubular in hollow; leaves opposite, oblong-obovate, apex acute or rounded-obtuse; capitulum globose, solitary leaf axils, with long peduncle, tepals oblong, white, accessory reduced stamens apex lobed forming narrow strips. Flowering period May–October.

Author: Hook.f. ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore rocks.
Morphology: Plant tufted, appressed wo, succulent, glabrous; leaves opposite, inverted ovate-shaped spatulate, wide 2–3 mm; small capitulum terminal, spherical-shaped or round column-shaped, flower two -like, filaments base connate forming cup-shaped, free parts not lobed, without reduced stamens, style very short, stigma 2-lobed. Flowering period May–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in arid gravelly ground.
Morphology: Plant covered with rolled curved short-pubescent; leaves alternate, awl-shaped-shaped, long 3–9 mm; flower two -like, bracteole membranous, 5 tepals, membranous, in fruit without attached genus structure, stamens 3, stigma 2, style very short. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, throughout China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Roots round conical-shaped to fusiform-shaped; leaves large dou basal, oblong, long 20–30 cm, with long petiole; flower 2–3 flowers clusters clustered, perianth lower part and ovary connate, connate parts in fruit enlarged thick and stiff becoming; embryo ring shape or nearly ring shape. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July.

Author: (L.) Moq.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or watersides sandy land.
Morphology: Plant young when densely covered with branched hairs; leaves lanceolate, apex needle-like spiny-shaped; inflorescence spike-shaped; fruit apex with fruit beak, fruit beak deeply lobed forming two flattened strips-shaped small beak, small beak apex outer side each with one small teeth projecting; seeds and pericarp free. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Lam.) Moq.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes.
Morphology: Fruit beak in middle part above forming two needle-like small beak, in fruit beak base outer li each with one conspicuous, flattened narrow three angles-shaped lateral wings, usually small beak relatively lateral wings long or equal long, fruit upper part margin without crown-shaped wings margin. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Fisch. & C.A.Mey. ex Fenzl
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Asia Southwest China part and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or river floodplains margins sandy land.
Morphology: Fruit beak only in its upper part divided forming 2 needle-like small beak, its middle part below outer side each with one conspicuous flattened narrow three angles-shaped lateral wings, usually small beak relatively two lateral wings short, fruit upper part margin with conspicuous crown-shaped wings margin. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Qinghai; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sand dunes or river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves wide, long elliptic or oblanceolate, long 1.2–4.5 cm, wide 0.5–1 cm, with 3 veins; spike round column-shaped; fruit disc-shaped-shaped, orbicular or suborbicular, glabrous, brown brown, with lustrous, fruit wings narrow, margin conspicuous toward adaxial surface reflexed. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Central Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant broom-shaped-shaped; leaves oblanceolate, 1 vein; spike slender, bract consisting of leaves-shaped zhu gradually guo du to lanceolate and ovate; fruit broadly elliptic, long 2–3 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm, fruit beak coarse short, fruit wings conspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Steph. ex Stev.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; also found in North Asia and north-central Asia.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy wasteland, fields or river floodplains.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide 2–3 mm, 1 vein; spike slender, long 5–15 cm, bracts narrow, wide 2–3 mm; fruit glabrous, obovate-oblong, long 3–4 mm, wide about 2 mm, fruit wings narrow or nearly absent. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia eastern part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy wasteland, fields or river floodplains.
Morphology: Fruit covered with stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Sukhor. & M.Zhang
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Ningxia.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves wide 3–5 mm; spike extending long, bract ovate-shaped, covering fruit; fruit ovate-shaped, long 2.7–3 mm, wide 1.6–1.8 mm, surface without stellate hairs, with wart-like projections, spreading borne papillae, apex rounded or acute, margin wingless. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Henan.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide about 1 mm; spike slightly coarse, bract wider, wide 2–3 mm; fruit long round-shaped broadly elliptic, long 4–5 mm, wide 2.5–3.5 mm, densely covered with stellate hairs, fruit wings wide, usually is stone wide 1/2. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Leaves oblanceolate or linear, wide 2–4 mm; spike long 4–18 cm, usually long 5–10 cm; fruit elliptic, long 2.5–3 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm, glabrous, fruit beak short, fruit wings narrow, not transparent, yellowish green, entire or teeth eroded-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy Gobi gravel desert, fixed dunes or sandy steppe.
Morphology: Spike long 3–6 cm; fruit relatively small, broadly elliptic, long 1.5–3 mm, wide 1–1.5 mm, abaxial side strongly projections, sometimes with bladder-like-shaped projections, glabrous, wings very narrow, several nearly wingless. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Xinjiang and Tibet; Afghanistan and Tajikistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide about 1 mm; spike long 1–6 cm; fruit inverted ovate-shaped broad elliptic, long 2–3 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm, abaxial surface projections its center slightly flattened, gan lan colored, with few black spots dots, glabrous, fruit wings very narrow. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Fruit densely covered with stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia and Pakistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas regions river valleys sandy beach or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant coarse robust, purplish red, sparsely branched; bract wide large, long 5–30 mm, wide 2–5 mm; fruit oblong-obovate, apex with conspicuous conical-shaped fruit beak, abaxial side with conspicuous tuberculate projections, fruit wings slightly wrinkled curved. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Sukhor.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; xi the Himalayas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy dry slopes.
Morphology: Fruit thick 0.6–0.8 mm, covered with stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet; Central Asia and xi the Himalayas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus floodplains or riversides sandy land.
Morphology: Plant relatively slender slender, green; bracts narrow; fruit broadly elliptic or long round-shaped broadly elliptic, apex acute or suborbicular, abaxial side projections, center flattened, smooth, yellowish green, fruit wings flat straight, not transparent, yan colored relatively fruit stone shallow. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores sand dunes, semi-fixed dunes or steppe.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide about 2 mm; spike slender cylindrical, long 4–5 cm, diameter 3–8 mm; fruit glabrous, oblong-obovate or broadly elliptic, long 2–4 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm, fruit wings conspicuous, not transparent, entire. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores sand dunes, semi-fixed dunes or steppe.
Morphology: Fruit both surfaces all covered with stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Henan.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Spike short, broadly elliptic or club-shaped-shaped, long 1–7 cm, short contracted, compact, bract consisting of leaves-shaped to lanceolate, with narrow membranous margin; fruit elliptic or broadly elliptic, long 4.5–6 mm, wide 3–3.5 mm, apex acute, base wide cuneate, covered with stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Inner Mongolia and Hebei.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-fixed dunes or riversides sandy beach.
Morphology: Spike round column-shaped or club-shaped-shaped, compact, long 1–25 cm, diameter 7–15 mm; fruit oblong-obovate or broadly elliptic, long 3–5 mm, wide 2–3.5 mm, apex orbicular, base suborbicular, hairy, wings conspicuous, not transparent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Moq.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Liaoning, Inner Mongolia and Hebei.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spike long 1–9 cm, diameter 8–10 mm, compact; fruit broadly elliptic, long 3.5–4 mm, wide 2.5–3 mm, apex orbicular, base usually cordate, glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spike cylindrical, long 1–4 cm, relatively slender, diameter about 4 mm, compact, bract consisting of lanceolate to ovate; fruit nearly ovate orbicular, long 2.5–3 mm, wide 2–2.5 mm, yellow-brown, guang bright, glabrous, fruit wings wider, thick, not transparent, entire. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Bunge ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang and Liaoning; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spike coarse robust, ovate-shaped to extending long club-shaped-shaped, long 1.5–20 cm, bract nearly herbaceous; fruit relatively large, broadly elliptic or wide elliptic round-shaped obovate, long 4.9–6 mm, wide 3.5–4.2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: W.Wang & P.Y.Fu
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in hills steppe sandy land.
Morphology: Spike club-shaped-shaped, slightly slender, diameter about 8 mm, bract nearly membranous; fruit oblong-obovate, long 3.5–4 mm, wide 2.5–3 mm, blackish brown, guang bright, wings strongly twisted, yan colored relatively fruit stone shallow. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, Inner Mongolia and Heilongjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes.
Morphology: Spike round column-shaped, compact, bract broadly ovate or suborbicular, with wide membranous margin, apex with long projecting pointed; fruit glabrous, inverted ovate-shaped or elliptic round-shaped, long 3–4 mm, wide 2–2.5 mm, apex emarginate, wings wider, entire, not transparent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: W.Wang & Fuh
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning, Heilongjiang and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes and riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Branched often used as of-shaped shape curved bent or curved; leaves linear; spike with dense flower, bract suborbicular, with wide membranous margins, stamens 5; fruit elliptic or nearly oblong, apex roundeded, beak inconspicuous, both surfaces covered with stellate hairs, margin with narrow wings. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Liaoning, Inner Mongolia, Hebei and Shandong.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides sandy land or sandy seashores.
Morphology: Spike cylindrical or club-shaped-shaped, compact, long 1–8 cm, diameter about 8 mm, bract with white membranous margin, stamens 1; fruit broadly elliptic or obovate-oblong, long 3.5–4 mm, wide 3–3.5 mm, fruit wings and fruit same colored, not twisted. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Kitag.) C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile dunes bottom, sand dunes or river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Spike inverted ovate-shaped or club-shaped-shaped, long 1–10 cm, diameter 1–1.5 cm, compact; fruit inverted ovate orbicular, long 3.7–4.5 mm, wide 2.9–4 mm, yellowish green, with few brown spots dots and bladder-like-shaped projections, wings shallow yellow, not transparent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Bunge ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Jilin and Liaoning; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land or fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spike club-shaped-shaped, long 1–8 cm, diameter 0.6–1 cm; fruit orbicular or suborbicular, long 3–4.5 mm, wide 3–4.3 mm, shallow yellow, with deep colored spots dots, fruit wings not transparent, yan colored relatively fruit stone shallow. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Bunge ex Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia and Northeast China; Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, fixed dunes or sand dunes margin.
Morphology: Spike round column-shaped, slender slender, sparse pine, long 3–11 cm, diameter about 6 mm; fruit oblong-elliptic, long 3.1–4 mm, wide 2.5–3 mm, apex notches shallow and wide, fruit wings narrower, wide 0.4–0.7 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Jilin, Liaoning, Inner Mongolia and Hebei.
Habitat: Occurs in fixed dunes, sandy seashores or sandy fields.
Morphology: Spike round column-shaped, slender slender, sparse pine, upper part flower slightly dense, bract wider, wide 1–1.5 mm; fruit suborbicular, long 4 mm, wide 3.5–4.5 mm, apex notches acute angles-shaped, fruit wings wide about 1 mm, textured thin, translucent. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Jilin, Liaoning and Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains or fixed dunes.
Morphology: Spike entire slender slender, with sparse flower, bracts narrow, wide 1–2 mm, conspicuous ground than fruit narrow; fruit suborbicular, long 4.5–5.5 mm, wide 3.9–5 mm, apex notches deep and narrow, fruit wings wide, textured thin, translucent. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus sandy land.
Morphology: Plant only with few branched; spike round column-shaped, upper part flower relatively dense, bract leaves-shaped, appearing falcate-shaped curved; fruit inverted ovate orbicular, long about 5 mm, wide about 4 mm, fruit wings wider, about 1 mm, and fruit stone same colored. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys sandy beach.
Morphology: Plant only with few branched; spike round column-shaped, bract leaves-shaped, appearing falcate-shaped curved; fruit oblong-obovate, long 3.5–4 mm, wide 2.5–3 mm, mo green, fruit wings narrow, wide about 0.5 mm, shallow yellow. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: C.P.Tsien & C.G.Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides sandy beach.
Morphology: Plant with numerous branched, dense; spike round column-shaped, relatively compact, bract and leaf blade with conspicuous zone bie; fruit glabrous, oblong-obovate, long 4–4.9 mm, wide 3–3.2 mm, abaxial side with few bladder-like-shaped projections. Fruiting period July–September.

Author: Sukhor. & M.Zhang
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in river basin sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves linear, wide reaching 2 mm; inflorescence very short, flower few, bract few short on fruit; fruit ovate-shaped, long 2.5–2.7 mm, wide 2 mm, glabrous, apex 2-lobed, margin wings conspicuous, wide 0.3–0.4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Spike round column-shaped or club-shaped-shaped; fruit both surfaces all covered with stellate hairs, ovate, long 4.5–5.5 mm, wide 3–4.5 mm, upper part acute, apex with deep and narrow notches, base nearly truncate or nearly center zang shape. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya, North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland, wasteland, river floodplains or fields.
Morphology: Plant erect, branched oblique spreading; leaves alternate, lanceolate, long 3–7 cm, abaxial surface densely covered with stellate hairs; male inflorescence spike-shaped, perianth lobes 3, stamens 3; female flowers several flowers gou forming compact two forked cyme; fruit long elliptic round-shaped obovate, apex crown-shaped attached genus structure 1, center emarginate. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Northeast China ya, Central Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains, slopes, sand dunes or fields.
Morphology: Leaf blade relatively small, long 0.5–3.5 cm, elliptic or ovate, both surfaces all densely covered with stellate hairs; fruit wide elliptic round-shaped obovate, lateral surface with same center round-shaped wrinkled striations, apex with 2 small three angles-shaped attached genus structure. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Sukhor.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Sichuan and Tibet; India, Nepal and Pakistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus sandy land.
Morphology: Stem and branched on on stellate hairs center branched very long, long 0.7–2 mm; perianth densely covered with pubescent; fruit two type, pericarp upper part attached genus structure all small or not conspicuous, black fruit pericarp surface wrinkled zhe, without radiate half same center round-shaped striations shi, brown fruit pericarp sometimes with rocky slender bao. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys, terraces, mostly rocky slopes or grass flat.
Morphology: Plant prostrate, densely covered with stellate hairs; leaf blade small, broadly elliptic or suborbicular, long 0.5–1 cm, both surfaces all covered with stellate hairs, petiole conspicuous, several and leaf blade etc. Long; male inflorescence head-shaped; fruit inverted ovate orbicular, with same center round-shaped wrinkled striations, apex attached genus structure 2, papillae-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, Gobi gravel desert, sandy land or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Plant covered with star-shaped-shaped or branched-shaped hairs; leaves alternate, strips-shaped lanceolate to needle-like spiny-shaped; flower single -like, monoecious, female flowers solitary leaf axils, 2 bract connate several reaching apex, apex two lateral each with 1 spiny-shaped attached genus structure; utricle obovate or cuneate, flattened. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: (L.) Gueldenst.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Tibet; western Asia, North Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, desert, half desert, dry slopes or steppe.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, flattened, oblong-ovate to ovate orbicular, base cuneate or orbicular, 1 vein; flower single -like same plant, male inflorescence spike-shaped, female flowers sessile, 1–2 flowers axillary, female flowers tube lobes angles-shaped, tube outer covered with four bundles long hairs, lobes relatively large. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Uses: Forage, windbreak and sand stabilization, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 489; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Gang Yang
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert mountain ground.
Morphology: Leaves narrow oblong, narrowly lanceolate, long about 8–10 mm, wide about 1 mm, margin revolute, base cuneate; female flowers tube lobes etc. Long on tube part 1/3, male spike borne on branches end, conspicuous with pedicel, covered with short soft hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Zhu Li bis
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe.
Morphology: Lateral branches short, nearly water flat spreading; leaves elliptic or ovate-shaped oblong, long about 2 cm, wide about 5–7 mm, lateral branches leaves narrow; female flowers bract very long, densely covered with gray soft hairs; utricle broad ovate, lobes short, in lower part attached 4 bundles white hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Stschegl. ex Losinsk.) Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Kazakhstan and north-central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy land, half desert or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves ovate or ovate-shaped oblong, long 2–3.5 cm, wide 1–2 cm, base cordate, with conspicuous pinnate-shaped veins; male inflorescence slender and long and soft, female flowers tube elliptic, flower tube lobes is tube long 1/5–1/6, in fruit tube outer only with four bundles long hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Uses: Forage, windbreak and sand stabilization, ecological use

Author: (Losinsk.) Czerep.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate, long 2–7 cm, wide about 7–15 mm, base cuneate or orbicular, with conspicuous pinnate-shaped veins; female flowers tube nearly obovate, flower tube lobes is tube long 1/4–1/5, in fruit tube outer in middle part above with four bundles long hairs, its lower with short hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Uses: Forage, windbreak and sand stabilization, ecological use

Author: (Losinsk.) Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; pa m er eastern part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas desert slopes or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Plant compact cushion-forming, branched short and dense; leaves small, dense, long about 1 cm, wide about 3 mm, margin toward abaxial side rolled bent, petiole longer, boat-shaped-shaped, after period persistent; male inflorescence short and compact, head-shaped, female flowers tube lobes tu auricles-shaped, tube outer usually covered with short hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: A.L.Fu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky desert dry slopes.
Morphology: Erect shrub, much-branched; leaves oblanceolate or narrow elliptic, long 4–7 mm, wide 1–2 mm, flattened, non- boat-shaped-shaped, petiole short, after period deciduous; female perianth tube very short in middle part below connate, outer surface covered with white bundles-shaped villous. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Moq.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and Europe southeastern part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil, floodplains or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Plant without glands, without qi wei, qiu after often band purplish red; leaves linear to narrowly lanceolate, entire; compound two forked type cyme borne on branches end and leaf axils, most tip end branched needle-like spiny-shaped; flowers small, two -like. Flowering period August–September, fruiting period October.

Author: (Schult.) Mosyakin & Clemants
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Southwest Asia, Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sand gravel ground, floodplains, grassland or fields.
Morphology: Plant with strongly qi wei, same when with nodes short-pubescent and yellow sessile granules-shaped glands; leaves oblong, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed to deeply pinnatifid; compound two forked cyme axillary, perianth lobes abaxial surface with longitudinal raised keel, in fruit spreading. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (L.) Mosyakin & Clemants
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Asia Southwest China part, North Africa and southern Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys, riverbanks terraces or sand soil wasteland.
Morphology: Plant with head-shaped glandular hairs and strongly qi wei; leaves oblong, margin deeply pinnatifid; compound two forked type cyme axillary, flower two -like, perianth lobes abaxial surface without longitudinal raised keel, in fruit erect and not spreading. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (R.Br.) Mosyakin & Clemants
Distribution: Native to Australia, Beijing, Henan, Shandong, Shaanxi and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks sandy land, wasteland, fields or saline-alkaline sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant spreading spreading or prostrate, densely covered with nodes pubescent; leaves oblong-elliptic or ovate-elliptic, lower surface densely covered yellow gland grains, margin with coarse teeth or shallowly lobed; clusters clustered cyme axillary, flower subsessile, tepals erect, erect, connate at the base, in fruit boat-shaped-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: (L.) Mosyakin & Clemants
Distribution: Native to the Americas tropical regions, East China, South China and Southwest China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or riverbanks sandy land.
Morphology: Plant with strongly xiang wei, stem with pubescent; leaf blade oblong-lanceolate to lanceolate, margin with not regular large serrate, lower surface with yellow glandular dots; clusters clustered cyme axillary, perianth lobes green, in fruit usually bi united; utricle oblate. Flowering and fruiting period nearly entire year.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia, Asia Southwest China part, Europe and North Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes wetland, forest margins or gullies and valleys.
Morphology: Stem branches smooth; leaves three angles-shaped narrowly ovate, without powdery or slightly with powdery, margin with not regular teeth; glomerule axillary, spherical-shaped or sang shen-shaped, perianth usually 3 deeply lobed, shallow green, fruit mature after becoming is thickened mostly sap and appearing red. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (L.) S.Fuentes, Uotila & Borsch
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Asia Southwest China part, North Africa, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant usually without powdery; leaves slightly thickened, wide triangular, wide slightly large on long, margin with round notched-shaped serrate or several entire; glomerule again clustered forming spike-shaped round conical-shaped inflorescence, perianth lobes large mostly connate to nearly apex, thickened. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 485; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: (L.) S.Fuentes, Uotila & Borsch
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline flats, watersides slightly saline-alkaline wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Plant without powdery, stem erect; leaves ovate to rhombic-shaped ovate, succulent, both surfaces all is shallow green or sometimes band red, margin serrate-shaped shallowly lobed; clusters clustered inflorescence arranged forming spike-shaped panicle, perianth lobes 3–4, only connate at the base. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 485; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) S.Fuentes, Uotila & Borsch
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores saline-alkaline land or watersides tidal wetland.
Morphology: Plant with powdery, stem prostrate or outer inclined; leaves oblong-ovate to lanceolate, thickened, margin with notches-shaped teeth, lower surface grayish white; glomerule arranged forming among broken spike-shaped or round conical-shaped inflorescence, perianth lobes 3–4, only connate at the base; utricle apex exposed protruding on perianth outer. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 484; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) S.Fuentes, Uotila & Borsch
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and North Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or fields.
Morphology: Plant without powdery, stem erect and coarse robust; leaf blade triangular, long 3–8 cm, wide degrees and long degrees equal or relatively small, slightly thickened, margin with not regular serrate; flower few clusters clustered and forming spike-shaped panicle, perianth lobes 5; seeds transversely borne. Flowering period August–September, fruiting period October.

Author: (Trautv.) Sukhor. & Uotila
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, waterside wetlands, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Leaf blade rhombic to rhombic-shaped ovate, lower part leaf blade up to 15 cm, nearly base 1 pairs serrate relatively large appearing lobes-shaped; inflorescence by terminal spike-shaped panicle is main, flower clusters consisting of numerous flower dense and forming, perianth lobes 3–5, narrow obovate, perianth base narrow slender appearing stalk-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) S.Fuentes, Uotila & Borsch
Distribution: Native to Europe and West Asia, China from Northeast to Southwest China and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy wasteland, slopes shrubland, forest margins or ditch margins.
Morphology: Plant plant height large, mostly few with powdery; leaf blade wide large, long 6–15 cm, wide 5–13 cm, margin palmate-shaped shallowly lobed; round conical-shaped inflorescence kai spreading, perianth lobes 5; seeds transversely borne, diameter usually 2–3 mm, surface with conspicuous orbicular deep jia or appearing concave convex not flat. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (H.W.Kung) Uotila
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China, Central China and South China; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy wasteland, valleys forest margins or river floodplains grassland.
Morphology: Plant slender shou; leaf blade relatively small, long 3–5 cm, wide 2–4 cm, nearly entire; spike slender and long, flowers small and arranged sparse, perianth 5 deeply lobed, lobes narrow obovate or linear; seeds transversely borne, surface with conspicuous wa dots. Flowering period July, fruiting period August.

Author: Hook.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Tibet; India, Nepal and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas river floodplains sandy land or sand gravelly ground.
Morphology: Plant often prostrate; leaves small, alternate, flattened, ovate, slightly thickened, nearly entire; flower very small, single -like, monoecious, female flowers bract 3-lobed, lateral lobes inner bent, each bract axil part often with female flowers 3–7, female flowers perianth reduced forming 5 filiform lobes; utricle surface with ji crown-shaped projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Aellen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; western Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land or lakeshores salinized and alkalized sandy land.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with silvery white glaucous bloom; leaves alternate, oblong to obovate, entire; fruiting pedicel up to 12 mm; bracteole long 2–3 mm, obtriangular, 3-lobed, in lobes very small, lateral lobes large and spreading. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (L.) G. L. Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores saline-alkaline land, river floodplains ground, channel along or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Stem sometimes four-angled; leaves often opposite, ovate-shaped oblong to ovate-lanceolate, nearly entire; glomerule axillary; fruit nearly without stalk; bract margin entire connate, with reticulate veins and 1–2 spines-shaped projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (M.Bieb.) Aellen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; northern Asia, Southwest China part and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wasteland, sand dunes or fields.
Morphology: Woody stem low dwarf; leaves large mostly opposite, with stalk, both surfaces with dense powdery, entire; flower clustered forming terminal with among broken spike-shaped panicle; fruit nearly without stalk; bracteole subglobose, succulent, margin nearly fully entire connate, surface with wart-like projections and bladder-like-shaped hairs. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 23, 488; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Southwest Asia and Europe, Northeast China and North China cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant nearly without powdery, stem obtuse four-angled; leaf blade ovate-shaped oblong to ovate-shaped triangular, base hastate to wide cuneate, entire or with not regular serrate; inflorescence spike-shaped round conical-shaped; bract in fruit suborbicular, resembling yu qian, diameter 1–1.5 cm, entire. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Moq.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, Gobi gravel desert or arid mountain gullies.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with powdery, stem upper part slightly is four-angled; leaf blade three angles-shaped hastate to three angles-shaped lanceolate, margin with serrate; inflorescence spike-shaped round conical-shaped; bract in fruit broadly ovate to oblong, long 6–10 mm, apex hun round or emarginate, entire. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Borkh.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains desert, wasteland or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant nearly without powdery; stem upper part slightly four-angled; leaf blade long elliptic or broadly lanceolate, base cordate to wide cuneate, upper surface without powdery, lower surface with dense powdery appearing grayish white; panicle terminal; bract in fruit long 0.5–1.4 cm, broadly ovate to oblong, entire. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline flats, lakeshores wetland, grass flat or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant slightly with powdery; lower part leaves opposite, triangular to hastate, base truncate to wide cuneate, nearly entire; spike-shaped round conical-shaped inflorescence terminal; bract in fruit orbicular or broadly ovate, entire, young when with powdery, with large small two type, large type bract long 3–4.5 mm. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Boucher ex DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia, North Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, lakeshores sandy land or valleys wetland.
Morphology: Leaves alternate or nearly opposite, three angles-shaped hastate, margin with not regular coarse serrate; inflorescence spike-shaped or spike-shaped round conical-shaped, male flowers perianth subglobose, yellow, female flowers bract rhombic to ovate-shaped triangular, with dense powdery, entire, reticulate veins inconspicuous. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Rafn) M.A.Gust.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia, North Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, lakeshores sandy land or valleys wetland.
Morphology: Leaves three angles-shaped hastate, with irregular undulate teeth; inflorescence spike-shaped, acute; bract up to 25 mm, hastate or center-shaped triangular, margin with serrate or lacerate lobed-shaped, abaxial surface without attached genus structure. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 17, 23, 486; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Y.Z.Zhao
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land.
Morphology: Stem four-angled, without powdery; lower part leaves opposite, leaf blade broadly ovate or triangular-ovate, lower surface with powdery, entire or shallow undulate, base shallow cordate or truncate; spike terminal, female flowers bract ovate orbicular, with dense powdery, entire, apex acute. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland.
Morphology: Leaf blade ovate-shaped triangular to lanceolate, base wide cuneate, lower surface usually with powdery appearing grayish white; flower arranged forming sparse spike, male flowers perianth green, bract in fruit ovate to ovate-shaped triangular, entire, reticulate veins inconspicuous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan, Russia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, half desert, lakeshores saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, narrow oblong to linear, long 1–3 cm, wide 2–7 mm, entire, both surfaces all is silvery white, with dense powdery; spike-shaped panicle among broken; bract in fruit upper part margin slightly xian 3 obtuse teeth, sometimes margin with several warts-shaped structure. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: Roth
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; India, Afghanistan and Iran also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open and exposed sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, ovate, thickened, entire, both surfaces all is gray-green, with dense powdery; spike short; bract in fruit margin with serrate, close to base in center part corky inflated, and often in linear two lateral each with 1 toward on projecting protruding structure. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Litv.) Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; western Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wet grassland, beach or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate to linear, long 3–9 cm, wide 4–10 mm, long degrees is wide degrees 3 times above; inflorescence spike-shaped; bract in fruit rhombic to ovate-shaped rhombic, with powdery, margin connate part position several reaching middle part, on half part margin usually with finely serrate. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.A.Mey. ex Bong.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Xinjiang; Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, lanceolate to linear, entire; glomerule clustered, female flowers bract in fruit broadly ovate or triangular-ovate, only base margin connate, surface smooth, nearly without powdery. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 38; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Southeastern Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wet grassland.
Morphology: Leaves linear to narrow oblong, both surfaces all is green, without powdery; spike sparse; bract in fruit ovate-shaped triangular to nearly cordate, several without powdery, margin only in most connate at the base, midrib two lateral middle part sometimes each with 1 gu qi projections. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline desert, valleys sandy land or riverbanks fixed dunes.
Morphology: Leaves ovate-shaped triangular to rhombic-shaped ovate, margin with sparse serrate or shallowly lobed, lower surface grayish white, with dense powdery; glomerule axillary; bract in fruit connate forming tube-shaped, only apex margin free, surface densely covering spines-shaped projections. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, Gobi gravel desert or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves ovate-shaped triangular to rhombic-shaped ovate, margin with sparse serrate, lower surface grayish white, with dense powdery; flower clusters entire axillary; bract in fruit flabellate to flattened campanulate, only base margin connate, one parts with attached genus structure, attached genus structure spiny-shaped, soft spines-shaped or warts-shaped. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Popov) G.L.Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and North Africa also found.

Morphology: Leaves ovate to broadly ovate, entire, thickened mostly sap; flower usually 3–8 flowers clustered leaf axils; bract in fruit cordate to jian head shape, only base margin connate, attached genus structure is three kuai xiang lian raised structure. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Aellen) G.L.Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Iran, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, sandy land or proluvial fans.
Morphology: Leaf margin nearly base often with 1 pairs shallowly lobed segments; female flowers bract in fruit three angles-shaped hastate or jian head shape, apex acuminate. Flowering and fruiting period 5–7 yue.

Author: Makino
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian; Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach.
Morphology: Stem erect, cylindrical; leaves rhombic-shaped ovate to ovate-shaped oblong, both surfaces dou with dense powdery, usually slightly is 3 shallowly lobed; spike-shaped panicle small type; bract in fruit with long 1–2 mm short-stalked, margin with triangular serrate. Flowering and fruiting period September–December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Lindl.
Distribution: Native to Australia, Taiwan naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in beach.
Morphology: Leaves broadly ovate to rhombic-shaped ovate, margin with 1–3 pairs undulate teeth or entire; spike-shaped panicle small type; bract in fruit half orbicular, sessile, margin with slender undulate teeth. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Asia, Africa and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline desert, Gobi gravel desert or saline-alkaline sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem erect or outer inclined, gray-green white; leaves broadly ovate to broadly lanceolate, margin with not regular serrate, lower surface grayish white; spike-shaped round conical-shaped inflorescence; bract in fruit rhombic-shaped ovate to ovate, sometimes with few warts-shaped attached genus structure, margin mostly few with teeth. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 16, 23, 487; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Iljin) G.L.Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Pakistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline desert, Gobi gravel desert or saline-alkaline sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem upper part leaves linear-oblong, oblong or narrow triangular, margin entire or with sparse serrate. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Pursh) Nutt.
Distribution: Native to North America, Gansu, Ningxia, Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, subsessile, linear to oblanceolate, oblong or obovate, entire; flower dioecious, rows forming spike-shaped panicle; bract in fruit with long 1–8 mm stalk, with 4 conspicuous long on fruit bracts wings, wings with teeth or entire. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: G.L.Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan.
Habitat: Occurs in shrubland, shrubland slopes, riverbanks or village margins wasteland.
Morphology: Stem erect, slightly four-angled; lower part leaves opposite, broadly ovate or three angles-shaped hastate, base cordate, with coarse teeth; male inflorescence slender slender, flower clusters clustered; female flowers 4–7 flowers clusters clustered borne on ovate cordate leaves-shaped bract inner, tepals in fruit linear-shaped elliptic or obovate, connate at the base; utricle oblique ovate. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China (Southwest China except); Northeast China ya, North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, riverbanks, sandy land, fields or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves broadly ovate to ovate, apex pointed, margin entire, with translucent ring margins, often appearing red; spike long on leaves, flower arranged compact, inflorescence axis with round column-shaped hairs bundles, perianth large mostly in fruit enlarged thick, and appearing five angles star-shaped-shaped. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Thunb.) Kitam.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern China coastal areas; Japan and Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, lakeshores or wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves relatively narrow small, narrowly ovate, oblong to lanceolate, long degrees conspicuous large on wide degrees. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part and North China; Korea, Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields, forest margins or grassland.
Morphology: In lower part leaves ovate-shaped long triangular to ovate-shaped rhombic, base wide cuneate, margin conspicuous three lobed, lateral lobes conspicuous, sometimes 2-lobed; spike-shaped round conical-shaped inflorescence long on leaves, flower arranged sparse, perianth lobes 5, ovate; seeds surface with radiate grooves striations. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Sukhor.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Nepal and xi the Himalayas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves long 3–4 cm, lower part leaves triangular, entire or with teeth, middle part leaves three lobed with long in lobes, upper part leaves three lobed or entire; perianth lobes with keel; seeds conspicuous with keel-shaped projections. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Murr) Aellen
Distribution: Distributed in North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus wasteland, sandy land or fields.
Morphology: Stem prostrate or ascending; leaves long 1.5–3 cm, ovate to broadly ovate, usually three shallowly lobed, lower surface gray-green white, with dense powdery; round conical-shaped inflorescence short on leaves, perianth lobes 5, ovate; seeds transversely borne, surface with feng wo-shaped slender wa. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Golosk.
Distribution: Distributed in western part and Sichuan; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river-valley terraces, slopes or dry grassland.
Morphology: Entire plant with powdery, stem usually prostrate or ascending; leaf blade long 0.5–1.5 cm, ovate to ovate-shaped triangular, both surfaces with dense powdery, entire or three shallowly lobed; short spike short on leaves, perianth lobes 5; seeds transversely borne, surface nearly smooth or slightly with grooves striations. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: D.Don
Distribution: Native to ground not specified, North China to Southwest China each ground cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, wasteland or watersides sandy land.
Morphology: Young leaves purplish red, with cai colored dense powdery; leaf blade rhombic to ovate, long up to 20 cm, margin with not regular serrate; round conical-shaped inflorescence large type, in fruit often pendulous, perianth lobes 5, ovate; seeds transversely borne, surface with shallow reticulate striations. Flowering period August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: Sm.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; worldwide widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves ovate-shaped oblong, long 2.5–5 cm, conspicuous appearing three lobed-shaped, in lobes and lateral lobes dou with serrate; terminal round conical-shaped inflorescence spreading, perianth subglobose, 5 deeply lobed, perianth lobes nie united-shaped bi united; seeds surface with six angles shape slender wa. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: Moq.
Distribution: Native to North America, China North China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaves shape mostly becoming, long up to 12 cm, often with 2 basal lobes and in lobes only slightly long on lateral lobes, or not divided and only with not regular teeth; panicle long 5–17 cm, perianth lobes 5, ovate to triangular; seeds orbicular, with feng wo-shaped wa dots. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Native to South America, throughout China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves relatively large, lanceolate to triangular, margin with irregular teeth or shallowly lobed; panicle large type, flower mostly and dense; seeds diameter about 2 mm, yan colored mostly becoming, can is white, red, yellow, purple, he or black. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Europe, North Africa to western Asia, China Northeast China possibly naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland.
Morphology: Stem erect or ascending; leaves with strongly foul odor, ovate or rhombic, entire, lower surface densely covered with glaucous bloom, upper surface gradually becoming smooth; clusters clustered inflorescence round spherical-shaped, again rows forming compound united inflorescence, perianth lower part connate, 5 tepals, triangular, in fruit enclosing covered with fruit; seeds black, smooth. Flowering and fruiting period autumn.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; world temperate zone and tropical regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaves rhombic-shaped ovate to broadly lanceolate, long 3–6 cm, non- three lobed-shaped, margin with not regular serrate; spike-shaped round conical-shaped or round conical-shaped inflorescence, perianth lobes 5, covering wa-shaped bi united or spreading; seeds transversely borne, surface with shallow grooves striations. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: H.W.Kung & G.L.Chu
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in open slopes.
Morphology: Leaves long 1–2.2 cm, wide 5–10 mm, entire; flower two -like, clustered leaf axils, with bract and 22 segments membranous scale-like bracteole, perianth subglobose, 5 deeply lobed, in fruit persistent, lobes abaxial surface nearly apex places succulent thickened, appearing dou-shaped; seeds erect. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Vietnam, Thailand, Pakistan, India and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy land.
Morphology: Plant spreading spreading, much-branched, glabrous; leaves opposite or accessory whorled, slightly succulent, spatulate, long 1–2.5 cm, wide 4–10 mm, fu with white needle-like bearing jing body; flowers small, mostly flowers clustered, 5 tepals, pale green, carpels 5, free. Flowering period xia qiu, fruiting period winter.

Author: (L.) L.
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Taiwan, Guangdong and Hainan; world widely distributed in pantropical regions.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy land.
Morphology: Plant succulent, prostrate or creeping; leaves opposite, succulent, linear, long 1.5–5 cm; flowers small, solitary leaf axils, tepals connate, with short tube, lobes 5, li surface red, stamens 15–40, ovary on position, 3–5 locules, style 3–5; capsule ovate. Flowering period April–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; world widely distributed in pantropical regions.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open and exposed dry sandy land.
Morphology: Plant creeping or erect; leaves opposite, thin succulent, ovate to obcordate; flower without stalk, solitary leaf axils; tepals connate, with short tube, 5-lobed, pale pink, stamens 10–25, ovary on position, 1–2 locules, style 1–2; capsule apex truncate, 2-lobed. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Pall.) Kuntze
Distribution: Native to Oceania, East China and South China and elsewhere cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant with needle-like bearing jing body; leaves opposite, ovate-shaped rhombic or ovate-shaped triangular, margin undulate; flowers solitary or 2–3 flowers clustered leaf axils, perianth lobes usually 4, inner surface yellowish green, apetalous, stamens 4–13, ovary lower position, 3–8 locules, each locules 1 ovules; nut tuo luo shape. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Africa, throughout China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, flattened, succulent, margin undulate, surface with developing bright granules; flower single flowers axillary, petals numerous, linear, white or shallow rose red, ovary lower position, 4 or 5 locules, each locules with numerous ovules, style 5, filiform, stigma 5; capsule 5 locules, lobed lobes keel with wings. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Burm.f.) N.E.Br.
Distribution: Native to South Africa, Beijing, Taiwan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, nearly round tube-shaped, linear-shaped obovate or spatulate, with small papillae; flower single flowers terminal, type resembling chu daisy-shaped, flower center dark purple, petals lower part one ring is white, petals upper part flower colored mostly becoming, can is yellow, orange, red, purple red, white etc. Colored. Flowering period March–September.

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Native to the Americas tropical regions, throughout China widely introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Climbing shrub, branches leaves densely covered pubescent, with axillary curved spiny; leaves alternate, elliptic or ovate, entire; inflorescence axillary or terminal, flower two -like, usually 3 flowers clustered branches end, outer enclosing 3 bright yan leaves-shaped bract, dark red or pale purplish red, perianth connate forming tubular, green, lobes yellow. Flowering period winter spring.

Author: R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open woodland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, elliptic or ovate, long 10–30 cm, base rounded or cordate, often oblique, entire; cyme long 1–4 cm, pedicel apex with bracteole, flower two -like, perianth tube funnel-shaped-shaped; fruit club-shaped-shaped, long about 1.2 cm, rough, with prickles. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: (J.R. Forst. & G. Forst.) Seem.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore shrubland or mountain ground shrubland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite or accessory whorled, elliptic or ovate-lanceolate, base wide cuneate; round conical cyme long 5–12 cm, pedicel apex without bracteole, flower mixed -like, perianth tube campanulate; fruit nearly cylindrical, long 2.5–4 cm, smooth, apex with expanded spreading persistent perianth. Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.

Author: (Ten.) Steenis
Distribution: Native to South America, southern China each ground naturalized or cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves ovate to suborbicular, base rounded or cordate, slightly succulent, leaf axils often borne small tuber; raceme long 7–25 cm, with mostly flower, pedicel persistent, tepals thin, white, not succulent, flowering period spreading, filaments in flower buds in curved; embryo half orbicular or ma shape. Flowering period June–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Asia tropical regions, throughout China widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves base slightly cordate or orbicular, decurrent forming stalk, entire; spike long 3–15 cm, flower sparse, without stalk, tepals pale red or pale purple, succulent, flowering period several not spreading, filaments in flower buds in erect; embryo spiral. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Jacq.)
Distribution: Native to Asia tropical regions, southern China each ground naturalized or cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Taproot conical; stem succulent; leaves alternate or nearly opposite, slightly succulent, obovate, entire; panicle sparse pine spreading, often two dichotomously branched, flowers small, 5 base several, petals pink or pale purplish red, stamens numerous, 3 stigmas -lobed; capsule fruit subglobose. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period September–November.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; widely distributed worldwide.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, watersides saline-alkaline land, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Plant glabrous, often prostrate; leaves alternate or nearly opposite, flattened thickened, obovate, entire; flower without stalk, diameter 4–5 mm, often 3–5 flowers clustered branches end, petals 5, yellow; capsule ovoid, gai lobed. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas; southern China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, riversides rocky gravel ground or open wasteland.
Morphology: Flower lower and stem upper part densely covered villous; leaves alternate, nearly round column-shaped linear or awl-shaped-shaped narrowly lanceolate, wide 1–4 mm; flower diameter about 2 cm, red purple, filaments yang red, base not connate, style short, 3 stigmas –6-lobed; capsule ovoid. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.
Ecological Type: Mesophytic or xerophytic; psammophytic or lithophytic

Author: (L.) Kurz
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; Indian Ocean, Pacific Ocean and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or coast.
Morphology: Leaves tufted branches apex, nearly leathery, long up to 40 cm, entire; raceme erect, flower relatively large, with long stalk, petals 4, long 5.5–8.5 cm, stamens numerous, long up to 12 cm; fruit often with 4-ridged, diameter reaching 10 cm, outer surface with glandular dots. Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak, tanning material
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Asia and Africa tropical regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or coast.
Morphology: Leaves papery, long 12–30 cm, margin with finely serrate; raceme pendulous, up to 70 cm above, pedicel short, petals long 1.5–2.5 cm, stamens numerous, up to 4.5 cm; fruit ovoid, long 5–7 cm, outer surface without glandular dots. Flowering period nearly entire year.
Uses: Ornamental, ecological use, windbreak, timber, tanning material
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Asia Southwest China part, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, linear to obovate, entire; raceme sparse pine, pedicel slender slender, bract lanceolate, attached borne on pedicel middle part, calyx 5-lobed, lobes triangular, flower after enlarged large, corolla white, nearly bell-shaped, 5-lobed, diameter 2–3 mm, ovary half lower position; capsule globose. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Europe, North Africa and western Asia, East China and South China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, fields or wasteland.
Morphology: Stem four-angled, with narrow wings; leaves opposite or whorled, ovate orbicular to narrowly ovate, entire; flower single flowers axillary, pedicel slender slender, calyx lobes linear-lanceolate, corolla fu-shaped, long 4–6 mm, pale red, 5 deeply lobed, filaments hairy, ovary on position; capsule gai lobed. Flowering period March–April.

Author: Lindl.
Distribution: Distributed in North China to Southwest China each ground; Japan, Vietnam and Myanmar also found.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, waterside wetlands or slopes tidal wetland.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, obovate to linear, entire, both surfaces with small glandular dots; raceme terminal, bract linear, calyx lobes lanceolate, corolla narrow campanulate, white, connate parts about is entire long 1/2, lobes oblong, stamens slightly short on corolla, anthers large. Flowering period March–June, fruiting period April–July.

Author: Lam.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and East China; Korea, Japan, the Philippines and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores rock crevices.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, spatulate to obovate-oblong, both surfaces spreading borne black grains-shaped glandular dots; raceme terminal, bract leaves-shaped, calyx lobes oblong, corolla white, stamens inner hidden, anthers oblong, style subulate, ovary conical. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, inland floodplains saline-alkaline land or marsh meadow.
Morphology: Leaves small, opposite, succulent, long 4–15 mm, wide 1.5–3.5 mm, entire; flowers solitary leaf axils, calyx campanulate, white or pink, corolla-shaped, long about 4 mm, divided reaching middle part, corolla not persistent in, stamens 5; capsule ovate-shaped globose. Flowering period June, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 16, 517; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Blanco
Distribution: Distributed in Fujian, Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves alternate, leathery, obovate or elliptic, entire; umbel terminal, flower two -like, 5 several, sepals leathery, oblique rhombic, asymmetric, corolla white, campanulate, li surface covered with villous, lobes ovate, stamens anthers with transverse ge; capsule crescent-shaped-shaped cylindrical. Flowering period winter spring, fruiting period autumn to winter.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in Hong Kong and Taiwan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coastal shrubland.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves alternate, textured thick, oblanceolate or obovate, entire, glabrous, without margin glandular dots, lateral veins inconspicuous; ya umbel or compound corymb axillary; flower pink to white, sepals orbicular, with dense glandular dots, petals broad ovate; fruit subglobose, red to purple black. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period August–December.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; southern Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, thin papery, broad obovate or broad elliptic, stipule caducous; cyme xie tail-shaped, flower two -like, fang xiang, calyx tube cup-shaped, corolla white, apex 7–8-lobed; drupe oblate, 5–6 locules. Flowering period April–July.

Author: C.F.Gaertn.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; southern Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves opposite, leathery, inverted ovate orbicular or broad elliptic, stipule caducous; inflorescence axillary, flower two -like, white or pale yellow, calyx tube nearly truncate, corolla throat hairy, filaments short; drupe long round-shaped cylindrical, with conspicuous longitudinal ridges 6–8 strips. Flowering period July–November, fruiting period August–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Sw.) Hayek ex Hand.-Mazz., Stadlm., Janch. & Faltis
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe, North Africa and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Stem four-angled; leaves opposite, sessile, small and narrow, elliptic to elliptic-lanceolate, entire; compound cyme two forked type or racemose, calyx lobes linear-lanceolate, corolla tao red, funnel-shaped, apex 5-lobed, stamens 5, style slender and long, linear, stigma 2-lobed. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (Griseb.) Kitag. & H.Hara
Distribution: Distributed in northern China and eastern part along sea; Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore, water wetland or fields.
Morphology: Flower with conspicuous pedicel, calyx lobes subulate, midrib in abaxial surface high high projections appearing keel-shaped, corolla white or pink. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (L.) G.Mans.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China Taiwan naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming wet meadow.
Morphology: Stem much-branched; leaves opposite, sessile, elliptic to lanceolate, apex pointed; spike narrow long, flower arranged sparse, nearly without stalk, 5 several, corolla pink, high base disc-shaped-shaped, lobes spreading, stamens anthers conspicuous, yellow, stigma two lobed. Flowering and fruiting period summer to autumn.

Author: R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; eastern Asia, southern part and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, valleys wetland or open wild grassland.
Morphology: Stem 4-ridged; leaves opposite, ovate to ovate-lanceolate, wide 1.5–2.5.mm; flowers small, diameter 2–3 mm, solitary leaf axils, with pedicel, 4 base several, corolla bell-shaped, throat covered with ran hairs, stamens inner hidden; capsule nearly round spherical-shaped, persistent style apex connate. Flowering period February–August, fruiting period May–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or nearly seashore moist ground.
Morphology: Leaves spiral alternate, thick papery, stalked, pinnate-shaped veins, glabrous; cyme, flower diameter about 5 cm, calyx 5 deeply lobed, inner surface base without glands, corolla high base disc-shaped-shaped white, fang xiang; drupe large, broad ovate or globose, at maturity orange yellow. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Eckl.) A.DC.
Distribution: Native to Africa, dong nan and South China and elsewhere cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Spiny two forked-shaped; leaves opposite, leathery, broad ovate, with pinnate-shaped veins; cyme terminal, corolla high base disc-shaped-shaped, white, fang xiang, corolla lobes oblong, toward zuo covering, long about is corolla tube 2 times; berry ovate orbicular to elliptic, at maturity bright red. Flowering and fruiting period nearly entire year.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wasteland, riverbanks alluvial ground or lakeshores sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, elliptic to linear-lanceolate, margin with slender teeth; round conical-shaped cyme terminal, corolla gu basin-shaped, pink with strips striations, diameter 1.5 cm, 5-lobed, base with corona and succulent disc; follicle long 17–24.5 cm. Flowering period April–September, fruiting period July–December.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wasteland, riverbanks alluvial ground or lakeshores sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, elliptic to linear-lanceolate, margin with slender teeth; round conical-shaped cyme terminal, corolla gu basin-shaped, pink with strips striations, diameter 1.5 cm, 5-lobed, base with corona and succulent disc; follicle long 17–24.5 cm. Flowering period April–September, fruiting period July–December.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wasteland, watersides sandy land or Gobi desert barren flats.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, elliptic-lanceolate, with slender teeth; round conical-shaped cyme terminal, corolla round tube-shaped campanulate, purplish red or pink, diameter 2–3 mm; follicle kuai-shaped, long 8–20 cm. Flowering period April–September, fruiting period July–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 520; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Burm.f.) P.S.Green
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan, Hong Kong and Macao; Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or rocks on.
Life Form: Evergreen woody climbing plant structure, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves opposite, oblong, glabrous; cyme axillary, flower buds apex obtuse, triangular-ovate, corolla high base disc-shaped-shaped, yellowish green, corona succulent, obtriangular, apex minutely mucronate, filaments free, pollen four united; follicle long lanceolate. Flowering period June–September, fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Dryand.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in hot and dry river valleys, open wasteland or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white soft hairs; leaves opposite, obovate-oblong, base cordate; cyme umbel shape-shaped, corolla purple blue, fu-shaped, diameter 3–4 cm, corona lobes than united rui column short, apex inner toward, base with spur; follicle long 7–9 cm. Flowering and fruiting period nearly entire year.

Author: (Willd.) Rech.f.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, three angles-shaped or long round-shaped hastate, base center-shaped hastate, two auricles orbicular; cyme, corolla purple, corona cup-shaped, lobes ovate, 3-lobed, small lobes apex acute or with tail pointed; follicle long 6.5–8.5 cm. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period August–December.

Author: (Willd.) Rech.f.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy soil or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, three angles-shaped or long round-shaped hastate, base center-shaped hastate, two auricles orbicular; cyme, corolla purple, corona cup-shaped, lobes ovate, 3-lobed, small lobes apex acute or with tail pointed; follicle long 6.5–8.5 cm. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period August–December.

Author: R.Br.
