Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized sandy land, seashore sandy land or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Plant structure covered with short-pubescent; leaves opposite, wide triangular-cordate, base cordate, leaves abaxial gray-green white; umbel shape cyme axillary, corolla white, corona two shape, cup-shaped, on end lobed forming 10 filiform body, outer whorls about and corolla lobes etc. Long; follicle paired fruit slender round column-shaped. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Hance) Hemsl.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Hong Kong.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or plains open woodland.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; leaves opposite, long round-shaped hastate to three angles-shaped lanceolate; umbel shape cyme axillary, corolla green white, corona cup-shaped, thin membranous, apex shallow 10-lobed; follicle solitary, long lanceolate. Flowering period May–October, fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Y.X.Liou
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, triangular-ovate or wide cordate, base cordate, glabrous; umbel locule-shaped cyme axillary, flowers small, diameter about 4 mm, corolla dark purple, corona two whorls, outer whorls apex teeth lobed or entire, inner whorls apex ovate; follicle solitary, narrowly lanceolate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period August–September.

Author: (Merr.) Meve & Liede
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or plains open wild shrubland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, ovate orbicular, glabrous; cyme axillary, corolla nearly bell-shaped, yellowish green, lobes oblong, corona lobes short, ovate, raised zhong; follicle paired borne, lanceolate, long 4–6 cm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period October–December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Retz.) R.Br. ex Sm.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China, South Asia, Southeast Asia, Oceania and tropical Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore reefs shrubland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, obovate or ovate-shaped oblong, lateral veins each margins 4–5 strips; umbel shape-shaped cyme axillary, corolla green white, bell-shaped, corona attached borne on corolla lobes wan notched lower, thick and forming stiff strips band; follicle ovate-lanceolate, base swollen large. Flowering period May–September, fruiting period winter to spring.

Author: J.Koenig ex Retz.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with short stiff hairs; leaves narrow oblong or lanceolate, wide 2–3 mm; cyme terminal, bract lanceolate, long 3–4 mm, spreading, calyx lobes lanceolate, corolla tube-shaped, white; drupe globose. Flowering and fruiting period June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, rocky gravel desert or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves elliptic or elliptic round-shaped ovate, wide 1–2.5 cm; falcate-shaped cyme long 2–4 cm, sepals narrowly ovate or ovate-lanceolate, corolla white; drupe, divided stone with inconspicuous wrinkled striations and slender dense wart-like projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Welw.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sand dunes or sloping ground.
Morphology: Leaves ovate-shaped oblong or oblong-elliptic, wide 0.5–1.5 cm; falcate-shaped cyme rows forming large type round conical-shaped inflorescence, sepals linear or oblong, corolla white, style densely covered toward lower white pubescent; drupe, divided stone linear-oblong, flattened, long about 5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore wasteland or open wild barren grassland.
Morphology: Leaves alternate or nearly opposite, ovate or elliptic, base decurrent to petiole appearing wings-shaped, lateral veins 5–7 pairs; falcate-shaped cyme solitary, sepals lanceolate, corolla pale blue or bluish purple, high base disc-shaped-shaped; drupe, divided stone with projections longitudinal ribs. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: (Lam.) Craven
Distribution: Distributed in Taiwan; the Philippines, Indonesia and Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast.
Morphology: Leaves alternate, elliptic, hairy; cyme terminal, calyx lobes lanceolate, corolla white, long 3–4 mm, stigma borne on short and conspicuous style on; drupe white, at maturity divided is 4 with single seeds divided stone. Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.

Author: (L.) J.I.M.Melo
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China and elsewhere; Northeast China ya also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant dense hairy; leaves alternate, lanceolate or oblong, long 1–5 cm, wide 6–10 mm; inflorescence terminal, sepals lanceolate, corolla bell-shaped, yellowish white; drupe elliptic or ovoid, with longitudinal ribs, at maturity divided is 2 each containing 2 grains seeds divided stone. Flowering period May, fruiting period July.

Author: (DC.) comb.ined.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China and elsewhere; Northeast China ya also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, saline-alkaline desert or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves linear to linear-lanceolate, wide 2–5 mm. Flowering period May, fruiting period July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Blanco) Mabb.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves clustered borne branchlets apex, oblanceolate or obovate, both surfaces densely covered filiform yellowish white hairs; falcate-shaped cyme terminal, appearing umbel locule-shaped arranged, calyx succulent, 5 deeply lobed, lobes oblong or obovate, corolla white, tube-shaped; drupe glabrous, subglobose. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Australia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or dried-up rice fields.
Morphology: Plant densely covered spreading strigose hairs; leaves alternate, oblong or obovate, long 1–2 cm; flowers small, 4 base several, corolla white, tube-shaped, style terminal, stigma 2, apex inconspicuous 2-lobed; nutlets 4, with wrinkled striations and spiny-shaped tubercles projecting. Flowering period April, fruiting period June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Willd.) G.Don
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, proluvial fans or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Plant covered with long stiff hairs; leaves alternate, linear-lanceolate; flowers solitary, bract leaves-shaped, calyx 5-lobed to middle part, corolla purplish red, corolla tube straight, attached genus structure position on throat of lower; nutlets 4, reniform, attached surface ju adaxial surface in lower part, bowl-shaped-shaped, margin with slender teeth. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Forssk.) Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; India, Pakistan and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, saline-alkaline land or mountain front of alluvial fans.
Morphology: Plant covered with bristles; leaves oblong; flowers solitary leaf axils, corolla blue or purple, tube-shaped, throat with 5 with pubescent ti shape attached genus structure, stamens attached borne on corolla attached genus structure of lower; nutlets shen-shaped crescent-shaped, adaxial surface ring-shaped projections oblong. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Vent.) Coss. & Kralik
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Pakistan, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain front of rocky gravelly dry slopes, sandy land or desert plains.
Morphology: Plant covered with long stiff hairs; leaves linear-oblong; sepals linear, fruiting period enlarged large stiff becoming, corolla yellow, tube-shaped campanulate, stamens inner hidden, ovary 4-lobed; nutlets densely covered wart-like projections, attached surface flat, without qi-shaped zu zhi. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Kanitz
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in desert rocky gravelly dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem densely covered short-pubescent; cauline leaves narrowly ovate to linear-oblong, long 1–2 cm; falcate-shaped cyme sparse pine, calyx lobes linear, corolla yellow, tube-shaped campanulate, often with purple spots dots; nutlets triangular-ovate, with wart-like projections and short appressed hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; northern Asia, middle part and Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, rocky slopes or lakeshores gravelly ground.
Morphology: Stem with extending spreading long stiff hairs and short strigose hairs; cauline leaves shi-shaped linear, long 1.5–5 cm; falcate-shaped cyme relatively compact, bract linear-lanceolate, calyx lobes linear, corolla yellow, often with purple spots dots; nutlets triangular-ovate, with wart-like projections. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu and Qinghai; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, mountain front of alluvial fans or gravel slopes.
Morphology: Plant densely covered grayish white long stiff hairs; leaves linear, long 8–25 mm, wide 2–4 mm; falcate-shaped cyme, bract linear, calyx lobes subulate, corolla pale bluish purple or pink; nutlets triangular-ovate, densely covered wart-like projections. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert rocky gravelly dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant densely covered erect white long stiff hairs; leaves shi-shaped oblong, long 1–3 cm, wide 3–6 mm; falcate-shaped cyme, bract linear or lanceolate, calyx lobes linear or lanceolate, corolla purplish red or bluish purple; nutlets oblong, with wart-like projections. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: (L.) I.M.Johnst.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; northern Asia and western part widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, hills, watersides sandy land or fields.
Morphology: Plant covered with short strigose hairs; leaves oblanceolate to linear; cyme, flower sparse, calyx lobes linear, corolla high base disc-shaped-shaped, white; nutlets three angles-shaped ovoid, gray brown, with wart-like projections. Flowering and fruiting period April–August.

Author: (Pall.) Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low mountain hills, open wild rocky gravel ground or fields.
Morphology: Roots purple brown; plant densely covered with long stiff hairs; leaves lanceolate-linear; falcate-shaped cyme, flower with short pedicel, calyx lobes subulate, corolla purple; nutlets oblique ovate, densely covered wart-like projections, with short-stalked, attached surface ju short-stalked di surface. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; northern Asia, middle part and Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, slopes grassland or fields.
Morphology: Plant trailing, stem with inverted hooked spiny; leaves stalked, spatulate or narrow oblong; flowers solitary leaf axils, corolla blue; calyx fruiting period irregular enlarged large, two lateral compressed, slightly appearing bang ke-shaped, margin with serrate; nutlets narrowly ovate, surface with warts dots, attached surface orbicular. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Ledeb.) G.Don
Distribution: Recorded distributed in Liaoning large lian; Japan, Korea, Russia and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves elliptic, ovate or obovate, entire, glabrous, covered with grayish white powdery-shaped structure; raceme terminal, corolla blue, bell-shaped, long 8–12 mm, 5 shallowly lobed, stamens 5, exserted, ovary 4-lobed, style exserted; nutlets 4, ovate-elliptic, flattened, smooth glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Forssk.) Asch. ex Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; South Asia, Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, desert or mountain front of tablelands.
Morphology: Leaves shi-shaped linear; flowers small, corolla bluish purple, 5 attached genus structure borne on throat of lower; nutlets 4, ovate, long 3.5–4 mm, adaxial surface ridges keel and pistil base xiang bearing united, abaxial surface with anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, pistil base conical-shaped, high protruding nutlets. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: C.J.Wang
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravel stony Gobi gravel desert or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant flattened spherical-shaped; leaves linear; infructescence slightly extending long and kai spreading, corolla campanulate; nutlets 4, three angles-shaped ovate orbicular, margin enlarged thick forming ridges-shaped wings, margin with 4–5 pairs single rows anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, long 1.5–2 mm; pistil base than nutlets slightly short. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Lehm.) Menyh.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand gravel wasteland, fields or river-valley terraces.
Morphology: Cauline leaves linear, both surfaces all covered with rough hairs; bract linear, distant relatively fruit is long; flowers small, pale blue, bell-shaped, long about 2.5 mm; fruit dense, oblique toward one lateral, nutlets ovate, abaxial surface without keel-shaped projections, margin with 1 rows anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, spiny long 1.5–2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: (Retz.) Dumort.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland or sandy land.
Morphology: Corolla pale blue, long about 4 mm; nutlets ovate-shaped, long 3–4 mm, abaxial surface often with granules-shaped warts projecting, margin with 2 rows nearly etc. Long anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, inner rows spiny long 1.5–2 mm, outer rows spiny relatively inner rows spiny slightly short or nearly etc. Long, style exserted nutlets. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Ledeb.) Gürke
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia, middle part and Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, dry grassland or dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with long rough hairs, without lotus base-shaped basal leaves; bract linear, than fruit long; calyx lobes fruiting period appearing star-shaped-shaped spreading, than fruit long; nutlets ovate, margin with 2 rows anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, inner rows spiny relatively slender and narrow, base expanded spreading xiang hu connate forming narrow wings, outer rows spiny long 0.5–1 mm. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Popov ex Golosk.) Ovczinnikova
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mountain front of alluvial plains arid terraces.
Morphology: Plant covered with gray strigose hairs; corolla pale blue, long 4–5 mm; nutlets 4, different shape, 2 nutlets with leathery wings, 2 wingless only with single rows anchor-shaped-shaped spiny; nutlets adaxial surface ridges keel only base and pistil base xiang bearing united. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: W.T.Wang
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert margin or semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Plant covered with white rough hairs; corolla pale blue, long about 4 mm; nutlets narrowly ovate, only by adaxial surface nearly base very small attached surface and pistil base xiang lian, abaxial disc margin wings toward on square shou contracted forming sac-shaped, entire but with dense short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Trautv.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wasteland.
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white rough hairs; calyx fully entire free, sepals linear, arcuate-shaped, rao attached nutlets, midrib not projecting protruding, corolla pale bluish purple, throat with 5 short ti shape attached genus structure; nutlets paired borne, oblique narrowly ovate, long 2–3 mm, surface with warts-shaped base disc stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, desert, slopes meadow or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Calyx fully entire free, sepals linear, arcuate-shaped, rao attached nutlets, midrib not projecting protruding, corolla pale blue, tube part than rim part short, rim part lobes suborbicular; nutlets wai ovate, smooth, lustrous, yellowish white. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: Stocks
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Calyx campanulate, fully entire free, sepals straight, lanceolate, with projecting protruding midrib, corolla blue, non- often small; nutlets ovate spherical-shaped oblong, densely covered with apex jian-shaped tubercles projecting. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Boiss.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Inflorescence terminal, fruiting period extending long; calyx small, from base above connate, lobes lanceolate, corolla blue, non- often small; nutlets densely covered with white tubercles projecting, tubercles on with small hooked. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes.
Morphology: Inflorescence pine spreading, calyx lobes linear, fruiting period extending long becoming wide, base triangular or cordate, midrib non- often thick, lateral veins conspicuous, forming reticulate-shaped, corolla blue, non- often small; nutlets yin on calyx in, densely covered with apex with stellate hairs tubercles projecting. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: (C.B.Clarke) Hemsl.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; Bhutan, Nepal and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus barren grassland, sandy soil, fields or mostly rocky grassland.
Morphology: Leaves with long-stalked, ovate to broadly lanceolate; inflorescence dense, corolla blue or bluish purple, attached genus structure low ti shape or half yue shape; nutlets ovate, with small tuberculate projections and short hairs, abaxial surface in upper part with kong-shaped depressed, narrow oblong, attached surface position adaxial surface center. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Benth.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Nepal and India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus sandy grassland or salt-lake shores sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant lotus base-shaped, cushion-forming; flower arranged compact, corolla blue or white, lobes round ovate, attached genus structure low ti shape; nutlets ovate or nearly rhombic, with small tuberculate projections, projections apex with anchor-shaped-shaped spiny hairs, abaxial kong not persistent in, attached surface position on adaxial surface middle part or middle part of on. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (W.W.Sm.) I.M.Johnst.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus river floodplains sandy land, meadow margins bare ground or mountain apex scree.
Morphology: Stem spreading flat, succulent; flowers solitary, calyx lanceolate, fruiting period enlarged large, corolla small, purple blue, pistil base flat tan; nutlets abaxial adaxial two surface body type, attached surface position on adaxial surface nearly apex, ridges margin spiny about 10, apex with anchor-shaped hooked, base connate forming wide wings. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Tibet; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus river floodplains grassland or shrubland meadow.
Morphology: Flowers solitary leaf axils, sepals narrow elliptic, corolla white or pale blue, throat attached genus structure shallow 2-lobed; nutlets narrow obovate, with anchor-shaped-shaped spiny and short rough hairs, abaxial surface with cup-shaped or ji crown-shaped projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Pall. ex Lehm.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in valleys stream banks, forest margins or rocky crevices.
Morphology: Stem glabrous; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves oblong, stem upper part leaves lanceolate, upper surface usually glabrous; corolla green yellow, style thickened; nutlets ovate, densely covered anchor-shaped-shaped spiny, abaxial surface depressed, margin enlarged thick and projections, attached surface ovate-shaped oblong. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Burm.f.) Hallier f.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, wasteland or shrubland grass slope.
Morphology: Leaves ovate, apex acuminate; cyme umbel shape-shaped, peduncle long 1–4 cm, bract small and subulate, sepals unequal large, corolla funnel-shaped or campanulate, pale purple, 5 shallowly lobed, style 1, stigma 2-lobed, lower curved; capsule globose, seeds abaxial side margin with narrow wings. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or dry rocky gravelly slopes.
Life Form: Deciduous subshrub or dwarf shrub, chamaephyte
Morphology: Branchlets with short stiff spiny; leaves lanceolate; flowers solitary, sepals unequal large, 2 outer sepals wide ovate-shaped orbicular, base cordate, conspicuous wide on 3 inner sepals; corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, rose colored, style 1, stigma 2, linear; capsule broad elliptic. Flowering period May–June.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or gravel flat.
Life Form: Deciduous subshrub or dwarf shrub, chamaephyte
Morphology: Branchlets with short stiff spiny; leaves oblanceolate to linear; flower single borne on apex with 2 spiny short lateral branches on, sepals nearly etc. Large, shape-shaped mostly becoming, dense hairy, corolla narrow funnel-shaped; capsule ovate. Flowering period April–July.

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, dry rocky gravelly slopes or slopes rock crevices.
Morphology: Plant spreading spreading cushion-forming, branchlets hard, with spiny; leaves narrow linear; flower 2–5 flowers dense on branches end, flower branches often extending long, without spiny, sepals nearly etc. Large, outer surface covered with brown yellow hairs, corolla funnel-shaped, pink, with strips striations; capsule globose. Flowering period May–July.

Author: Desr.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, salinized and alkalized sandy soil or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant prostrate, densely covered with appressed borne yin gray silky hairs; leaves linear or narrowly lanceolate; flowers solitary branches end, long 9–15 mm, sepals 5, outer sepals oblong, inner sepals wider, elliptic, corolla small, funnel-shaped-shaped, pale rose colored or white band purple strips striations; capsule globose. Flowering and fruiting period May–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia, Europe and northern Africa widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides sandy soil, fields or barren slopes grassland.
Morphology: Stem prostrate or twining; leaves ovate-shaped oblong to lanceolate, base cordate or sagittate; inflorescence axillary, with often peduncle, flower often single flowers or 2–3 flowers, bract 2, linear, sepals slightly unequal large, corolla wide funnel-shaped, white or pink, with strips striations; capsule ovate-shaped globose. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Wall.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; southern Asia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in plains wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Plant prostrate, glabrous; basal leaves oblong, apex rounded, base hastate, upper part leaf blade 3-lobed; flower single flowers axillary, bract broadly ovate, long 0.8–1.6 cm, covering sepals, sepals oblong, corolla pale purple or pale red; capsule ovoid. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: (L.) R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; widely distributed worldwide.
Habitat: Occurs in plains wasteland, fields, stream banks grass tuft or slopes forest margins.
Morphology: Plant twining, glabrous; leaves shape mostly becoming, triangular-ovate or broadly ovate, base hastate or cordate, entire or divided; flower single flowers axillary, bract broadly ovate, long 1.5–2.3 cm, covering sepals, corolla white or pale red; su calyx and bract enlarged large enclosing hidden fruit. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (L.) R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and East China; Asia Northeast China along sea and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or coast rock crevices.
Morphology: Stem prostrate; leaves reniform, textured thick, apex rounded or concave, entire or shallow undulate; flower single flowers axillary, bract broadly ovate, than sepals short, sepals nearly etc. Long, corolla pale red, bell-shaped, crown rim slightly lobed; capsule ovoid. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Desr.) Hallier f.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore shrub forest.
Life Form: Evergreen woody climbing plant structure, phanerophyte
Morphology: Leaves broadly ovate or suborbicular, abaxial surface with star-shaped spreading small glandular dots; cyme axillary, flower 1–3 flowers, sepals orbicular, fruits when enlarged large, up to 4–5 cm, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, up to 8–10 cm, purplish red, center dark purple; capsule globose, is enlarged large of sepals surrounding. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Roem. & Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves 3 deeply lobed, in lobes linear-lanceolate, lateral lobes broadly linear; flowers solitary leaf axils, peduncle very short, bract linear, sepals with 1 strips conspicuous midrib, hairy, corolla tubular funnel-shaped, long about 1.25 cm, purplish red; capsule globose, glabrous. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys shrubland, wasteland or seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Stem covered with grayish white stiff hairs; leaves palmate-shaped deeply lobed, covered with sparse stiff hairs; cyme with several flowers flower, dense forming head-shaped, axillary, inflorescence stalk long 4–l 1 cm, involucre conspicuous, oblong, 2 layers, several segments, sepals lanceolate, corolla white, funnel-shaped-shaped; capsule covered with short soft hairs. Flowering and fruiting period autumn to winter.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Ker Gawl.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open wild sandy land, seashore open woodland or shrubland.
Morphology: Leaves center-shaped orbicular or center-shaped ovate, not divided; cyme not forming umbel shape, 1–3 flowers flower, inflorescence stalk slender slender, without involucral bracts, sepals ovate or oblong, apex with small mucronate head, corolla long about 2 cm, white or pale yellow. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) R.Br.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant glabrous, prostrate; leaves succulent, ovate to oblong, long 3.5–9 cm, wide 3–10 cm, apex slightly notched or 2-lobed; mostly forked cyme axillary, sepals glabrous, ovate, corolla purple or deep red, long 4–5 cm; capsule globose. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Uses: Medicinal, ornamental, sand stabilization, ecological use
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Vahl) Griseb.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores, sand dunes or watersides open ground.
Morphology: Leaf blade relatively small, leaves shape mostly becoming, usually oblong, long 1.5–3 cm, wide 0.8–2 cm, apex usually depressed, entire or 3–5-lobed, with relatively large ovate to oblong in lobes and relatively small lateral lobes; corolla white, long 3.5–4 cm. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Pers.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, valleys, roadsides or forest understory.
Morphology: Leaves cordate or center-shaped triangular, both surfaces covered with long stiff hairs; inflorescence axillary, often attached 2 flowers flower, sepals 5, outer sepals three angles-shaped lanceolate, long 0.8–1 cm, base auriculate, decurrent, corolla white, lobes in band covered with short-pubescent, ovary glabrous, seeds usually hairy. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: Blume
Distribution: Distributed in nan sand archipelagos; Southeast Asia, South Asia, Oceania and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Stem prostrate, glabrous; leaves ovate-shaped cordate to oblong, long 1–10 cm, wide 1–7.5 cm; inflorescence axillary, inflorescence stalk short, pedicel slender and long, sepals glabrous, inner concave, oblong-elliptic, corolla pale red or pale red purple, funnel-shaped-shaped, long 3–4.5 cm. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or nearly sea tablelands shrubland.
Morphology: Plant glabrous, stem woody becoming, with longitudinal wrinkled striations or with small tubercles body; leaves orbicular or ovate, base deep cordate; cyme axillary, sepals suborbicular, fruits when enlarged large, initially when like cup-shaped surrounding capsule, and after reflexed, corolla high base disc-shaped-shaped, white, stamens and style inner hidden. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Murray
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline soil desert, sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Branched curved bent, mostly spines spiny; leaves linear or nearly cylindrical, succulent; flower 1–2 flowers borne on short branches on, calyx narrow bell-shaped, irregular 2–4 shallowly lobed, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, shallow purple, tube part long about is rim part lobes long 2–3 times; berry mature after purple black. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Y.C.Wang
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Branches rou weak, few spines spiny; leaves generally middle part wider, narrowly lanceolate or lanceolate; calyx bell-shaped, sometimes yin lobes broken lobed forming truncate head-shaped, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, tube long about is rim part lobes long 2 times; berry mature after red, long round-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Pojark.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Pakistan, Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, sandy beach or oases.
Morphology: Branches hard, with spines spiny; leaves usually in front of end wider, oblanceolate to broadly lanceolate; calyx often 2– 3-lobed to the middle, lobes not broken lobed, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, tube part long about is rim part lobes long 2 times, lobes margin with sparse margin hairs; berry red, ovate round-shaped or long round-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Europe and elsewhere cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized wasteland, grooves shore, slopes or fields.
Morphology: Branches with short spines spiny and long spines spiny; leaves lanceolate, slightly succulent; calyx bell-shaped, usually 2-lobed to the middle, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, tube part long on rim part lobes, stamens filaments base slightly on places and corolla tube inner bi borne one ring dense soft hairs; berry red, large, long 8–20 mm. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Kuang & A.M.Lu
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized desert.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate; calyx bell-shaped, 3-lobed to the middle or sometimes 2-lobed to the middle, corolla tube cylindrical, long 5–6 mm, distinctly longer than the limb lobes, corolla-lobe margins ciliate, tube slightly shorter than the lobes, or longer than the lobes and cylindrical; fruit red, ovate. Flowering and fruiting period unclear.

Author: Mill.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; East Asia, South Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, hilly ground, saline-alkaline land or slopes.
Morphology: Leaves ovate to ovate-lanceolate; calyx usually 3-lobed to the middle or 4–5 teeth lobed, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped, tube part toward on zhou ran expanded large, slightly short on or nearly etc. On rim part lobes, lobes margin margin hairs nong dense, stamens relatively corolla slightly short; berry red, ovate-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–November.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Wettst.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Southwest China; world widely distributed in pantropical regions.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest, watersides, wetland or sandy beach.
Morphology: Plant creeping, succulent, resembling ma teeth xian; leaves long round-shaped oblanceolate; flowers solitary leaf axils, with pedicel, sepals in front of after two in number ovate-lanceolate, its remaining 3 lanceolate to linear, corolla blue to white, stamens 4; capsule long ovate-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Maximum Salinity: 500 mM (sea water concentration, measured ground observation); 200 mM

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe and western Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in marshland.
Morphology: Stem upper part four-angled; leaves opposite, sessile, lanceolate; flowers solitary leaf axils, pedicel slender and long, bract 2, linear, calyx lobes 5, linear-lanceolate, corolla tube yellow, corolla lobes white, with slender deep purple veins, two bilabiate, stamens 2; capsule broadly ovate. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: Miq.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves opposite or 3–4 whorled, sessile, ovate to oblong; raceme terminal, corolla bright yellow, on lip long on lower lip, lower lip lateral lobes wide reaching 3–5 mm, spur long 3–6 mm; capsule spherical-shaped, seeds reniform, margin enlarged thick. Flowering period August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Juss. ex Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in relatively arid calcareous soil low grassland.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse, round column-shaped; leaves wide and round, thick papery, with 7–11 strips veins; spike compact, coarse robust, diameter 8–10 cm, corolla silvery white, lustrous, transparent, filaments pale purple; seeds 2–4, long 1.9–2.4 mm, without angles. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Eurasia widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow, watersides marshland, fields or wasteland.
Morphology: Fibrous roots numerous; leaves wide and round, thin papery or papery, with 3–7 strips veins; spike slender and long, flower without stalk, corolla green white, dull, not transparent, filaments white, anthers pale purple; seeds 8–34, long 0.8–1.2 mm, with angles. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Cham. ex Link
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse, mostly few woody becoming; plant densely covered with white villous; leaves elliptic to ovate-lanceolate, veins 5–7 strips; spike slender round column-shaped, corolla small, anthers purple; capsule long 2.5–3 mm, seeds 3–5, slender small, adaxial surface flat tan. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: Gouan
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in oases, water wetland or salinized and alkalized meadow.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse robust; leaves ovate orbicular or elliptic, textured thick, upper surface glabrous, lower surface sparse hairy; spike with sparse flower, bract small, ovate, sepals broadly ovate, corolla lobes acuminate; seeds 4–5, long 2–3 mm. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 21, 23, 523; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky slopes, saline-alkaline land, meadow or river floodplains.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse long; plant dense covered with arachnoid hairs; leaves lanceolate to linear, nearly entire or sparsely covered small obtuse teeth, veins 1–3 strips; spike often compact, long 1–5 cm, corolla white, glabrous; seeds 1–2. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe band clay, saline soil or fixed sandy land.
Morphology: Straight roots slender slender; leaves narrow linear or filiform linear, thick papery, glabrous, entire; spike round column-shaped or nearly head-shaped, bract suborbicular or broadly ovate, sepals broadly elliptic or orbicular, corolla white, lobes erect; seeds 12–30, fang duo shape, with angles. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Waldst. & Kit.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe, fixed dunes low ground or saline-alkaline wetland.
Morphology: Straight roots slender slender; leaves linear or narrow linear, thick papery, entire, glabrous; spike narrow round column-shaped, bract ovate, sepals obovate, corolla white, glabrous; seeds 7–15, fusiform, with angles. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period June–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Xinjiang wild borne, eastern China naturalized, Asia, Europe and Africa widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in beach, river floodplains, steppe wetland, sandy land or wasteland.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse long; leaf blade lanceolate, long 6–20 cm, papery, entire, veins 3–7 strips; spike compact, bract densely covered with long coarse hairs, in front of pairs sepals to nearly apex connate, corolla white, glabrous; seeds 1–2, long 2–2.6 mm. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: Michx.
Distribution: Native to North America, Liaoning, Shandong and Jiangsu and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores, plains grassland or valleys roadsides.
Morphology: Straight roots slender and long; plant densely covered with villous; leaves lanceolate to linear, jian papery, entire, veins 3 strips; spike narrow round column-shaped, bract apex with awn-shaped long pointed, long is flower 2 times above, sepals separate, corolla pale yellowish white, glabrous; seeds 2. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, Gobi gravel desert, saline-alkaline land or floodplains.
Morphology: Straight roots slender and long; plant densely covered with villous; leaves linear to narrowly lanceolate, stiff papery, entire, veins 3 strips; spike short round column-shaped to head-shaped, sepals separate, corolla white, glabrous; seeds 2, long 3–4 mm, without angles. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Pakistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in rocky slopes, saline-alkaline land dry open grassland.
Morphology: Straight roots slender slender; plant covered with villous; leaves lanceolate, jian papery, veins 3 strips; spike ovoid, after pairs sepals two lateral very not equal, corolla yellowish white, corolla tube glabrous, lobes abaxial surface densely covered with stiff hairs-shaped villous, hairs up to lobes 3 times. Flowering period April–May, fruiting period May–June.

Author: Printz
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, salt-lake shores, saline-alkaline land or floodplains.
Morphology: Straight roots coarse long; leaves linear, slightly succulent, veins 3–5 strips; spike round column-shaped, after pairs sepals two lateral equal, corolla pale yellow, corolla tube bian covered with short hairs, lobes only with short margin hairs. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 20, 522; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Siebold & Zucc.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or mostly rocky ground shrubland.
Morphology: Leaves spiral alternate, soft leathery, elliptic, two end pointed, entire; cyme axillary, without peduncle, pedicel long 1–2 cm, calyx 5 deeply lobed, corolla funnel-shaped-shaped campanulate, 5-lobed, white, with purple spots dots; drupe ovoid, with 5–8 strips longitudinal ridges. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, Northwest China and Southwest China; world temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks, stream sides or forest margins marshland.
Morphology: Stolon slender and short; leaves basal lotus base-shaped, wide linear or narrow spatulate, entire; flower 3–10 flowers from leaves tuft mesophytic protruding, pedicel slender and long, calyx bell-shaped, corolla white or band red, radiate campanulate, corolla lobes 5, stamens 4; capsule ovate orbicular. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: (Forssk.) Vierh.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or salt marsh ground.
Life Form: Evergreen shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Branchlets four square shape; leaves opposite, leathery, ovate to obovate, entire; cyme compact forming head-shaped, flowers small, covered with soft hairs, bract 5, calyx 5-lobed, corolla yellow-brown, apex 4-lobed, stamens 4; fruit subglobose, hairy. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Fujian; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or beach.
Morphology: Stem yellowish green; leaves oblong, nearly leathery, apex acute, margin 4–5 pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed, lobes apex with pointed acute stiff spiny; spike terminal, bract without spiny, bracteole 2, calyx lobes 4, corolla white, on lip reduced, lower lip obovate. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: Vahl
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Hainan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest.
Morphology: Leaves apex flat truncate, margin pinnately divided, lateral veins coarse, straight guan teeth pointed; flower without bracteole. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.f.) Seem.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or sandy soil.
Morphology: Odd-pinnate leaves opposite, leaflet 2–3 pairs; raceme with 2–8 flower, calyx tube-shaped, corolla la ba-shaped, initially green after white, crown tube outer surface with glands; capsule tube-shaped, pendulous, slightly arcuate curved, long 25–50 cm, 2 lobes lobed, seeds with corky wings. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Uses: Ornamental, fiber, windbreak, ecological use, timber
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Greene
Distribution: Distributed in China long river south of each ground; world widely distributed in pantropical regions.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, flat ground or river floodplains etc. Moist ground.
Morphology: Plant creeping, densely covered with appressed short hairs; simple leaves opposite, spatulate to oblanceolate, margin with acute serrate; spike axillary, bract wide obovate, calyx membranous, 2-lobed forming two bilabiate, corolla lobes two bilabiate, white to purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: Gillies & Hook.) N.O'Leary & Múlgura
Distribution: Native to South America, China Yunnan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in moist sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant compact forming cushion-forming; leaves small, grayish white, obovate to elliptic, long 1–7 cm, wide 0.4–2.5 cm, on half part with coarse teeth. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: (Boriss.) Rech.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, Sichuan and Tibet; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riversides salinized and alkalized marshland.
Morphology: Plant entire body densely covered with gray white soft hairs; stem obtuse four leng shape; leaves opposite, elliptic or oblong, margin with serrate-shaped teeth; verticillaster rows forming compact spike, calyx campanulate, corolla purplish red, stamens 4, exserted. Flowering period July–August.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China, South China and Yunnan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains, river floodplains, open woodland or wasteland.
Morphology: Branchlets four-angled; trifoliolate leaves, leaflet ovate or obovate-oblong, entire, abaxial surface densely covered with grayish white soft hairs; panicle terminal, corolla pale purple or bluish purple, two bilabiate, stamens 4, exserted corolla outer; drupe suborbicular. Flowering period July, fruiting period September–November.
Uses: Medicinal, ornamental, aromatic use, ecological use, fiber

Author: Steenis
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores or lakeshores sandy land.
Morphology: Stem creeping; simple leaves opposite, leaflet obovate or suborbicular, apex usually rounded-obtuse or with mucronate head, base cuneate, entire. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period August–October.
Uses: Medicinal, ornamental, aromatic use, ecological use, fiber
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest, sandy beach or plains mountain ground.
Life Form: Evergreen climbing shrub or small tree, phanerophyte
Morphology: Simple leaves opposite, oblong-ovate to suborbicular, entire, both surfaces along veins with short-pubescent; cyme in branches apex composed of umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, calyx two bilabiate, corolla small, pale yellow, slightly appearing two bilabiate, on lip entire, lower lip 3-lobed; drupe globose or obovate. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Hayata
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Japan and Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides wet grassland.
Morphology: Stem four-angled; simple leaves opposite, membranous, obovate, very part cuneate or decurrent, margin with teeth or lobes, both surfaces nearly glabrous; verticillaster arranged forming spike, bracts leaves and stem leaves different shape, calyx teeth ovate-shaped triangular, corolla white, on lip short on lower lip lateral lobes. Flowering and fruiting period March–June.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, sand dunes barren wild or arid alkaline soil ground.
Morphology: Young branches purple brown; simple leaves opposite, linear-lanceolate or linear-oblong, entire, abaxial surface densely covered grayish white soft hairs; cyme axillary, inflorescence stalk slender and long, corolla bluish purple, 5-lobed, stamens 4, and style all exserted corolla tube outer; capsule elliptic round-shaped globose. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; South Asia, Southeast Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores.
Morphology: Young branches four-angled; leaves opposite, thinly leathery, ovate to ovate-lanceolate, entire, often glabrous, with yellow slender small glandular dots; cyme often with 3 flower, flower with xiang wei, corolla white, apex 5-lobed, corolla tube long 2–3 cm, stamens 4, slender and long; drupe obovate. Flowering and fruiting period March–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Hebei, Shanxi and Shandong.
Habitat: Occurs in sand gravel ground, wasteland or dry grassland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with gland pubescent; stem four-angled; simple leaves opposite, lanceolate to linear, wide 2.5–8 mm, entire; raceme terminal, long about 4–7 cm, calyx with conspicuous peltate segments, corolla yellowish white or white. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Nakai
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river lake shores margins sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem four-angled; simple leaves opposite, lanceolate or three angles-shaped lanceolate, wide 3–6 mm, margin entire but slightly involute; flowers solitary leaf axils, calyx with small peltate segments, corolla purple. Flowering period June–July, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Fisch. ex Schrank
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Northeast China ya, North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains sandy soil, slopes grassland or wet meadow.
Morphology: Stem four-angled; simple leaves opposite, three angles-shaped narrowly ovate to lanceolate, wide 0.4–1.4 cm, margin with shallow acute teeth; flowers solitary leaf axils, calyx with peltate segments, corolla bluish purple. Flowering period June–August, fruiting period August–September.

Author: Hemsl.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and East China; Korea, Japan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Rhizome slender and long horizontally creeping; stem four-angled; simple leaves opposite, with short-stalked, elliptic, wide 0.3–1.5 cm, margin with shallow obtuse teeth; flowers solitary leaf axils, calyx with small peltate segments, corolla purple. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Retz.) Sm.
Distribution: Distributed in Hainan and Taiwan; Japan, India and the Philippines and elsewhere also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open wasteland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with white soft hairs; simple leaves opposite, leaves small small and rounded-obtuse, long 1–3 cm, wide 0.6–1.5 cm; verticillaster axillary, calyx teeth 10, three angles-shaped conical shape, narrow long, with several in number slightly long, corolla white, covered with white villous. Flowering period November–December, fruiting period December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) W.T.Aiton
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi and Hainan; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy soil, fields or gentle sloping ground.
Morphology: Plant covered with bristles; simple leaves opposite, oblong-lanceolate, long 3.5–5 cm, wide 0.5–1 cm, margin sparsely covered serrate; verticillaster with few flower, bract relatively calyx short, calyx mouth slightly oblique, teeth relatively gui ze, needle-like spiny-shaped, corolla white to blue. Flowering and fruiting period entire year.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Sm.
Distribution: Distributed in Yunnan and Guangdong; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, open river floodplains, wasteland or forest margins shrubland.
Morphology: Stem and each part covered with slightly short-pubescent; simple leaves opposite, long round-shaped linear to linear, wide 0.5–1.2 cm, nearly entire or sparsely covered undulate serrate; verticillaster with mostly flower, calyx mouth very oblique, in fruit expanded large and straight, teeth irregular, corolla white. Flower fruiting period winter to spring.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Sichuan; Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly sand wasteland, slopes, riverbanks or fields.
Morphology: Roots succulent and long; stem much-branched, green, often dense appearing sao broom-shaped-shaped; leaves small and sparsely covered, not conspicuous; raceme terminal, flower often 3–7 flowers, sparse, calyx nearly leathery, corolla purplish red, corolla tube long tube-shaped, end 2 shallowly lobed; capsule subglobose. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Beck
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Ningxia and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy ground, gravelly ground or hills sloping ground.
Morphology: Plant succulent, bright yellow; leaves compact, scale-like; spike terminal, bract and bracteole lanceolate, calyx nearly bell-shaped, 4 deeply lobed, lobes linear or oblong-lanceolate, corolla tube-shaped campanulate, pale yellow, in filaments attached places densely covered one ring villous. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: (Schenk) Wight ex Hook.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; India, Pakistan and a la bo peninsula also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or tamarisk willow tuft, jioccurs in tamarisk willow genus plant structure.
Morphology: Plant succulent; leaves milky white, triangular; spike terminal, bract lanceolate, calyx tube-shaped, apex 5 shallowly lobed, milky white, corolla tube part often yellowish white, lobes band purple, only filaments base covered with villous, anthers base rounded-obtuse. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.

Author: Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sand dunes, jioccurs in saxaul or white saxaul.
Morphology: Plant succulent; leaves ovate to lanceolate; bract and corolla etc. Long or slightly long, calyx long degrees about is flower 1/2, corolla tube pale yellowish white, lobes yan colored with becoming different, pale yellowish white, pale purple or margin purple, yao locules base minutely mucronate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.

Author: (C.A.Mey.) Beck
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert lake basins low ground or salinized and alkalized sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant succulent; leaves ovate-shaped oblong; bract long about is corolla 1/2, calyx long about is corolla 1/3, corolla tube pale yellowish white, lobes purple or pale purple, yao locules base minutely mucronate. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (C.A.Mey.) Beck
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline soil, clay or gravel slopes.
Morphology: Plant succulent; leaves ovate-lanceolate; bract and corolla etc. Long or slightly long, calyx 4–5 deeply lobed, after margins lobes appearing acute pointed-shaped or notched, its remaining lobes relatively large, 2 segments relatively obtuse, 2 segments relatively pointed, corolla tubular campanulate, white, lobes pale blue or purple jin colored. Flowering period April–May.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Stephan
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; North Asia, Central Asia and West Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, slopes or ditch banks grassland.
Life Form: Biennial or perennial parasitic herb, geophyte
Morphology: Plant succulent, densely covered with spider-like filiform long woolly hairs; leaves scale-like, ovate-lanceolate; spike terminal, bract short on flower, without bracteole, calyx small lobes narrowly lanceolate, corolla bluish purple, on lip apex 2 shallowly lobed, filaments hairy, anthers glabrous. Flowering period April–July, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Wallr.) Beck
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; northern Asia, western part and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land or sand dunes, jioccurs in wormwood genus plant structure.
Life Form: Annual to perennial parasitic herb, geophyte
Morphology: Plant succulent, densely covered with glandular hairs; bract ovate or ovate-lanceolate, calyx lobes apex often 2 shallowly lobed, corolla geniculate-shaped curved, in filaments attached places swollen large, lower lip lobes suborbicular, tube part pale yellow, lobes pale purple or pale blue, filaments and anthers glabrous. Flowering period May–July, fruiting period July–September.

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and East China; Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, slopes or steppe.
Life Form: Biennial or perennial parasitic herb, geophyte
Morphology: Plant succulent, densely covered with short glandular hairs; leaves scale-like, lanceolate; flower without bracteole, calyx 2-lobed reaching base, lobes again 2 shallowly lobed, corolla yellow, on lip apex 2 shallowly lobed, lower lip long on on lip, filaments base sparsely covered with short glandular hairs, anthers hairy. Flowering period April–June, fruiting period 61–August.

Author: C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy slopes.
Life Form: Biennial or perennial parasitic herb, geophyte
Morphology: Plant succulent, nearly glabrous or covered with sparse few short glandular hairs; filaments base densely covered with white villous, corolla bluish purple, on lip apex 2 shallowly lobed, lower lip long on on lip, anthers hairy. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Moench
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wet grassland or watersides marshland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with white slender stiff hairs; leaves opposite, lanceolate to strips-shaped lanceolate, margin sparsely covered serrate; spike terminal, bract leaves-shaped, calyx fruiting period enlarged large, lobes narrow triangular, corolla purplish red, covered with white pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Gansu, Inner Mongolia and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys mostly rocky gravel ground or lakeshores flat plain ground.
Morphology: Leaves in stem lower part opposite, upper part 3–4 whorled, oblong, nearly smooth, pinnatisect; spike terminal, leaves-shaped bract long on flower, calyx membranous, with ribs, corolla yellow, long 20–25 mm, kui apex with short beak. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land or submerged water grassland.
Morphology: Leaves basal and cauline, twice deeply pinnatifid; spike compact forming head-shaped, covered with white soft hairs, bract linear, calyx wide obtuse shape, corolla rose colored or white, long 22–25 mm, tube straight, lower lip spreading, kui with 1 pairs very short teeth. Flowering period May, fruiting period June.

Author: Rudolph
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Gansu and Tibet and elsewhere; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas wet meadow or streams margins.
Morphology: Leaves basal and cauline, pinnately divided, dense forming tuft; flowers axillary, calyx tubular, corolla yellow, long 5–8 cm, kui apex with half ring-shaped rolled curved slender beak, lower lip with two conspicuous dark red brown spots dots. Flowering period July–September.

Author: (L.) Kunth
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang and Inner Mongolia; North Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, alkaline soil meadow or shrubland.
Morphology: Plant covered with white pubescent; leaves long linear to strips-shaped lanceolate, entire, base protruding three veins; spike terminal, bract large on leaves, ovate-lanceolate, yellowish white, calyx tubular, base swollen large, upper part divided, corolla pale yellow or white, tube part long tubular, hidden on calyx inner. Flowering period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Gaertn.) Roxb.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Southeast Asia, South Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or coast cliffs.
Morphology: Plant erect or spreading spreading; leaves spirally arranged on branches apex, spatulate to obovate, long 10–22 cm, entire; cyme axillary, corolla bilaterally symmetrical, after square dehiscent to base, lobes white, base pale yellow, inner surface densely covered with white long hairs; drupe ovate spherical-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period April–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore mangrove forest or salt fields.
Morphology: Plant trailing; leaves spiral attached, succulent, strips-shaped spatulate, long 1–2.5 cm, entire; flowers solitary leaf axils, corolla pale blue, after square dehiscent to base, its remaining lobed to middle part, lobes toward one square spreading, wings margin lower part mostly few fringed. Flowering and fruiting period March–December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Turcz.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, slopes gravelly ground or river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Stem pale yellow, covered with head-shaped stalked glandular hairs; leaves linear or linear-lanceolate, base amplexicaul, margin with spiny teeth, both surfaces green, covered with sparse hairs; compound capitulum solitary branches end, diameter 2–3 cm, capitulum only containing with 1 florets, small flowers blue or white. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, slopes, wasteland or water ditch banks.
Morphology: Stem wings wide 2–5 cm, with needle-like spiny; leaves long elliptic to oblanceolate, margin with spiny teeth and needle-like spiny; capitulum rows forming corymb, involucre ovate or globose, diameter reaching 5 cm, involucral bracts mostly layers, leathery, subulate, florets purplish red; pappus bristles jie hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert mountain ground.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with woolly, with wide stem wings; leaves long elliptic or elliptic, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed, margin with yellow stiff needle-like spiny; capitulum solitary stem apex, involucre globose or long globose, diameter 4–6 cm, involucral bracts erect; pappus bristles feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, grassland, fields or water channel margins.
Morphology: Stem branches grayish white, with wide 1.5–2 cm stem wings; leaves long elliptic, margin with needle-like spiny, both surfaces grayish white; capitulum terminal, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts mostly layers, apex acuminate forming needle-like spiny, florets purple or white; pappus shallow brown, pappus bristles slender rough hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: Hemsl.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; xi the Himalayas also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river floodplains ground, lakeshores sandy land or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant lotus base-shaped, without ground on stem, entire plant glabrous; leaves narrowly lanceolate or linear, both surfaces green, deeply pinnatifid; capitulum rows forming diameter 3–4 cm half globose overall inflorescence, involucre cylindrical, small flowers bluish purple; pappus brown. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Hance
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China.
Habitat: Occurs in salt marsh marsh, mountain ground meadow or forest margins.
Morphology: Leaves linear-oblong to lanceolate, margin with sparse acute slender teeth; bracts leaves large type, membranous, purple, elliptic, surrounding overall inflorescence; capitulum 4–7, involucre wide bell-shaped, involucral bracts entire or upper part margin purple, florets purple; pappus pale brown. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline land, wasteland or sand dunes.
Morphology: Stem without wings; leaves with stalk to sessile, elliptic to lanceolate, margin entire or with teeth, both surfaces green; involucre bell-shaped or cylindrical, in layers and inner layers involucral bracts apex with pale purplish red and margin with small serrate expanded large orbicular attached segments, florets pale purple; pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.Winkl.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline meadow or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem with narrow wings; leaves lanceolate or linear-lanceolate, pinnately divided, both surfaces green, covered with rough stiff hairs; capitulum numerous, involucre bell-shaped, covered with white villous, involucral bracts 4–5 layers, in layers and inner layers apex with expanded large purplish red membranous attached segments, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Lipsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Qinghai.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, barren slopes grassland or fields.
Morphology: Stem with narrow wings; leaves often with stalk, lanceolate, pinnate-shaped half lobed, deeply lobed or pinnate-shaped serrate, both surfaces rough; involucre bell-shaped or ovate-shaped campanulate, involucral bracts 4–5 layers, apex with serrate attached segments, attached segments outer layers leaves textured, inner layers purplish red membranous, florets purple. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grass flat, salt ground, wasteland or barren slopes.
Morphology: Stem with narrow wings; leaves sessile, long elliptic to lanceolate, entire, both surfaces green, dense covered with short stiff hairs; capitulum numerous, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 5 layers, outer layers apex reflexed, gu needle-like, in layers and inner layers apex with expanded large pink membranous attached segments, florets purple. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Lipsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt flower meadow or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem slightly with wings; petiole base sheath-shaped expanded large, leaf blade ovate to oblong, entire or with teeth, green, thickened; involucre bell-shaped, covered with white short-pubescent, involucral bracts 5–6 layers, outer layers small, in layers and inner layers apex with pale purplish red with teeth membranous attached segments, florets pale purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, salt saline low ground or river floodplains tidal wetland.
Morphology: Petiole base expanded large half amplexicaul, leaf blade pinnately divided, both surfaces glabrous; involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 4–6 layers, outer layers with small pointed tip, inner layers apex red membranous expanded large, florets purplish red; pappus pale yellow-brown. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land or desert steppe.
Morphology: Stem with narrow wings; petiole base sheath-shaped expanded large, leaf blade twice deeply pinnatifid, textured thick, both surfaces covered with sparse short-pubescent; involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 5 layers, outer layers apex green, inner layers apex with pale purple with teeth membranous attached segments, florets red purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Inner Mongolia; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Stem with wide wings; petiole base sheath-shaped expanded large, leaf blade large head pinnate-shaped or pinnately divided, both surfaces green, covered with short rough hairs; involucre long round-shaped or ovate-shaped, involucral bracts 5 layers, inner layers apex with purple membranous expanded large attached segments, attached segments margin with small serrate, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline wetland, wet meadow or river valleys gravelly ground.
Morphology: Plant cushion-forming; leaves awl-shaped-shaped linear, long 0.8–1.2 mm, wide 1 mm, leathery, glabrous, entire, apex with small pointed tip; involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 4 layers, upper part black purple, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Krasn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized sandy land, mostly rocky slopes or ice saline ground.
Morphology: Stem covered with short-pubescent or nearly glabrous; basal leaves long-petiolate, leaf blade lanceolate, both surfaces covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, margin with teeth or pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed, teeth end with cartilaginous textured small pointed; involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, florets pale purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period August.

Author: Danguy
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas saline-alkaline land or stony dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem covered with white short stiff hairs; basal leaves long-petiolate, leaf blade twice deeply pinnatifid, both surfaces green, both surfaces glabrous or nearly so, with glandular dots; involucre inverted round conical-shaped, involucral bracts 4–5 layers, wheat-like gan yellow, outer surface glabrous, florets pale purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: B.Fedtsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline meadow.
Morphology: Basal leaves with short-stalked, leaf blade pinnately divided, textured thick, gray-green, densely covered with spider-like filiform pubescent or sparse short stiff hairs; capitulum small and mostly, involucre elliptic round-shaped or bell-shaped, involucral bracts 4 layers, pale red purple, densely covered with short-pubescent, apex obtuse, florets pink or pale red purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Lipsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river lakeshores salt becoming ground or gravel shore flats.
