Morphology: Leaf blade oblong, textured thick, succulent, gray-green or blue green, covered with rough hairs and pubescent, margin with undulate teeth, teeth end with cartilaginous textured pointed; capitulum small and mostly, involucre round column-shaped or narrow bell-shaped, covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, involucral bracts 5 layers, toward inner gradually long, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: Adams
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in riverbanks alkaline ground, moist river floodplains or saline meadow.
Morphology: Plant gray-green; petiole base expanded large, leaf blade lanceolate or long elliptic, entire, with teeth or divided, succulent, glabrous; involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts 6–7 layers, upper part band purplish red, florets pink. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Pall.) Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, Asia Southwest China part and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline soil grassland, Gobi gravel desert or lakeshores wetland.
Morphology: Leaves large head pinnately divided, textured thick, succulent, both surfaces green; involucre narrow round column-shaped, involucre 5–7 layers, outer surface covered with spider-like filiform woolly, florets powdery purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Lipsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia and Gansu.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem sparsely covered with sparse white short-pubescent; leaves linear to oblong, sessile, margin with sparse small serrate; involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts 4–5 layers, outer surface covered with sparse white spider-like filiform short-pubescent, florets pink. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang, Inner Mongolia and Gansu; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand gravel ground, alpine areas shrubland or meadow.
Morphology: Stem covered with spider-like filiform hairs or short-pubescent; leaves linear or linear-lanceolate, entire, revolute, upper surface green, lower surface white, covered with white dense dense soft hairs; involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts 4–5 layers, purplish red, outer surface covered with sparse spider-like filiform hairs, florets pink. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Klatt
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; India and Kashmir also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert broken stones slopes, grassland or rock crevices.
Morphology: Plant cushion-forming tufted; leaves stalked, narrow linear-lanceolate, margin serrate, both surfaces densely covered with gland-shaped short-pubescent; capitulum solitary, often is linear leaves surrounding, involucral bracts lanceolate, erect, covered with pubescent or woolly, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (DC.) Sch.Bip.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; India and Kashmir also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus slopes wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves basal, conspicuous exceeding protruding capitulum, inverted toward pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed, both surfaces densely covered with white soft hairs; capitulum solitary, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 6 layers, purplish red, densely covered with grayish white soft hairs, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: K.M.Shen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized marshland.
Morphology: Stem band purplish red, glabrous; leaves narrowly lanceolate or linear, both surfaces green, glabrous, entire; capitulum few compact arranged forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre bell-shaped, covered with plant filiform pubescent and glandular dots, involucral bracts 4 layers, toward inner gradually long, band purplish red, florets pale purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: N.D.Simpson
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky salt becoming terraces or saline meadow.
Morphology: Stem covered with white villous and dense glandular hairs; leaves oblong-ovate or long elliptic, pale green, both surfaces covered with glandular hairs, margin with small pointed teeth; capitulum arranged forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts 5–6 layers, band purplish red, covered with glandular hairs and long woolly hairs, florets pale rose red. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi, Ningxia and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land or terraces.
Morphology: Stem base coarse thick, covered with dense thick woolly hairs and can persistent withered petiole; leaves pinnately divided; capitulum solitary branches end, involucre bowl-shaped-shaped, involucral bracts leathery, erect, corolla red; achene smooth without spiny tubercles, pappus bristles not connate forming ring, not deciduous, yong jiu gu bearing in achene on. Flowering period May–August.

Author: (Ledeb.) Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravel Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Stem base not ten divided developing yu; leaves long elliptic, deeply pinnatifid; capitulum solitary branches end, involucre bowl-shaped-shaped, involucral bracts apex spiny awn-shaped long-acuminate, florets red purple; achene densely covered with spiny tubercles, pappus bristles short feathery-shaped, base connate forming ring, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period June.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or desert.
Morphology: Stem without wings; leaves margin with large serrate or shallowly lobed, with pale yellow needle-like spiny, upper surface green, lower surface grayish white; capitulum solitary branches apex, involucre globose or ovate, diameter 1.5–2 cm, with pale yellow needle-like spiny, involucral bracts mostly reaching 100 above, florets white. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; Nepal, India and Pakistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland or river floodplains gravelly ground.
Morphology: Leaves pinnatisect, lobes gu needle-like, with long stiff needle-like spiny; capitulum solitary branches end, involucre subglobose, covered with dense dense and swollen pine spider-like filiform hairs, diameter 3–4 cm, involucral bracts leathery, purplish red, apex acuminate forming stiff needle-like spiny, florets purplish red. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Franch.) Mattf. ex Rehder & Kobuski
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu, Qinghai, Sichuan and Tibet; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores salinized and alkalized sandy land, wasteland, fields or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Plant without stem, lotus base-shaped; basal leaves dense, pinnately divided, margin mostly needle-like spiny, both surfaces green, with mostly slender bao long nodes hairs; capitulum is leaves tuft surrounding, involucre wide bell-shaped, involucral bracts mostly needle-like spiny, florets purplish red; pappus bristles base connate forming ring, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (S.Gmel.) Bobrov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in lakeshores floodplain floodplain flat, grassland or fields.
Morphology: Leaves long elliptic or lanceolate, margin serrate and slender and long needle-like spiny, base decurrent forming stem wings, both surfaces green, glabrous; capitulum solitary stem apex, involucre ovate orbicular, with short needle-like spiny, florets red purple; pappus base connate forming ring, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 530; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Scop.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; ya ou large lu widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in ditch banks water wetland, fields or lakeshores wetland.
Morphology: Leaves pinnately divided, lateral lobes with 2–5 large small unequal spiny teeth, both surfaces same colored, green or lower surface with very sparse spider-like filiform hairs; involucre ovate ovate-shaped, with short needle-like spiny, florets purplish red; pappus base connate forming ring, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Nägeli
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland, tidal wetland or wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves both surfaces conspicuous different colored, upper surface green, glabrous, lower surface grayish white, covered with dense thick soft hairs. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Willd.) Besser ex M.Bieb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China and elsewhere; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plains wasteland, waterside wetlands or fields.
Morphology: Leaves pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed or with coarse teeth, margin mostly needle-like spiny, upper surface green, lower surface covered with grayish white soft hairs; capitulum numerous, involucre narrow ovate orbicular, often band purple, involucral bracts apex with short needle-like spiny, florets purplish red or white; pappus dark-stained white, mostly layers, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (Bunge) Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem white, much-branched; leaves long elliptic to linear, margin with white hard needle-like spiny; terminal capitulum is upper part stem leaves surrounding, not xian exposed, involucre ovate-shaped long elliptic, florets yellow; pappus bristles different type, outer layers pappus bristles serrate-shaped or rough hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to Europe, China North China, Northwest China and East China and elsewhere cultivated or naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore or watersides sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant grayish white; leaves lanceolate to linear, entire, upper surface green, bian surface grayish white, covered with thin soft hairs; capitulum borne on branches end, involucre elliptic round-shaped, involucral bracts apex with shallow brown or white attached genus structure, attached genus structure margin fringed, margins flower long on center disc flower, blue to purple. Flowering and fruiting period February–August.

Author: (Ledeb.) Gugler
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; Central Asia, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, terraces, floodplains or fields.
Morphology: Leaves pinnatisect, lobes margin entire, both surfaces green, rough, covered with short rough hairs; involucre ovate or bowl-shaped-shaped, entire involucral bracts apex with membranous attached genus structure, attached genus structure dark brown or brown, apex with short needle-like spiny, margin with margin hairs-shaped serrate, florets etc. Large, purple. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Boiss.) L.Martins
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan, Russia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river lake shores or salinized meadow.
Morphology: Stem with wings; leaves long elliptic or elliptic round-shaped oblanceolate, both surfaces green, rough, covered with short spiny hairs and small glandular dots, margin entire; involucre long elliptic, involucral bracts apex with membranous attached genus structure, florets yellow; pappus bristles different type, outer layers serrate-shaped or rough hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert mountain slopes, sandy land or desert.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate, entire, both surfaces green, covered with sparse hairs; involucre bowl-shaped-shaped or long ovate-shaped, involucral bracts mostly layers, apex with white transparent membranous attached genus structure, attached genus structure half yue shape or ovate, easily deciduous, florets purplish red, margins flower without -like, center flower two -like, filaments with pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Leaves elliptic or lanceolate, margin serrate, textured thin, both surfaces green, smooth glabrous; involucre ovate to half globose, involucral bracts apex with membranous transparent lanceolate attached genus structure, easily diao luo, florets yellow; entire pappus bristles membrane segments-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in half desert or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Leaves elliptic to lanceolate, textured thick, margin serrate; involucre bowl-shaped-shaped, involucral bracts 7 layers, inner layers involucral bracts apex with hyaline-membranous attached genus structure, florets purple; achene apex orbicular, without fruit margin, pappus base not connate forming ring, but easily deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: B.Fedtsch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in tamarisk willow shrubland, desert poplar forest or yellow soil hills.
Morphology: Leaves pinnatisect, lobes linear, both surfaces green, smooth, glabrous; capitulum small, involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts with red veins striations, apex without attached genus structure, florets yellow or red, corolla covered with white pubescent; achene covered with white pubescent, apex with fruit margin. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (Ledeb.) L.Martins
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert.
Morphology: Leaves textured stiff, long elliptic, deeply pinnatifid, lobes margin serrate or shallowly lobed-shaped, both surfaces green, rough; involucre round column-shaped, covered with white soft hairs, involucral bracts apex needle-like spiny spreading or reflexed, florets entire two -like, purple or red; pappus bristles serrate-shaped, divided spreading deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period June.

Author: Briq.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, South Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, sand dunes among low ground, hills or mountain base plains.
Morphology: Stem very short contracted, branched spreading spreading; leaves elliptic, margin with finely serrate and cartilaginous textured small pointed tip; involucre elliptic, involucral bracts apex with needle-like spiny, outer surface with white pubescent, corolla pink; pappus different type, outer rows pappus hairs-shaped, inner rows pappus membrane segments-shaped, very short. Flowering and fruiting period July.

Author: (L.) D.J.N.Hind
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.

Morphology: Leaves textured stiff, long elliptic to linear, entire, both surfaces gray-green; involucre ovate, involucral bracts apex with white hyaline-membranous attached genus structure, entire florets two -like, pink or pale purple; entire pappus bristles base not connate forming ring, not deciduous or divided spreading deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (Krasch. & Lipsch.) Zaika, Sukhor. & N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in broken stones slopes or mountain front of plains.
Morphology: Roots with globose swollen large tuberous roots; stem densely covered with pubescent; leaves lanceolate, margin wrinkled undulate, both surfaces densely covered with soft hairs; capitulum conical, involucral bracts outer surface densely covered with short-pubescent, ligulate florets yellow; achene round column-shaped, densely covered with silky-shaped pubescent; pappus bristles feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, South Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes sheltered from wind slope, clay or stony ground.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate, margin wrinkled undulate; involucre ovate round column-shaped, involucral bracts 2 layers, nearly etc. Long, margin slightly membranous, ligulate florets pale yellow; achene round column-shaped, close to nearly apex with pian zhi body ring, ring above glabrous; pappus shu gray, long feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Bunge) C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, South Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, clay ground or grassland.
Morphology: Leaves long elliptic round-shaped ovate or lanceolate, both surfaces coarse se, margin with slightly serrate; involucre ovate-shaped cylindrical, involucral bracts 2 layers, outer layers herbaceous, inner layers hard, leathery, florets yellow; achene middle part with 1 jia thick pian zhi body ring, ring above hairy, hairs covered with structure and shu gray pappus xiang zhan lian. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: S.A.Nikitin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.

Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Pavlov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly ground or salinized and alkalized gravelly ground.
Morphology: Leaves broadly lanceolate, wrinkled undulate, margin white membranous; inflorescence stalk in fruiting period not swollen large, involucre round column-shaped campanulate, involucral bracts 1 layers, lanceolate, ligulate florets purple; achene fruit beak coarse, pappus 1 layers, feathery-shaped, pale yellow or shallow red brown. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: (Turcz.) Zaika, Sukhor. & N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry riverbeds, dry gullies and valleys or fixed dunes.
Morphology: Stem from base much-branched, gray-green, base without sheath-shaped can ji; leaves linear or filiform, midrib wide thick; capitulum solitary stem branches apex, with 4–5 ligulate florets, involucre narrow round column-shaped; achene round column-shaped, pappus dark-stained yellow, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Pall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly ground or dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaves linear, textured thin, glabrous; capitulum small, involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts 2 layers, ligulate florets yellow; achene 6–8, slender and long, linear-shaped cylindrical, xie tail-shaped inner curved, abaxial surface with numerous needle-like spiny, apex with needle-like spiny, needle-like spiny fang she-shaped arranged, without pappus. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia, Southwest Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow, desert or grass floodplain flats.
Morphology: Stem unbranched, flower scape-shaped, smooth, glabrous; basal leaves long elliptic, flattened, with 3–5 veins, both surfaces smooth glabrous; capitulum large, involucre round column-shaped, diameter 1–1.5 cm, ligulate florets yellow; achene round column-shaped, with longitudinal ribs, pappus dark-stained white, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 532; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes hills or dry valleys grassland.
Morphology: Stem covered with rolled hairs, base covered with sheath-shaped can ji; basal leaves linear, margin flat, with 3–5 veins, midrib white, raised; capitulum forming umbel locule raceme, involucre round column-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus 5–10 roots exceeding long, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Willd.) Zaika, Sukhor. & N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and East China; northern Asia, western part, the Mediterranean region and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes, grassland or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Stem clustered, unbranched, smooth glabrous, stem base covered with dense dense sheath-shaped can yi structure; basal leaves linear to long elliptic; involucre round column-shaped, smooth glabrous, ligulate florets yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus pale yellow, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: (Pall.) Nazarova
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land, saline-alkaline land, rocky slopes or desert gravelly ground.
Morphology: Roots with globose swollen large tuberous roots; stem upper part usually with branched, stem base covered with sheath-shaped can ji; basal leaves narrow linear, arcuate shape curved, hooked; involucre narrow round column-shaped, covered with chen-shaped short-pubescent, ligulate florets yellow; achene glabrous, round column-shaped, pappus white, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Lipsch.) Zaika, Sukhor. & N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Sichuan; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly ground, dry slopes, Gobi gravel desert or sandy land.
Morphology: Stem from middle part above branched forming broom-shaped-shaped, base sheath-shaped can ji fiber-shaped lacerate lobed; leaves linear; capitulum containing 7–12 ligulate florets, involucre narrow round column-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus dark-stained white, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: (Maxim.) Zaika, Sukhor. & N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming sandy land, saline-alkaline land or rivers and lakes floodplain flats.
Morphology: Stem gray-green, smooth, glabrous; basal leaves long elliptic to linear-lanceolate, cauline leaves opposite, texture thick, succulent, smooth glabrous, gray-green; involucre narrow round column-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene covered with villous, round column-shaped, pappus white, feathery-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period April–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and Africa also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore wasteland, salinized and alkalized moist slopes or mostly rocky river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Basal leaves lotus base-shaped, large head pinnate lobed, cauline leaves lanceolate, base half amplexicaul, leaves covered with mostly slender bao long nodes hairs; capitulum often 2–8 is one zu along flower branches arranged forming spike, involucre round column-shaped, covered with glandular hairs, ligulate small flowers blue; pappus very short, membrane segments-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides marshland, wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Leaves linear, pinnately divided or not lobed, margin entire or with serrate, both surfaces glabrous, gray-green; capitulum numerous rows forming panicle, involucre long 1.5 cm, ligulate florets yellow; achene elliptic, compressed, margin with wide wings, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period April–November.

Author: (L.) C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline land, river floodplains sandy land, fields or sand gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant milky sap well developed, smooth glabrous; leaves long elliptic to linear, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed or with large serrate, texture slightly thick; capitulum numerous, involucre round column-shaped or cuneate, band purplish red, ligulate florets purple or purple blue; achene slightly compressed, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 533; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Roxb.) Babc. ex Kerr
Distribution: Distributed in South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, slopes or grassland.
Morphology: Plant nearly without stem, ye without creeping branches; basal leaves lotus base-shaped, spatulate to linear-shaped oblanceolate, textured slightly thick, with slender teeth or nearly entire; capitulum in flower scape apex rows forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre round column-shaped, florets yellow. Flowering and fruiting period April–May.

Author: (Willd.) Kuntze
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Hainan; Southeast Asia, South Asia, Africa and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore open sandy land.
Morphology: Plant with creeping branches, branches at nodes borne not ding roots and leaves; basal leaves lotus base-shaped, oblanceolate, margin shallow undulate or pinnate-shaped half lobed; capitulum single borne on lotus base-shaped leaves tuft in, involucre round column-shaped, florets yellow. Flowering and fruiting period June–December.

Author: (Roxb.) Ramayya & Rajagopal
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; Central Asia, Southwest Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, river floodplains, grassland or fields.
Morphology: Stem slender slender, spreading spreading or ascending, unequal two dichotomously branched; basal leaves lotus base-shaped, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed, margin with spiny pointed, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum rows forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre round column-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene different shape, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Ostenf.) Amin ex N.Kilian
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Stem mostly times two dichotomously branched, dense forming spherical-shaped, glabrous; leaves few, linear, leathery; capitulum small and mostly, involucre round column-shaped, glabrous, ligulate florets two -like, yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus white, base lian forming ring, overall deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: (L.) Hill
Distribution: Native to Europe, in China and worldwide naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Leaves long elliptic, base auricles-shaped amplexicaul, margin with pointed teeth spiny; capitulum in stem branches apex rows forming dense dense corymb, involucre wide bell-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene inverted spreading needle-like, pappus white, base connate forming ring. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline land, slopes grass slope or river floodplains wetland.
Morphology: Leaves long elliptic to oblanceolate, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed or not lobed, base half amplexicaul, both surfaces smooth glabrous; capitulum rows forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre bell-shaped, ligulate florets yellow; achene slightly compressed, pappus white, single hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Fisch., C.A.Mey. & Avé-Lall.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or floodplains gravelly ground.
Morphology: Stem lower part woody becoming, covered with spider-like filiform pubescent; in upper part leaves lanceolate to linear, entire; capitulum solitary branches end, fruiting period long 11–13 mm, outer surface covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, ligulate florets 9–12, yellow; achene narrow round column-shaped, crown scales 5, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Boiss. & Buhse
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia and Asia Southwest China part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy dry slopes or plains wasteland.
Morphology: Basal leaves pinnately divided or with dentate notches, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum single borne on branches end or branches forked places, nearly without stalk, involucre short round column-shaped, florets yellow; inner layers involucral bracts fruiting period becoming hard, and toward inner curved surrounding outer layers achene, pappus bristles single hairs-shaped, short. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Fisch. & C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or mostly rocky river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Stem spreading spreading, unequal two dichotomously branched; basal leaves long elliptic, margin serrate to pinnate lobed; capitulum solitary branches end or solitary branches forked places, involucre bell-shaped, florets yellow; achene different shape, outer layers rhombic, without pappus, inner layers inverted golden-shaped pyramidal-shaped, apex with long beak and pappus. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist saline-alkaline land, plains wasteland or gravelly ground.
Morphology: Basal leaves shallowly lobed or not lobed, margin with teeth or nearly entire; flower ting often covered with arachnoid hairs, involucre small, long 8–12 mm, outer layers involucral bracts apex abaxial side without small angles, or sometimes slightly enlarged thick; achene upper part with spiny-shaped projections, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Ledeb.) Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas wet meadow.
Morphology: Leaves linear, pinnatisect, lateral lobes linear, entire, both surfaces covered with spider-like filiform short hairs; involucre small, bell-shaped, involucral bracts green, without angles, outer layers appressed appressed, apex flat tan, with wide membranous margin; achene middle part above with large liang small spiny, below with small tuberculate projections. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Hornem.) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains meadow, saline-alkaline land or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves linear to narrow oblanceolate, not lobed to pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed; involucre small, bell-shaped, involucral bracts apex without angles, outer layers wide only 1–1.5 mm, conspicuous narrow on inner layers involucral bracts, often entire xian pale purplish red; pappus dark-stained white. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 17, 532; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland, fields wasteland or river floodplains wetland.
Morphology: Leaves lanceolate, margin sometimes with undulate teeth or deeply pinnatifid, lobes among often jia borne small teeth; involucre bell-shaped, outer layers involucral bracts apex with conspicuous small angles; achene upper part with small spiny, lower part with forming rows arranged small tubercles. Flowering period April–September, fruiting period May–October.

Author: Rodin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains meadow, salinized and alkalized meadow or fields wetland.
Morphology: Leaves not divided, entire or with undulate teeth; involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts apex often band purplish red, with longer and pointed angles, outer layers pale green, appressed appressed, inner layers with projections angles 1, corolla throat and tongue segments lower part abaxial surface with short-pubescent; achene fusiform. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Dahlst.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in floodplains meadow, saline-alkaline land or fields wetland.
Morphology: Leaves pinnately divided, sometimes xian gray blue green; involucre bell-shaped, outer layers involucral bracts gray-green white green, appressed appressed, apex often xian purplish red, with long angles, inner layers with projections angles 1–2, corolla throat and tongue segments lower part abaxial surface with short-pubescent; achene cylindrical. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert water wetland, salinized meadow or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves gray-green, pinnate-shaped shallowly lobed or deeply lobed; involucre bell-shaped, outer layers involucral bracts pale green, spreading to revolute, often with relatively coarse large angles, inner layers green, with angles; achene gray brown. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: F.H.Wigg.
Distribution: Native to northern Asia, western part and Europe, eastern China and southern part widely naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, watersides sandy soil or fields.
Morphology: Leaves coarse large, large head pinnately divided; capitulum large, involucre wide bell-shaped, outer layers involucral bracts revolute or spreading, apex flat tan; achene shallow yellow-brown, middle part above with large liang small pointed spiny, its remaining parts with small tuberculate projections. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: A.Gray
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Japan, Korea, Vietnam and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant creeping, horizontally creeping; leaves long-petiolate, leaf blade 1–2 times palmate-shaped 3–5 divided, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum solitary leaf axils, involucre round column-shaped, outer surface glabrous, ligulate florets 12–60, yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period May–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Ledeb.) W.A.Weber
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, river floodplains ground or marshland margins.
Morphology: Plant round spherical-shaped, stem dense, much-branched; leaves pinnately divided, green green, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum numerous rows forming corymb, involucre narrow round column-shaped, involucral bracts 4 layers, outer surface glabrous, ligulate florets yellow; achene fusiform-shaped, pappus white. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (Turcz. ex DC.) Sennikov
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Gansu; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe sandy land or saline land.
Morphology: Stem erect, glabrous; leaves narrow, linear or linear-lanceolate, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum small, in stem branches apex rows forming narrow round conical-shaped raceme, involucre narrow round column-shaped, long 8–9 mm, involucral bracts apex with angles-shaped attached genus structure. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized sandy land or riverbanks.
Morphology: Stem densely covered with red he woolly; leaves sagittate or triangular, margin with teeth, base cordate, veins pinnate-shaped; capitulum numerous, arranged forming pyramidal shape round conical-shaped corymb, bract linear, involucre cup-shaped, wide 6–9 mm, involucral bracts 6–8, with white membranous margin. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Ledeb.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Pakistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in moist salinized sandy land or riverbanks.
Morphology: Plant gray-green; leaves erect, smooth, with la powdery, base decurrent forming stalk, veins pinnate-shaped; round conical-shaped raceme, capitulum small, involucre narrow tube shape or narrow tuo luo shape, involucral bracts 4–5, oblong, ligulate flower 1–3, tubular flower 2–7. Flowering period July–August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.Jeffrey & Y.L.Chen
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand stones ground or fields.
Morphology: Leaves sessile, spatulate to linear, pinnate lobed to entire; inflorescence sparsely covered, inflorescence stalk long, long 1.5–4 cm, capitulum disc-shaped, involucral bracts about 15, outer layers bracteole 4–5, without or sometimes with short black pointed, florets entire tubular, yellow. Flowering period May–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Eurasia widely distributed, China from Northeast to Southwest China and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, fields wasteland or open grassland.
Morphology: Leaves sessile, spatulate to linear, pinnate lobed to with teeth; inflorescence dense, inflorescence stalk short, long 0.5–2 cm, capitulum disc-shaped, involucral bracts 18–22, outer layers bracteole 7–11, apex with long black pointed, florets entire tubular, yellow. Flowering period April–October.

Author: Gaertn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in meadow or oases fields.
Morphology: Stem erect; leaves large head pinnately divided, base half amplexicaul; capitulum numerous arranged forming terminal compound corymb, involucre wide bell-shaped or half globose, involucral bracts about 13, ligulate flower 12–15, tongue segments yellow, long 8–9 mm; pappus deciduous. Flowering period May–July.

Author: (L.) Moench
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, gravelly ground, moist salt ground or grassland.
Morphology: Rhizome woody; leaves elliptic round-shaped spatulate, oblanceolate to linear, entire, both surfaces covered with woolly; capitulum small, broad obovate or globose, numerous arranged forming compound corymb, involucral bracts ning meng yellow or orange yellow, apex toward outer reflexed, margin dry membranous. Flowering period August.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Beauverd) Hand.-Mazz.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus grass floodplain flats, salt-lake shores or rocky gravel ground.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted appearing cushion-forming; basal leaves lotus base-shaped, spatulate or linear-shaped spatulate, both surfaces covered with white or silvery white dense soft hairs; bracts leaves qun diameter about 1.5–2.5 cm, capitulum dense, densely covered with white villous-shaped soft hairs. Flowering period July–August.

Author: (Jacq.) Dobrocz.
Distribution: Distributed in northern China and western part each ground; Northeast China ya, North Asia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline land, salt-lake shores or watersides marshland.
Morphology: Leaves strips-shaped or oblong-lanceolate, glabrous, succulent; capitulum rows forming umbel locule-shaped, with long inflorescence stalk, margin tongue segments bluish purple, bisexual flowers yellow, involucre nearly tubular, flower after bell-shaped; achene narrow oblong, with thick margins ribs, pappus in flower after enlarged long, reaching 14–16 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Handbook of Halophytes 17, 525; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland, saline-alkaline land steppe.
Morphology: Entire plant covered with dense papillae-shaped short hairs and slightly bristles; leaves linear-lanceolate or linear; capitulum few arranged forming sparse corymb, involucre nearly half globose, ligulate flower not bearing measured, tongue segments pale purple red or purple blue; achene oblong, without ribs, pappus rough hairs-shaped. Flowering period July–September.

Author: (L.) Greuter
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe, Central Asia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes grassland.
Morphology: Leaves linear-lanceolate or linear, with 3 strips veins, upper surface conspicuous with glandular dots; capitulum numerous arranged forming dense corymb, involucre wide inverted conical shape, involucral bracts leaves textured, ligulate flower 5–10, tongue segments pale purplish red, achene oblong, pappus rough hairs-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (L.) Rchb.f.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Southwest Asia, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salt becoming marshland, terraces or mostly rocky slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white soft hairs; leaves oblong or long round-shaped linear; capitulum numerous arranged forming relatively dense corymb, involucre short cylindrical or inverted conical, involucral bracts thinly leathery, flower entire is tubular, corolla yellow. Flowering period August–September.

Author: (Less.) Soják
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy alkaline ground, half desert or gravel dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with thin spider-like filiform short soft hairs; leaves very small, nearly leathery, linear or long round-shaped linear, long 1–3 cm; capitulum very small, numerous rows forming dense corymb, involucre short cylindrical, involucral bracts thinly leathery, flower entire two -like, tubular, pale yellow. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Kitam.) Nemoto
Distribution: Distributed in Zhejiang pu tuo, Japan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land.
Morphology: Plant nearly glabrous; basal leaves spatulate, sparse spreading, both surfaces glabrous or with sparse pubescent, long 2 – 3 cm, entire; capitulum solitary branches end, involucre without margin hairs, pappus different shape, ligulate corolla hairs short scale-like, tubular corolla hairs bristles-shaped, pale brown. Flower period 10 – 12 yue.

Author: Maxim.
Distribution: Distributed in Zhejiang boat-shaped mountain archipelagos; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sea island reefs.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with white mostly slender bao long glandular hairs, stem woody becoming; basal leaves dense, lotus base-shaped, spatulate or obovate, long 3–9 cm, margin with teeth, texture thick; involucre wide bell-shaped, margin with margin hairs, ligulate flowers bluish purple; pappus same shape, bristles-shaped, yellowish white. Flower period 10 – 12 yue.

Author: (Turcz.) Novopokr.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy ground or dry ground.
Morphology: Leaves very small, long round-shaped oblanceolate, long 6–8 mm, wide 2–4 mm, both surfaces covered with white spider-like filiform short soft hairs; involucre wide obovate, involucral bracts covered with spider-like filiform short soft hairs, apex obtuse, ligulate flower tongue segments pale blue. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Novopokr.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe band sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves elliptic or lanceolate, long 1–5 cm, wide 3–9 mm, covered with longer spider-like filiform rolled hairs or woolly; involucre half spherical-shaped, involucral bracts apex acuminate and slightly purplish red or brown, ligulate flower relatively mostly, tongue segments white or shallow red purple. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: (C. Winkl.) Novopokr.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, dry river valleys, floodplains or desert steppe.
Morphology: Stem broom-shaped-shaped branched, covered with thin soft hairs; leaves dense, linear, long 10–20 mm, wide 1–1.5 mm; inflorescence stalk slender and long, involucre wide obovate, involucral bracts covered with short soft hairs, apex green or white, ligulate flower with or without, tongue segments pale purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Novopokr.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, rocky gravelly floodplains or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Stem lower part much-branched, covered with grayish white short soft hairs; leaves long round-shaped linear or nearly linear, long 8–15 mm, wide 1.5–2 mm; inflorescence stalk coarse robust, involucre wide obovate, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with sparse spider-like filiform short soft hairs, apex acuminate, purplish red, ligulate flower tongue segments pale purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Novopokr.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert or desert grassland.
Morphology: Leaves oblong or oblong-lanceolate, long 15–25 mm, wide 2–4 mm, both surfaces covered with grayish white spider-like filiform soft hairs; involucre wide obovate, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with white spider-like filiform dense short hairs, apex slightly obtuse, middle part and apex purplish red, ligulate flower tongue segments pale purple. Flowering period September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to North America, China East China introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized sandy soil.
Morphology: Stem much-branched; cauline leaves large parts rhombic, base cuneate, lower part margin entire, apex to middle part with serrate, both surfaces glabrous, with oil and fat; capitulum clustered, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts ovate to lanceolate, margin membranous, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period August–December.

Author: (Ledeb.) G.L.Nesom
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline flats, valleys river floodplains or saline-alkaline wetland.
Morphology: Leaves linear or linear-lanceolate, entire; capitulum often rows forming racemose panicle, involucre half spherical-shaped campanulate, involucral bracts 2–3 layers, linear, outer layers green, leaves textured, ligulate flower conspicuous short on pappus, bisexual flowers radially symmetrical. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 526; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Michx.) G.L.Nesom
Distribution: Native to North America, throughout China naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline wetland, floodplains or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; leaves lanceolate to linear-lanceolate, entire, succulent; capitulum small and mostly, dense arranged forming umbel locule-shaped or round conical-shaped inflorescence, involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts lanceolate, very unequal long, ligulate flowers small, tongue segments purple blue. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Pursh) Dunal
Distribution: Native to North America, Liaoning naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in open wasteland.
Life Form: Biennial or perennial herb or subshrub, hemicryptophyte
Morphology: Stem glabrous, with zhan ye; leaves textured thick, oblong, base slightly amplexicaul, margin with serrate, both surfaces glabrous, with glandular dots; involucre broad urceolate, involucral bracts club-shaped-shaped to lanceolate, green, leathery, with zhan ye, outer layers hooked reflexed, ligulate flower yellow. Flowering period August–September.

Author: C.Winkl.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Gansu, Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky slopes or riverbanks alluvial fans.
Morphology: Plant covered with white short soft hairs and brown glandular dots; leaves long round-shaped linear, deeply pinnatifid; capitulum 2–5 in branches end rows forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts with pale brown narrow membranous margin, corolla yellow; achene with 5 strips longitudinal ribs and glands. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (Ledeb.) Poljakov ex Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Tibet; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, sand gravelly wasteland or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant covered with white woolly; leaves gray-green, oblong or ovate, twice deeply pinnatifid; capitulum solitary, involucral bracts herbaceous, receptacle conspicuous projections, conical-shaped globose, flower yellow; achene glabrous, with 5 strips longitudinal ribs. Flowering and fruiting period April–September.

Author: C.Winkl.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu and Tibet; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with white woolly; leaves gray-green, spreading needle-like ovate to oblong, pinnatisect, lobes entire or shallowly lobed; capitulum solitary stem apex, involucral bracts herbaceous, corolla yellow; achene long sparsely covered long hairs, with 5 strips longitudinal ribs. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Kar. & Kir.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in slopes mostly rocky ground, meadow or gravelly river floodplains.
Morphology: Plant covered with woolly; leaves oblong or ovate, deeply pinnatifid, lobes again secondary 2–4 deeply lobed or shallowly lobed; capitulum solitary, involucral bracts herbaceous, with brown wide membranous margin, corolla yellow; achene glabrous or sparse in apex mostly few with sparse pubescent, with 5–6 strips longitudinal ribs. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Maxim.) Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi, Ningxia and Gansu; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes.
Morphology: Stem much-branched; leaves linear, with 3 veins, both surfaces covered with sparse pubescent, base with 2–3 pairs pinnate-shaped lobes; capitulum half globose, pendulous, diameter 8–20 mm, numerous rows forming sparse pine corymb, involucral bracts membranous or margin membranous, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Franch.) C.Shih
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly gravel dry river floodplains or sandy land.
Morphology: Stem much-branched, forming globose branches tuft; leaves with woolly hairs, pinnately divided, lobes linear; capitulum numerous, diameter 5–6 mm, in stem branches apex rows forming sparse pine corymb, involucre cup-shaped half globose, corolla pale yellow. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant covered with appressed single hairs or dichotomously branched hairs; leaves palmate type pinnatisect, gray-green or green, lobes linear-shaped subulate; involucre inverted conical or bell-shaped, involucral bracts margin shallow brown membranous, ligulate flower about 8, tongue segments yellow, apex 2 slightly teeth lobed. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: (Hand.-Mazz.) C.Shih
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu, Qinghai and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant covered with dense dense appressed chen-shaped stellate hairs; leaves palmate-shaped, palmate type pinnate-shaped or pinnately divided, lobes linear, gray-green; capitulum often curved pendulous, involucre half globose or inverted conical, involucral bracts margin brown membranous, ligulate flower yellow, apex with 2 slightly pointed teeth. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert hilly ground or sand gravel Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant covered with sparse zhan hairs-shaped short-pubescent, after becoming glabrous; leaves small, slightly succulent, long 1–1.5 cm, nearly glabrous, pinnatisect or 3-lobed, lobes linear; involucre round column-shaped, involucral bracts brown membranous, lacerate lobed, ligulate flower yellow, tongue segments linear-lanceolate. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert rocky dry slopes.
Morphology: Plant covered with zhan hairs-shaped short-pubescent, after becoming glabrous; leaves succulent, long 1.5–2 cm, pinnatisect, lobes linear; capitulum solitary branches end, with long pedicel, inverted round conical-shaped cylindrical, involucral bracts abaxial surface raised or with keel, ligulate flower yellow, tongue segments short. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, river valleys stony flats or rocky Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant grayish white; leaves succulent, palmate-shaped 3 deeply lobed, lobes linear; capitulum half globose, involucral bracts grayish white, margin membranous, ligulate flower yellow, apex 3 teeth lobed. Flowering and fruiting period September–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant covered with short-pubescent and glands; leaves gray-green, slightly succulent, long 1–2 cm, pinnate-shaped –5 deeply lobed, lobes linear; capitulum oblong, involucral bracts apex obtuse, margin membranous, without ligulate flower. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: (C.Winkl.) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry valleys.
Morphology: When year borne stem branches numerous, broom-shaped-shaped, upper part with slender and long lateral branches; leaves linear or narrow lanceolate-linear, entire; capitulum ovate, rows forming umbel locule-shaped inflorescence, involucre narrow cup-shaped, involucral bracts herbaceous, with membranous margin, florets corolla outer surface spreading borne stellate hairs and sessile glandular dots. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Krasch. & Rubtzov) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or desert Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: When year borne stem branches numerous, spreading borne short stellate hairs, unbranched or with shorter high position branches; leaves spatulate or oblanceolate, apex 3–5-lobed; capitulum ovate, rows fascicled-shaped corymb, involucre narrow cup-shaped, involucral bracts herbaceous, margin membranous, florets corolla sparsely covered stellate hairs. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Hand.-Mazz.) Y.Ling & C.Shih
Distribution: Distributed in Shaanxi, Gansu, Sichuan and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in high elevations sand soil-textured dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaves twice pinnately divided, one times entire lobed, twice deeply lobed or several entire lobed, ultimate lobes long elliptic or falcate, lower surface grayish white; compound corymb, capitulum small, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts margin brown membranous, corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Dunn) C.Shih
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; India also found.
Habitat: Occurs in high elevations slopes or grassland.
Morphology: Leaves twice palmate-shaped 3–5 entire lobed, ultimate lobes elliptic, both surfaces grayish white; corymb, capitulum slightly large, involucre wide bell-shaped, involucral bracts outer surface covered with short-pubescent or slender pubescent, margin brown wide membranous, margin female flowers apex 3–4 obtuse lobed or deeply lobed. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Hook.f. & Thomson ex C.B.Clarke) Tzvelev
Distribution: Distributed in Sichuan and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in mostly rocky gravelly dry slopes.
Morphology: Leaves twice pinnately divided, one times entire lobed, twice shallowly lobed or deeply lobed, ultimate lobes long elliptic, both surfaces grayish white; corymb, capitulum few, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts margin brown brown membranous. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Poljakov ex Grubov
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe or sandy land.
Morphology: Leaves twice pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear or linear-shaped long elliptic, both surfaces grayish white; compound corymb large type, capitulum small, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts lustrous, wheat-like culms yellow, margin white membranous. Flowering period August.

Author: (Ledeb.) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Tibet; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, dry river valleys or desert steppe.
Morphology: Leaves twice palmate-shaped or palmate type pinnate-shaped 3–5 entire lobed, ultimate lobes linear, both surfaces grayish white or pale green; compound corymb, capitulum small, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts lustrous, wheat-like culms yellow, margin white or band shallow brown membranous. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: (C.Winkl.) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, river floodplains ground or gravel stony ground.
Morphology: Leaves twice pinnatisect, ultimate lobes long elliptic or oblanceolate, both surfaces grayish white; compound corymb, capitulum numerous, involucre bell-shaped, involucral bracts lustrous, wheat-like culms yellow, margin white membranous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Pall.) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, river valleys gravelly ground or slopes wasteland.
Morphology: Plant covered with white silky hairs; leaves pectinate dentate pinnatisect, lobes linear-shaped subulate; spike-shaped or narrow round conical-shaped inflorescence compact, capitulum ovate, involucral bracts broadly ovate, glabrous, herbaceous, with wide membranous margin. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground also found.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands, seashore saline land or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Ground lower stolon long or short; leaves ovate, pinnately divided or with serrate, base truncate or slightly cordate, both surfaces pale green; capitulum diameter 1.5–2.5 cm, involucral bracts margin white or brown wide membranous, ligulate flower yellow, tongue segments apex entire or 2–3 teeth. Flowering period June–November.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Jacquem. ex Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert valleys, proluvial fans, sand gravel ground or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant sparsely covered with grayish white slightly pubescent; stem lower part and middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum subglobose, diameter 5–15 mm, pendulous, involucral bracts herbaceous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, dry river valleys or dry steppe.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with silvery white silky textured short-pubescent; stem lower part and middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes elliptic; capitulum half globose or nearly ovate campanulate, diameter 4–7 mm, with short stalk and small bracts leaves, pendulous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Steph. ex Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, dry river valleys, mountain among basins or desert steppe.
Morphology: Plant with nong lie fragrant, covered with grayish white filiform short-pubescent; stem lower part and middle part leaves twice three protruding entire lobed or twice palmate-shaped type pinnatisect, small lobes nearly lanceolate; capitulum half globose or subglobose, diameter 3–4 mm, with short stalk, pendulous or oblique spreading. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, half desert steppe or semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Stem often woody; leaves small, half succulent, both surfaces covered with gray yellow short soft hairs, basal leaves and stem in lower part leaves twice pinnatisect; capitulum subglobose, diameter 3.5–4.5 mm, with short stalk, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with gray yellow short-pubescent, in among with green in ribs. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, half desert steppe or saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Leaves initially covered with long soft hairs, after glabrous, bright green, basal leaves and stem lower part leaves base with 3–5-lobed accessory stipule, middle part leaves twice ti qin-shaped pinnatisect, small lobes slender small; capitulum globose or broadly ovate, diameter 2–4 mm, nearly without stalk, oblique borne or pendulous. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: Jacquem. ex Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu, Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; the Himalayas regions also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert floodplains, proluvial fans, salt-lake shores or gravel sloping ground.
Life Form: Cushion type subshrub-shaped perennial herb, hemicryptophyte
Morphology: Plant densely covered with grayish white flat appressed filiform woolly hairs; stem lower part and middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes lanceolate; capitulum half globose or subglobose, diameter 5–10 mm, rows forming spike type raceme, involucral bracts margin purple, corolla purple. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Hook.f. & Thomson ex C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; India and Pakistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus slopes, river floodplains or lakeshores etc. Gravelly floodplain flats.
Morphology: Plant with foul odor, densely covered with gray yellow silky textured soft hairs; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes linear; capitulum half globose, diameter 6–10 mm, with short stalk, pendulous, rows forming raceme, involucral bracts margin brown. Flowering and fruiting period July–November.

Author: Pamp.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and East China; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline flats or watersides saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Plant young when densely covered with spider-like filiform silky textured pubescent; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves one to three times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum elliptic round-shaped inverted conical, diameter 3–4 mm, pendulous, involucral bracts abaxial surface initially when densely covered with spider-like filiform woolly hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Weber ex Stechm.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alkaline -like floodplain flats, salinized steppe, dry slopes or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Plant with nong lie fragrant; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves one to three times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum half globose or broadly ovate, diameter 2–4 mm, involucral bracts abaxial surface slightly with white short-pubescent or nearly glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Mattf.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, watersides sandy land, dry slopes or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant with nong lie fragrant, stem band red; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves three to four times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear, both surfaces densely covered with white soft hairs; capitulum subglobose, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm, involucral bracts abaxial surface densely covered with white short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ling & Y.R.Ling
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas slopes grassland or river floodplains sandy land.
Morphology: Stem brown or purplish red; leaves half succulent, stem lower part leaves one to twice pinnately divided, lobed teeth apex roundeded-obtuse, with small glands, petiole base with 1 pairs accessory stipule; capitulum globose or half globose, diameter 3–5 mm, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with grayish white short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Ningxia, Gansu and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly sloping ground, arid river valleys or barren slopes.
Morphology: Plant base woody, coarse robust; basal leaves and stem lower part leaves two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes slender small, elliptic or short linear; capitulum subglobose, diameter 2.5–3.5 mm, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with grayish white or gray-green short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Y.R.Ling
Distribution: Distributed in Gansu and Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes or desert.
Morphology: Stem numerous tufted, glabrous; leaves thin papery, three to four times pectinate dentate pinnatisect, upper surface glabrous, with glandular dots, abaxial surface slightly hairy or glabrous, base with accessory stipule; capitulum globose, diameter 4–5 mm, rows forming raceme, involucral bracts abaxial surface nearly glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Northeast China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized grassland or meadow.
Morphology: Stem usually solitary, herbaceous, nearly glabrous; leaves oblong or long ovate, one to twice pinnately divided, lobed teeth wide 2–4 mm; capitulum subglobose or half globose, diameter 3–4 mm, rows forming narrow panicle, involucral bracts abaxial surface glabrous, margin wide membranous, brown. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline soil -like steppe, grass slope or shrubland.
Morphology: Stem lower part unbranched; stem in lower part leaves two to three times pectinate dentate pinnately divided, each lateral with lobes 6–8, small lobes lanceolate, wide about 1 mm, abaxial surface covered with white short soft hairs; capitulum diameter 2–3 mm, rows forming sao broom-shaped-shaped panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Roxb.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline flats, riverbanks sandy land or watersides marshland.
Morphology: Plant with fragrant; middle part leaves twice pectinate dentate pinnately divided, abaxial surface dark green, without glandular dots, leaves in axis or rachis two lateral with pectinate teeth, in ribs not projections; capitulum diameter 3.5 mm above, in stem on rows forming in etc. Spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in semi-moist river floodplains, valleys, fields or gentle slopes.
Morphology: Stem solitary; middle part leaves two- to three-times pinnately divided, each lateral with lobes 4–10, ultimate lobules forming pectinate, short-lanceolate teeth, less than 2 mm wide; capitulum diameter 1.5–2 mm, arranged along the stem into a narrow elongated panicle, involucral bracts abaxial surface glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang and Inner Mongolia; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized steppe, meadow or rocky slopes.
Morphology: Stem unbranched or only with attached capitulum branchlets; middle part leaves long 1–3 cm, both surfaces sparsely covered with short-pubescent or initially when slightly covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, after deciduous, one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear, apex pointed or obtuse pointed; involucral bracts abaxial surface slightly with short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu, Qinghai and Tibet.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly dry slopes, saline-alkaline land or floodplains.
Morphology: Stem numerous tufted, densely covered with grayish white short-pubescent; middle part leaves small lobes narrow linear-shaped clavate shape or narrow linear, apex roundeded-obtuse or slightly swollen large; capitulum half globose or ovoid, diameter 3–4 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy steppe, river lakeshores sandy ground or low-lying meadow.
Morphology: Stem solitary, unbranched or branched; stem middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 2–4, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum subglobose, diameter 2–3 mm, densely covered forming clusters, again rows forming panicle; corolla sheng kai when golden yellow. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: Jacq.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline soil -like grassland, sandy grass flat, dry gullies or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with silvery white or pale gray yellow silky textured soft hairs; middle part leaves two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear-lanceolate or narrow linear; capitulum diameter 1–2 mm, corolla rim part outer surface with short-pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in low-lying and moist marsh, mountain among depressions or fields.
Morphology: Stem usually single one; leaves textured thick, half succulent, both surfaces glabrous, middle part leaves one to twice pinnately divided, each lateral with lobes 1–2, apex roundeded-obtuse; capitulum half globose, diameter 4–5 mm, involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with filiform short soft hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.

Author: Makino
Distribution: Distributed in Zhejiang and Taiwan; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline flats or nearly sea riverbanks mudflat.
Morphology: Plant with fragrant; middle part leaves one times pinnatisect, lobes narrow linear or narrow linear-lanceolate; capitulum inverted conical, diameter 4–5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: C.B.Clarke
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Sichuan; Northern Hemisphere temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, valleys, barren slopes or barren wild.
Morphology: Middle part leaves elliptic to long ovate, one to twice deeply pinnatifid or entire lobed, small lobes elliptic-lanceolate or linear-lanceolate; capitulum oblong, diameter 2.5–3.5 mm, rows forming narrow and compact panicle; involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with arachnoid hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; bei temperate regions widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, dry river valleys, riverbanks terraces or steppe.
Morphology: Leaves linear-lanceolate or linear, wide 2–3 mm, textured thick, entire; capitulum subglobose, diameter 2–2.5 mm, rows forming spreading or slightly narrow panicle; involucral bracts abaxial surface green, glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or dry slopes.
Morphology: Stem coarse robust, gray brown or gray yellow; middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, long 2–5 cm, wide 1.5–3 cm, small lobes narrow linear, often forming falcate-shaped; capitulum subglobose, diameter 3–4 mm; involucral bracts abaxial surface smooth. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Liou & W.Wang
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia and Hebei; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in mobile or semi-fixed dunes.
Morphology: Stem coarse robust, grayish white; middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, long 6–8 cm, wide 3–4 cm, small lobes narrow linear, not toward outer arcuate curved; capitulum subglobose, diameter 4–5 mm; involucral bracts nearly leathery, glabrous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Turcz. ex Besser
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy land, desert steppe or gravelly sloping ground.
Morphology: Stem branched, lower part cha brown, upper part red brown; leaves textured slightly thick, twice pinnatisect, lower part leaves each lateral with lobes 3–5, middle part leaves each lateral with lobes 3–4, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum ovate, erect, in stem on rows forming large type spreading or slightly narrow long conical inflorescence. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Uses: Oil and fat, windbreak and sand stabilization, ecological use

Author: Schrenk ex Fisch. & C.A.Mey.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or gravelly small hill.
Morphology: Branched mostly, nearly spreading; middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes linear, apex obtuse, mucronate, base with accessory stipule; capitulum ovoid, diameter 1.5–2 mm, composed of spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period June–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or sandy land.
Morphology: Stem branched mostly and long; middle part leaves usually one times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear, wide 0.5–1 mm, two lateral middle part and base lobes usually not divided; capitulum ovate, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm, composed of spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China and North China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry steppe, dry slopes, fixed dunes or sand alkaline ground.
Morphology: Branched oblique toward on extending spreading, stem and old branches surface forming thin segments-shaped bo luo; middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes linear, long 1.5–2 cm, wide 1.5–2 mm, apex acute pointed; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1.5–3 mm. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Gansu; Russia, Central Asia, Europe and North America also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, gravel rocky slopes or desert margin.
