Morphology: Middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes linear, long 0.4–1 cm, wide 0.5–1.5 mm, apex acute pointed; capitulum ovoid, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, dry steppe or gravelly sloping ground.
Morphology: Plant initially when slightly covered with short-pubescent; lower part leaves twice pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 4–6 in number, middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 3–4, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum ovoid, in stem on rows forming narrow long, sao broom-shaped shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe, meadow, barren slopes or gravelly slopes.
Morphology: Plant initially when covered with grayish white pubescent; stem middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear, wide 0.5–1.5 mm; capitulum oblong or ovoid, diameter 1.5–2 mm, composed of spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China and Southwest China; India, Afghanistan and Tajikistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in plateaus sand gravel ground or salt-lake shores grassland.
Morphology: Plant initially when densely covered with pale gray yellow pubescent, lower part branches often fu ground borne long, usually purplish red; middle part leaves one times pinnatisect, lobes narrow linear; capitulum ovoid, diameter 1.5–2 mm, rows forming spike type panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in from Northeast to Southwest China each ground; Asia each ground widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land, riverbanks sandy land or fields wasteland.
Morphology: Plant with nong lie fragrant, initially when densely covered grayish white silky textured pubescent; middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum ovoid, diameter 1.5–2 mm, rows forming spreading large type panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: Waldst. & Kit.
Distribution: Distributed in throughout China; ya ou large lu widely distributed.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, grassland, slopes or water wetland margins.
Life Form: Perennial herb or one, biennial herb, hemicryptophyte
Morphology: Plant with nong lie fragrant, initially when covered with grayish white silky textured pubescent, taproot single one; lower part leaves two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes slender soft, narrow linear or hairs developing-shaped; capitulum subglobose, diameter 1–1.5 mm, without stalk, in stem on rows forming spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: C.Winkl.
Distribution: Distributed in Qinghai, Xinjiang and Tibet; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert steppe or gravelly sloping ground.
Morphology: Middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes filiform linear or nearly hairs developing-shaped, both surfaces covered with persistent short-pubescent; capitulum diameter 0. 5–1.5 mm, with short stalk, in stem on rows forming spreading much-branched panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi and Ningxia; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert sandy land, desert steppe or riverbanks sandy beach.
Morphology: Plant with foul odor; stem middle part leaves twice pinnately divided, first times entire lobed, each lateral with lobes 5–8, lobes two lateral each with 5–8 deeply lobed pectinate teeth, pectinate teeth long and wide 0.3–1.5 mm; capitulum elliptic, diameter 1.5–2 mm. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Spreng.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China, Northwest China and Southwest China; Northeast China ya and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in steppe band sandy land, gravelly sloping ground or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Plant initially when covered with grayish white short-pubescent; basal leaves ovate, long 2–3 cm, twice deeply pinnatifid, small lobes elliptic or long ovate, middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect or deeply lobed; capitulum diameter 1. 5–2.5 mm, rows forming narrow long sao broom-shaped shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Kitam.
Distribution: Distributed in Heilongjiang and Inner Mongolia; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in beach, riversides saline-alkaline land or marshland.
Morphology: Plant with vegetative branches; middle part leaves from leaves on end toward base oblique toward or nearly palmate-shaped 3–5 deeply lobed, small lobes linear-lanceolate; capitulum diameter 2–3 mm, with short stalk, rows forming spreading pointed pyramidal shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert becoming steppe, saline land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Plant covered with persistent spider-like filiform soft hairs; lower part leaves broadly ovate, three times pinnatisect, middle part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes long 2–4 mm; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm; involucral bracts abaxial surface densely covered with grayish white soft hairs. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Weber ex Stechm.
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia, Gansu and Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, Gobi gravel desert, gravelly sloping ground or salinized meadow.
Morphology: Plant initially when covered with spider-like filiform pubescent; lower part leaves twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum oblong, diameter 1.5–2 mm, in branched on star-shaped spreading attached, and in stem on composed of narrow long or slightly spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (Krasch.) Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert, Gobi gravel desert or lakeshores saline land.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves one to twice pinnatisect; capitulum slightly dense, in stem on rows forming spike type panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Ledeb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized Gobi gravel desert.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, with vegetative branches; middle part leaves one times sparse twice pinnatisect, each lateral lobes 3–4, lobes or small lobes elliptic or ovate-elliptic, sometimes with 2–3 deeply lobed teeth; capitulum ovate, diameter 2–3 mm, rows forming pointed pyramidal shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy sloping ground, Gobi gravel desert or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Stem from upper part kai shi branched; middle part leaves one times pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 1–3, small lobes long 3–5 mm; capitulum arranged slightly sparse pine, composed of spreading broad ovate panicle, flowers after capitulum inner flower and involucral bracts one qi deciduous. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Filatova
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy sloping ground, Gobi gravel desert or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Small lobes long 1–3 mm; capitulum in branched on arranged slightly dense, and in stem on rows forming in etc. Spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: O.Berg
Distribution: Native to Central Asia and Iran, China Xinjiang and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy soil.
Morphology: Plant initially when covered with short-pubescent, stem from in lower part kai shi branched; lower part leaves long 3–6 cm, wide 1.5–4.5 cm, twice pinnatisect, each lateral with 2–4 lobes; capitulum in branched on rows forming dense spike, and in stem on composed of narrow and compact panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Wall. ex DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Tibet; India, Pakistan and Afghanistan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in alpine areas desert salinized ground.
Morphology: Plant initially when covered with spider-like filiform pubescent, branched spreading, long 5 cm above; stem lower part and middle part leaves long not and 2.5 cm, two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes short small, linear, long 2–4 mm; capitulum elliptic; involucral bracts abaxial surface densely covered with grayish white pubescent. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch. ex Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline land, desert becoming steppe or sand gravelly slopes.
Morphology: Stem from on half part branched, branches spreading, up to 10–15 cm; stem in lower part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear, long 6–9 mm; capitulum diameter 3–3.5 mm, in stem on rows forming spreading pointed pyramidal shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch. & Iljin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly sloping ground, Gobi gravel desert, dry slopes or dry river valleys.
Morphology: Stem mostly in number, often composed of dense tuft, branched long not and 3 cm; plant initially when covered with pubescent, leaves and involucral bracts abaxial surface covered with slender pubescent and glandular dots; middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 2–3, small lobes narrow linear, both surfaces with glandular dots; involucral bracts abaxial surface often projections. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Krasch.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert or sand gravelly Gobi desert.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with spider-like filiform soft hairs, upper part branched long 2–5 cm; lower part leaves long 1–3 cm, wide 0.8–1 cm, one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1.5–3 mm, rows forming sparse pine spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: (Krasch. & Lavrenko) Filatova
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert becoming steppe or salt eroded ground.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, covered with dense soft hairs or pubescent, upper part branched spreading; leaves one to twice pinnatisect, each lateral with lobes 3–5, flowering period leaves mostly withered, half deciduous; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm, rows forming nearly pointed pyramidal shape panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (B.Keller) Krasch. ex Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in gravelly sloping ground, Gobi gravel desert, dry river valleys or sand dunes.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, upper part branched long 2–5 cm; leaves textured stiff, in lower part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1–2 mm, in stem on composed of narrow panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Poljakov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes, Gobi gravel desert or gravelly sloping ground.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, upper part branched long 1–2 cm; leaves textured stiff, in lower part leaves one to twice pinnatisect, small lobes narrow linear; capitulum long ovate, diameter 1–2 mm, in stem on end rows forming spike or is spike type narrow panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–November.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Schrenk
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Iran, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes.
Morphology: Plant dense tufted, with vegetative branches; middle part leaves one times pinnatisect, each lateral with irregular lobes several in number to 10 remaining in number, ci lobes suborbicular or is wide lobed teeth or leaves entire, not divided, linear-shaped; capitulum ovate, diameter 2–3 mm, rows forming sparse pine spreading panicle. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Rupr.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert gravelly sloping ground, dry steppe or terraces.
Morphology: Plant nearly cushion-forming, densely covered with white soft hairs; leaves small, lower part leaves two to three times pinnatisect, first times nearly on palmate-shaped type divided, middle part leaves one to twice pinnatisect; capitulum ovate, diameter 1.5–2.5 mm; corolla yellow. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang, eastern China cultivated; Europe, northern Asia and western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland or fields.
Morphology: Plant glabrous; lower part leaves oblong or oblanceolate, twice pinnatisect, lobes linear; capitulum different type, diameter 1–1.5 cm, rows forming umbel locule-shaped; involucral bracts 2 layers, gray-green green, tongue segments white, reflexed, tubular flower yellow; achene with 5 ribs, without crown-shaped pappus. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Waldst. & Kit.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Europe, Africa and northern Asia western part also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, riverbanks or wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves three times pinnatisect, main axis wide 0.5–1 mm, ultimate lobes filiform linear to lanceolate, wide 0.1–0.5 mm; involucre narrow oblong, long 4–4.5 mm, wide 2–2.5 mm; involucral bracts band pale brown colored narrow margins; tongue segments pale yellowish white, relatively small. Flowering and fruiting period July–August.

Author: (Krasch.) K.Bremer & Humphries
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land or slopes.
Morphology: Plant green, basal leaves wide 2–5 cm, two to three times pinnatisect, covered with sparse long single hairs; capitulum often solitary, involucral bracts textured thin, margin blackish brown or brown membranous, receptacle without receptacle hairs, ligulate flower white; crown-shaped pappus long 0.4–1.5 mm, lobed to middle part. Flowering and fruiting period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Sch.Bip.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in foothills river valleys.
Morphology: Leaves pinnatisect, tip secondary lobes narrow linear, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum diameter 2–3 cm, involucre half globose, involucral bracts with lustrous membranous margin; tongue segments white, large, long about 10 mm, wide about 3.5 mm; achene with 3 ribs, crown-shaped pappus short, hard nearly entire. Flowering and fruiting period September.

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Central Asia, Europe and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in watersides saline-alkaline wetland, fields or wasteland.
Morphology: Middle part leaves long elliptic, base wide large, half amplexicaul; capitulum diameter 2.5–5 cm, involucre half globose, involucral bracts linear-lanceolate, tongue segments linear, yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus white. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period August–October.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 21, 526; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition

Author: Ostenf.
Distribution: Distributed in North China and Northwest China; Mongolia, Afghanistan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert saline-alkaline flats, Gobi gravel desert, sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Plant tufted, much-branched, covered with long coarse hairs; leaves spreading needle-like or long round-shaped linear, base half amplexicaul; capitulum diameter 1–1.5 cm, involucral bracts linear-shaped to lanceolate, tongue segments shallow yellow; achene round column-shaped, pappus white. Flowering period May–August, fruiting period July–September.

Author: (F.K.Blum ex Ledeb.) G.V.Boiko, Korniy. & Mosyakin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang and Tibet; North Asia, Central Asia, Southwest Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline meadow, low-lying ground or arid wasteland.
Morphology: Leaves linear-lanceolate, base half amplexicaul, lower surface with rough hairs and glandular dots; capitulum diameter 2–3 cm, involucral bracts linear-lanceolate, lower part leathery, margin often red purple, ligulate flower yellow, up to 10 mm, apex with 3 teeth; achene nearly cylindrical, pappus white. Flowering period August–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Gaertn.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in grassland, sandy land, wetland margins or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant covered with pubescent; leaves oblong to oblanceolate, entire, base half amplexicaul; capitulum small, up to 5 mm, single borne on forked-shaped or umbel locule-shaped branched apex, involucral bracts linear, tongue segments yellow; achene cylindrical, pappus white. Flowering period June–September.

Author: Less.
Distribution: Distributed in Northwest China; North Asia, Central Asia and Southwest Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi gravel desert, sandy land, sand dunes or meadow saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem coarse robust, glabrous; leaves ovate orbicular, base amplexicaul, textured thick, nearly succulent, both surfaces covered with short rough hairs; capitulum 3–7 borne on branches end, involucre ovate orbicular or short cylindrical, florets yellow or purplish red; achene cylindrical, pappus white. Flowering period July–September, fruiting period September–October.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (L.) Less.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Hainan and Taiwan; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores or mangrove forest.
Morphology: Leaves subsessile, obovate, margin with teeth, both surfaces covered with rolled short-pubescent; capitulum diameter 3–5 mm, rows forming corymb, involucre ovate or bell-shaped, involucral bracts ovate to linear, with margin hairs; achene cylindrical, with 4-ridged, pappus white, persistent. Flowering period entire year.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Hemsl. ex F.B.Forbes & Hemsl.
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Taiwan; Vietnam also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or rock crevices.
Life Form: Evergreen perennial herb or subshrub, hemicryptophyte
Morphology: Stem ascending or prostrate, glabrous; middle part leaves sessile, obovate-oblong, margin serrate, both surfaces glabrous; capitulum diameter 8–10 mm, rows forming corymb, involucre ovate-shaped globose or broad campanulate, involucral bracts ovate to linear, glabrous. Flowering period May–December.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Patrin ex Widder
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in dry slopes, saline-alkaline land or river floodplains sand rocky ground.
Morphology: Plant covered with grayish white strigose hairs; leaf blade three angles-shaped ovate cordate; male capitulum globose; female capitulum elliptic, inner layers involucral bracts bearing united forming sac-shaped, in fruit becoming stiff, lian same beak part long 12–15 mm, outer surface sparsely covered hooked spiny. Flowering period July–August, fruiting period September–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Americas, Beijing, Henan and Xinjiang and elsewhere naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or salinized and alkalized sandy land.
Morphology: Stem at nodes conspicuous with yellow stiff needle-like spiny; leaf blade lanceolate, entire or with 1–2 pairs teeth or lobes, lower surface grayish white; male capitulum accessory terminal, female capitulum 1–2 axillary; spiny fruit yellow-brown, inverted ovate-shaped elliptic round-shaped, covered with woolly hairs, with slender inverted hooked spiny. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: (L.) Small
Distribution: Native to the Americas, Taiwan naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem four-angled; leaves opposite, with petiole, leaf blade triangular, often with three protruding veins, margin with irregular crenate-serrate, both surfaces with straight hairs or stiff bristles; capitulum terminal, involucre half globose, involucral bracts broadly ovate to lanceolate, corolla white, achene with 3 or 4-ridged, with tubercles projecting. Flowering period March.

Author: (L.) DC.
Distribution: Distributed in Central China, South China and Southwest China; Southeast Asia, South Asia and Oceania also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore sandy land or grassland.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, with long-stalked, leaf blade ovate to ovate-lanceolate, margin with numerous serrate, lower part leaves wide reaching 3 cm above, with 3 main veins; capitulum sometimes paired borne, involucre half globose, ligulate flower yellow, receptacle segments apex obtuse or mucronate; achene apex truncate, without pappus and pappus ring. Flowering period several entire year.

Author: (H.Lév. & Vaniot) Orchard
Distribution: Distributed in Guangdong and Taiwan; Japan, Korea, Vietnam and Thailand also found.
Habitat: Occurs in coast dry sandy soil.
Morphology: Leaves opposite, with inconspicuous petiole or without petiole, leaf blade lanceolate, margin with 1–3 pairs sparse teeth ke or entire, lower part leaves wide not exceeding 1.3 cm; capitulum terminal, involucre subglobose, involucral bracts short on receptacle segments, ligulate flower 1 layers, yellow; achene apex truncate, without pappus ring. Flowering period June–October.

Author: (L.) Urb.
Distribution: Distributed in qin ling south of each ground; East Asia, Southeast Asia and South Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in shaded wet grassland or waterside wetlands.
Morphology: Stem slender and long creeping, at nodes borne roots; leaves stalked, leaf blade orbicular, reniform or ma ti shape, margin with crenate-serrate, base broad cordate; umbel 2–4, clustered borne on leaf axils, flowers small, purplish red or milky white; fruit laterally compressed ya, subglobose, with longitudinal ridges. Flowering and fruiting period April–October.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Beijing and Taiwan and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in coast or sandy grassland.
Morphology: Basal leaves gray-green, with long-stalked, leaf blade pinnately divided, leathery, margin with spiny, cauline leaves mostly few amplexicaul; inflorescence umbel locule-shaped, small umbel nearly head-shaped, base with 4–7 linear-lanceolate involucral bracts, corolla white or pale purple; fruit inverted ovate-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, Beijing and elsewhere introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in coast.
Morphology: Basal leaves with coarse stalk, leaf blade suborbicular to broad ovate, margin 3–5 deeply lobed, with band awn pointed coarse teeth, cauline leaves relatively small and sessile; inflorescence axillary, two forked branched-shaped, small umbel round spherical-shaped, involucral bracts 4–7, broad ovate, with spiny teeth, corolla bluish purple. Flowering and fruiting period spring to summer.
Salinity Data Source: Maximum salinity not recorded in eHALOPH

Author: L.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Afghanistan, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in tidal wetland or saline meadow.
Morphology: Stem lower part leaves long-petiolate, without transverse ge membrane, leaf blade one times pinnatisect, pinnae 4–5 pairs, broadly lanceolate, with pointed serrate; compound umbel terminal, corolla white; fruit ovate, carpels stalk and separate valves free, ridges among oily tube 3–4. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: Handbook of Halophytes 511; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, central China and Southwest China part regions naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in wasteland, fields or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant covered with white coarse stiff hairs; basal leaves two to three times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear or lanceolate; compound umbel, involucral bracts numerous, appearing leaves-shaped, pinnately divided, flower white or band pale red; fruit round ovate, ridges on with white spiny hairs. Flowering period May–July.

Author: K.M.Shen
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or gravel clay slope.
Morphology: Plant with foul odor, stem rough, hairy; basal leaves three protruding type three times pinnatisect, small lobes narrow small, long 10 mm, lower surface densely covered with short-pubescent, early withered; petals yellow, outer surface hairy; separate fruit etc. On or short on fruiting pedicel, each ridges cao inner with oily tube 3–4. Flowering and fruiting period April–June.

Author: Korovin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized grassland.
Morphology: Plant with foul odor; basal leaves three protruding type three times pinnatisect, small lobes oblong, long 10 mm, lower surface densely covered with short-pubescent, early withered; small umbel with flower 10–13, petals yellow, outer surface hairy; separate fruit up to 14 mm, each ridges cao inner with oily tube 2. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Koso-Pol.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in salinized and alkalized gravelly ground, fields wasteland or sandy land.
Morphology: Plant without foul odor, stem slender, erect or slightly appearing " of "-shaped shape curved; basal leaves two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes gray-green, both surfaces densely covered with short-pubescent, early withered; small involucral bracts densely covered with white villous; fruit long 5–8 mm, long on fruiting pedicel, connate surface oily tube 2. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: Kitag.
Distribution: Distributed in Northeast China, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes, sandy land, Gobi gravel desert, gravelly ground or wasteland.
Morphology: Plant densely covered with short-pubescent, blue green; basal leaves lotus base-shaped, two to three times pinnatisect, small lobes thickened; compound umbel sparse spreading, petals yellow; separate fruit broad elliptic, dorsiventrally compressed ya, fruit ridges projections. Flowering period May–June, fruiting period June–July.

Author: M.Bieb.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; North Asia, Central Asia, West Asia and Europe also found.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or rocky crevices.
Morphology: Basal leaves three times pinnatisect, rough, covered with short stiff hairs, early withered; umbel few, sparse, umbel fu 1–6, without involucral bracts; separate fruit elliptic, and fruit stalk nearly etc. Long, each ridges cao inner with oily tube 1, connate surface oily tube 2. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: Korovin
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in Gobi desert, desert, sandy land or sand dunes.
Morphology: Basal leaves three times pinnatisect, upper surface smooth, lower surface densely covered with short-pubescent, not early withered; umbel few, sparse, umbel fu 2–7, without involucral bracts; separate fruit elliptic, long on fruit stalk, each ridges cao inner with oily tube 1, connate surface oily tube 2. Flowering and fruiting period June–July.

Author: Bunge
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Iran and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy land.
Morphology: Tuber-shaped enlarged coarse, sometimes appearing spherical-shaped; basal leaves not appearing lotus base-shaped, leaf blade three protruding type pinnately divided, ultimate lobes linear, cauline leaves leaf sheath narrow small; calyx teeth lanceolate, flower after enlarged large; fruit with short and dense slender hairs. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: Willd.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sand dunes or river floodplains ground.
Morphology: Plant blue green, upper part branches whorled; leaves three protruding three times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear, entire; small umbel nearly head-shaped, flower subsessile, petals pale green; small involucral bracts with white short spiny hairs; separate fruit elliptic, lateral ridges wide wings-shaped. Flowering and fruiting period May–June.

Author: (Pers.) Sprague ex Britton & P.Wilson
Distribution: Native to the Americas, eastern China and southeastern part naturalized.
Habitat: Occurs in waterside wetlands, wasteland or grassland.
Morphology: Stem slender slender, smooth; leaves three to four times pinnate-shaped mostly lobed, lobes linear to filiform; compound umbel terminal or axillary, without involucral bracts and small involucral bracts, petals white or slightly band pink; fruit round center zang shape or round ovate, rounded-obtuse. Flowering and fruiting period May–July.

Author: L.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China and worldwide widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields.
Morphology: Plant with strongly xiang wei; basal leaves with coarse robust petiole, leaf blade usually 3-lobed reaching middle part or 3 entire lobed, lobes nearly rhombic, margin serrate; compound umbel terminal, petals white or yellowish green; separate fruit orbicular or long elliptic, fruit ridges pointed acute. Flowering and fruiting period April–July.

Author: Mill.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, China and worldwide widely cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in fields, wasteland, slopes or river valleys.
Morphology: Plant with strongly xiang wei, stem smooth, gray-green or gray-green white; leaves stalked, 4–5 times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear; compound umbel large type, petals yellow; fruit oblong, main ridges 5 strips, pointed acute. Flowering and fruiting period May–September.

Author: Gaertn.
Distribution: Native to the Mediterranean region, eastern China regions introduced and cultivated.
Habitat: Occurs in alkaline soil steppe or arid sloping ground.
Morphology: Basal leaves pinnately divided, stem upper part leaves 2–3 times pinnately divided, ultimate lobes slender slender, linear; umbel with long stalk, diameter 6–10 cm, involucral bracts numerous, 1–2 times pinnately divided, petals white; fruit smooth, ovate. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.

Author: Willd. ex Schult.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Mongolia, Russia and Central Asia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy dry slopes or floodplains.
Morphology: Roots jing appearing finger-like-shaped forked, stem numerous; basal leaves 2 times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear; small involucral bracts 6–8, smooth glabrous; petals suborbicular, white, without calyx teeth; ovary and fruit base with orbicular white membrane disc; separate fruit with coarse ridges. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.

Author: Ma
Distribution: Distributed in Inner Mongolia.
Habitat: Occurs in desert dry slopes or rocky crevices.
Morphology: Roots jing coarse short, stem single one or forked; basal leaves 2 times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear; small involucral bracts 7–10, calyx teeth slender small, petals suborbicular, white; separate fruit oblong, fruit ridges linear-shaped projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: (Pall. ex Spreng.) Schischk.
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kazakhstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in saline-alkaline land, water wetland or rocky gravel ground.
Morphology: Roots jing not forked, stem single one, cylindrical, robust straight, sui part chong measured, branched toward on; leaf blade 2–3 times pinnately divided, ultimate lobes relatively slender small, wide 2 mm below; separate fruit oblong, long 6 mm, wide 2 mm, densely covered long coarse hairs, fruit ridges pointed acute projections. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.
Literature Sources: Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (H.Boissieu) Pimenov & Kljuykov
Distribution: Distributed in Xinjiang; Kyrgyzstan also found.
Habitat: Occurs in river valleys saline-alkaline land, rocky gray textured slopes and gullies and valleys terraces.
Morphology: Basal leaves tufted, 1–2 times pinnately divided, ultimate lobes ovate, with shallow finely serrate; involucral bracts 2–5, long subulate, calyx teeth short, with villous, petals yellowish white; separate fruit long ovate, densely covered villous. Flowering and fruiting period July–October.

Author: Liou & al.
Distribution: Distributed in eastern part, North China and Northwest China; Mongolia and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in wet meadow, saline-alkaline flats or marshland.
Morphology: Stem solitary; leaves 1–2 times pinnatisect, ultimate lobes linear-lanceolate or curved falcate; involucral bracts caducous, small involucral bracts linear, only with narrow membranous margin, without slender jie hairs, petals white; separate fruit oblong-ovate, main ridges 5, all expanded large forming wings. Flowering and fruiting period July–September.

Author: Miq.
Distribution: Distributed in Liaoning; Japan and Korea also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore saline-alkaline land.
Morphology: Stem tufted; leaves 1–2 times pinnatisect or deeply lobed, ultimate lobes oblanceolate to obovate; involucral bracts not luo, small involucral bracts linear, only with narrow membranous margin, without slender jie hairs, petals white; separate fruit oblong, main ridges 5, all wings-shaped, plant shuan becoming. Flowering and fruiting period August–October.

Author: Thunb.
Distribution: Distributed in East China and South China; Japan, Korea and the Philippines also found.
Habitat: Occurs in seashore floodplain flats or nearly sea mountain ground.
Morphology: Plant often appearing wan ting-shaped; basal leaves with long-stalked, leaf blade wide large textured thick, one to twice ternately divided, pinnae 3-lobed, base cordate, with coarse serrate, glabrous, powdery green; petals purple; separate fruit small, long 4–6 mm, wide 2.5–4 mm, lateral ridges wings-shaped relatively thick. Flowering and fruiting period June–September.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition

Author: (A.Gray) F.Schmidt ex Miq.
Distribution: Distributed in North China, East China and South China; Japan, Korea and Russia also found.
Habitat: Occurs in sandy seashores.
Morphology: Leaves basal, thick-textured, long-petiolate, ternately divided, ultimate lobes obovate, margin incised-serrate; inflorescence densely villous, flowers white or violet-tinged; fruit densely covered with villous and soft hairs, fruit ridges with corky wings. Flowering and fruiting period June–August.
Literature Sources: eHALOPH; Chinese Halophytes, 2nd edition; Chinese Halophytes, 1st edition
